The guild building stood tall in the market's center, crafted entirely from sturdy wood. People came and went, some grinning with excitement, others dragging their feet, faces pale with grief.

Leon's expression darkened.

I have kids to feed this winter. I need the commission. I already lost two sales yesterday. You're my first customer today. I-I promise, no more questions!"

Lisa exhaled shakily, regaining her composure.

She hurried out of the room with the items.

'Five coppers per horn... that's twenty. Eight coppers for the teeth, and thirty for the fur... fifty-eight coppers total. Not bad for rabbit scraps.'

Click.

• When a place becomes enriched with mana and there's no escape route, the mana seeps into the ground. Over time, this leads to the formation of a Dungeon Core.

• Once the core forms, it starts at F-Rank.

F-Rank dungeons have a very basic design-two small rooms at start, one big final room.

• Gradually, the dungeon attracts low-level monsters due to the mana-rich environment.

The first monster to reach the core absorbs half its mana and gains a certain level of intelligence-becoming the Dungeon Boss.

• The Boss bonds with the core, gaining limited control over dungeon structure and basic trap-making abilities.

• The Boss will protect the core with its life. It may die, but it won't ever leave the core unguarded.

Chapter 94: Julia Klen
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Leon's House – 4:00 AM

*Yaaawn*

Leon slowly blinked awake, rubbing the sleep out of his eyes. The room was still dark, and a cold breeze slipped in through the window, brushing against his skin.

He glanced to either side—Selene and Elaine, both completely naked, were curled up beside him, breathing softly in their sleep. A small smirk touched Leon's lips before he carefully slid off the mattress, put on his clothes, and quietly left the room.

*Swish*

A sharp gust of wind crept through a crack near the entrance, making the door creak ever so slightly.

"I've got enough money to rebuild this whole damn house..." Leon muttered as he stretched, "...but the moment I do, the new chief's gonna ask where the hell I got the money from and I don't have a proper buisness.

A big house draws too much attention... fucking annoying. I can either patch this dump up or build a place outside the village and I like the second option more."

With that thought, he grabbed a few candles and a matchbox, then stepped outside.

He started jogging slowly through the still-sleeping village.

'Hmm... adaptive body really is insane. Even when I'm not actively using it, my stamina holds up. I could run for hours like this. But if I try to go full speed... that's when I start to burn out. Maybe one day I'll be able to run at full tilt without even turning it on.' Leon thought with a neutral expression as he continued to run.

As Leon neared the village gates, he noticed the guards—passed out, asleep at their posts. Typical. He didn't bother waking them. But the moment he stepped beyond the village boundaries, he came to a stop.

A thick, heavy mist blanketed the world in white.

"Woah..." he muttered, unable to see anything more than a few feet ahead.

"Adaptive Body." Leon immediately activated the skill.

[Adjusting User's Eyes]

His pupils shifted into narrow slits—cat-like and glowing faintly in the mist. Suddenly, the haze around him seemed to vanish. His vision turned crystal sharp.

"It's like all the fog just disappeared..." Leon grinned as he started running once again.

...

About five hundred meters down the road, something came into Leon's view.

"What the hell is that?" He narrowed his enhanced vision and focused.

A carriage.

Not just any carriage—this one gleamed with expensive polish and golden trim. Two guards on horseback led the way, both decked out in bright silver armor. No doubt it was a Noble carriage.

"Where the fuck are they going this early? The damn sun's not even up." He asked with a very confused expression.

Leon kept his distance, still jogging, pacing himself. But even as he tried to keep out of sight, one of the mounted guards spotted him through the mist.

"Oi! Commoner!" the guard barked loudly, his voice easily cutting through the most.

The carriage and horses came to a halt.

Leon sighed, already annoyed, and turned around with a deadpan look on his face.

"Hup! Get over here, commoner!" the guard repeated, dismounting from his horse and gesturing Leon over like he is some kind of dog.

'How the hell did he see me through all this mist?' Leon thought but walked up, keeping his expression blank.

The guard's eyes widened the moment he got a good look at Leon's face.

"Woah—what the hell's wrong with your eyes?!"

*Blink-Blink*

Leon cocked his head slightly. "What? Something in my eyes?" he asked, casually deactivating the skill. His pupils shifted back to normal as he blinked again, now looking completely human.

"What's wrong?" the other guard asked, glancing at his partner.

"Am I hallucinating or what?" the first one muttered, his eyes squinting as he looked at Leon's face again.

After giving him a second, longer look, the guard finally backed off with a frown.

"Kid, are you from Vernon Village?" his partner asked.

"Yeah..." Leon replied casually.

"Can you show us the way? I think we're a little—"

"Why are we stopping? Did we get lost again?"

A girl's annoyed voice rang from inside the carriage as the window slid open. Her head popped out.

She had short black hair, pale skin, and dark eyes. Not quite pretty, not quite ugly—just average with a noticeable air of superiority.

"Huh...? Who are you?" she asked, eyes narrowing as she scanned Leon up and down.

Leon didn't reply he just stared at her.

"I asked you a question," she repeated, now with irritation in her voice.

"My lady," the guard said quickly, "this commoner belongs to Vernon Village. We were just asking him for directions."

"Can't he talk? Or is his mouth sewn shut?" she snapped with a confused scowl.

'This bitch… what's her problem? Talking like she owns the damn world.' Leon thought, his expression tight as he activated his skill.

Chapter 95: Slimes
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Leon arrived in front of the Dungeon and descended down the stone stairs.

As he made his way down, he noticed a thin mist lingering inside—but it didn't go beyond the first floor.

'Teleport me to the last room...' Leon thought with a neutral expression.

In an instant, his figure vanished from the stairwell and reappeared in the deepest chamber of the Dungeon.

"I greet my Master," Elis said with a neutral expression, immediately bowing before him.

"Hello, Elis. Where's our guest?" Leon asked with a smile as he pulled out the candles he'd brought from home and began placing them around the throne in the center of the room.

"Our guest is sleeping in that corner, Master," Elis replied, pointing toward the southern edge of the chamber. "I created a raised platform for her to rest on."

Leon turned his head, spotting Olga curled up on a stone bed, sleeping soundly like a child.

Before he could say anything else, Elis spoke again.

"Master... we have visitors."

"Hmm?" Leon gave her a confused look. "What visitors ?"

"Small monsters, Master. About twenty of them. They're on the first floor right now."

"Are they strong? Do you know what they are ?" Leon asked, now serious.

"They're weak... but I can't identify their species without seeing them directly. However, they've made a request to meet you master, which is why they haven't advanced past the first floor." Elis spoke with a neutral expression as she looked at Leon.

'Monsters...?' Leon started thinking but he was interrupted by Elis.

"Should I crush them like I did with that human last time, master ?" Elis added, her tone flat.

"Wait... they want to meet me?" Leon asked, raising an eyebrow.

"Yes, Master." Elis replied.

Leon paused for a second, then spoke with a neutral expression. "Alright, let's go see them."

"As you command master..." Elis replied.

"And just in case... Threads." He drew his knife, binding it with an invisible mana thread.

Moments later, both Leon and Elis teleported to the first floor.

What greeted them was... unexpected.

Small, green creatures crawled across the stone floor. Their gelatinous, transparent bodies shimmering under the sun rays coming down the stairs from the dungeon's entrance, each with a mana core floating in the center of their bodies.

"Wait... these are slimes, aren't they?" Leon muttered, tilting his head as he activated his skill.

<Inspect>

[Name: Unknown]

[Class: Slime]

[Race: Monster]

[Level: 1]

[HP: 100/100]

[MP: 50/50]

[Description: A small monster species considered endangered due to their low levels and slow growth. Adventurers often kill them before they can mature or reproduce.]

"Prrrrr... Prrrrrr..."

Suddenly, one of the slimes crawled up to Leon's feet, letting out a soft, vibrating hum.

"Prrrrr..."

"Prrrrr..."

"Prrrrr..."

"Prrrrr..."

...

"You can understand them?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Yes, Master," Elis replied simply.

"Prrrrr... Prrr... Prrrr... Prrrr..."

"I am the leader of this group of slimes, Dungeon Master," Elis translated.

'They acknowledge me as the dungeon master, is it because of my {Dungeon Master} Title ?' Leon thought.

"Ask him why they're here," Leon said, glancing at her.

Elis mimicked the soft sounds—"Prrr... Prrr... Prrr... Prrrr."

"Prrrrr.... Prrrr.... Prrrr.... Prrrr.... Prrrrrrr.... Prrrrrr...." The slime leader responded with a long series of hums and vibrations.

"Master," Elis began, "they say humans have hunted their kind to near extinction. They're here seeking shelter, hoping this dungeon can be their safe home."

"Prrrrr... Prrr.... Prrrr...."

She paused. "They're afraid of you, since you're human, but they believe you're different because you are a dungeon master too. They say this place is their last hope in this whole forest."

Leon stared silently for a moment.

'Even if I killed them, the EXP would be trash... What should I do?' he thought, glancing over the slimes again.

"What can they do ?" Leon asked as he looked at Elis. "Is there anything useful they can offer me or to the dungeon ?"

Elis relayed the question in slime-language.

"Prrr... Prr... Prrrrr... Prrr..."

This time, the slime leader responded with an even longer stream of "Prrrrr"s, pulsing rhythmically as the others hummed along like a chorus.

"They say," Elis began, "after being hunted by humans on the outsides, they fled deep into the forest. But the monsters there were even worse, they hunted them in large groups. So they wandered for a long time—so long, they've memorized every inch of this forest."

She looked at Leon.

"They know every herb, every rare plant, and their exact locations. If you let them stay, they'll share everything they know with you master." Elis replied.

'Information is more valuable than gold. According to my memories, no adventuring party has ever fully mapped the inner forest and no human has ever been to the deep forest. These guys might actually be a good thing to keep around.' Leon thought as a smile spread across his face.

"Alright. They can live on the second floor," Leon said. "But make it clear: if even one of them leads a human or monster to this dungeon, they will be kicked out immediately. No second chances."

Elis translated immediately. "Prrrr... Prrr... Prrrr..... Prrrrrrrrr... Prrrr..."

The slime leader bounced slightly before responding.

"Prrrr.... Prrrr... Prrr... Prrr... Prrr...."

"Master, they say they swear to protect the secret of this dungeon with their lives," Elis relayed. "Also… since they eat dirt, they'll keep the floors clean from moss and grime."

Leon blinked. "Well, that's... helpful."

He stepped aside. "Okay. Go to the second floor."

"Prrr!" Elis chirped in slime-speak, and the slimes began joyfully bouncing toward the stairway.

"I'll head out now," Leon said. "I'll be back soon with some food for Olga and materials you need to build gates and traps for the dungeon."

"In the meantime, talk to them and start gathering some information."

"As you wish, Master," Elis said, bowing before vanishing into thin air.

Chapter 96: Legendary Blacksmith
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

'It's cold... Adaptive Body.' Leon thought as he activated the skill.

[Adjusting Host's Body Temprature]

In just a moment, Leon felt no more cold.

His boots crunched softly against the frozen ground beneath.

As he continued walking, Leon noticed a party of adventurers moving towards the forest with slow, heavy steps, their faces painted with sadness.

Leon instinctively maintained his distance from them, not wanting to attract unnecessary attention, but his sharp ears still managed to catch fragments of their conversation as they passed by.

"This is not good, man... If this kept going, we will lose the war," one of them said, his voice filled with despair.

"Yeah, those Elves are bringing too many mages," another replied, his tone grim.

"I heard that there are some Elves hiding in the Royal Castle as spies, giving them information," a third adventurer muttered, glancing around nervously.

"Losing 300 men in a single day is too much. I don't want to get enslaved by some fucking Elves," another one spat bitterly.

Leon's expression turned thoughtful as he absorbed their words.

'Ah... I forgot that the Human Kingdom is in a war with the Elf Kingdom. But... Did they really lose 300 men to some Elves?

In the mangas that I read back on Earth, these creatures were always portrayed as the wisest and most peaceful beings,' Leon thought to himself, his brows furrowing slightly.

Leon kept moving forward. As he reached the kingdom's outer gate and stepped inside, a heavy, suffocating silence enveloped him. He immediately noticed how different everything felt. The lively chatter and hustle that usually filled the streets were gone.

The market was silent, and every face he saw looked heavy with grief, sorrow casting a long shadow over the bustling kingdom he had seen yesterday. Even the vendors, who were normally shouting to attract customers, sat quietly behind their stalls, their heads bowed low.

Leon's steps carried him further into the middle of the city, curiosity gnawing at him. Then, he noticed a group of people gathered near the fountain in the southern market.

They were lighting candles and kneeling in silent prayer. Curious, Leon approached carefully, not wanting to disturb the people. As he neared the fountain, he peeked down at what they were doing and saw writings beneath the candles.

There were names, countless names scrawled around the base of the fountain—the names of the deceased in the ongoing war.

'K. Catherine, Kraus Kon, Remedy L. Kolank...' Leon started reading the names, but he quickly realized there were far too many for him to read them all. They circled the entire fountain, written in shaky, sorrowful handwriting.

Leon slowly closed his eyes, standing silently for a moment, offering his respects to the fallen. Then, without a word, he continued walking forward...

Eventually, he arrived at Dekkn's Smithy and knocked on the door.

The dwarf's curiosity was instantly piqued. He grabbed the paper and studied it closely.

"Hm… What kind of weapon is this? I've never seen anything like it," he muttered.

The dwarf scratched his beard. "It'll be tough... but now that I know how hard it'll be, I want to craft it even more."

Because if you walk away, people will die in the war because of you and me," the guard said, pointing towards Leon.

"I-I am s-sorry... K-Kid, please give us the potions at 10 coppers and 8 coppers," the guard said heavily.

"Fine... Meet me back here in two hours," Leon replied with a smile.

Chapter 98: Pure Mana
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Inside the Alchemist's Shop...

Leon stood near the counter while the old man picked up some crates of empty glass vials from under it.

"Kid... You don't know what you've done. Even if you had ten alchemists with you, the amount they've given you is too much to make in two hours.

I would've completed the order myself, but three of my alchemists are Velthorne Academy students, and they refused because their monthly duels are coming early this month... I think it's in two days," the old man said.

"I see," Leon replied.

'Hmm... I'll have to go to the Monthly Duels too, since it was clearly stated in the Non-Attending Students clause,' Leon thought with a neutral expression.

*Thud*

"Here are your 600 empty vials, and those boxes over there are filled with Hermit and Limin Tree leaves.

All of this will cost you 14 silvers, with a 2 silver discount from my side, since I failed to complete the contract. I hope your alchemists can complete it in time, kid," the old man said.

"What about pure mana?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"What about it?" the old man replied.

"Don't I have to buy that?" Leon asked.

"No one sells pure mana, kid. Alchemists can easily make it on their own. It's not that hard... Your alchemists should know about it," the old man said.

"I see... Here," Leon said, handing over the 14 silvers.

"So, how are you going to take all this? Do you have a slave or a wagon?" the old man asked.

"How much do you think this crate of empty vials weighs?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"This ? I don't know... 7–8 kilos," the old man replied.

"What about the boxes of leaves?" Leon asked.

"If you pick up both at the same time... I think the weight should be around 20 to 22 kilos. But why are you asking about their weight?" the old man asked, puzzled.

"Threads..." Leon muttered, immediately binding an invisible mana thread to a small wooden statue mounted on the wall behind the old man.

*Pull*

Leon pulled his hand back, and the statue immediately fell to the ground.

*Thud*

"What the- !? How did this fall down?" the old man asked, confused, as he bent down to pick it up.

He picked up the statue, and when he stood up again, he noticed the shop was empty. Leon was gone — and so was all the stuff he had just bought.

"Where did that guy go?" the old man muttered, looking around with a puzzled expression.

...

After leaving the Alchemist's Shop, Leon stared at his inventory.

There were nine slots in total — three filled with leaves and empty vials, one holding a matchbox, and one holding a sharp knife.

Four slots remained empty.

"Let's buy something to eat for Olga," Leon said as he stopped at a fruit shop and bought some fruits.

'Now, I need to learn how to make pure mana and to learn that, I'll need to go to the Helix Library again,' Leon thought as he immediately started walking towards it.

After walking for about 10 minutes, Leon reached the Helix Library.

He entered inside and noticed it was much busier than usual.

Even the first floor was crowded with people.

"Sir Leon, you're back!" a familiar voice rang out.

Leon immediately turned to his right and saw Rita standing there with a smile, hugging the same book to her chest as last time.

"Ah... Rita. How are you?" Leon asked with a smile.

"I'm good! How about you?" Rita asked.

"Yeah, I'm still alive," Leon replied.

"Looking for dungeon-related books again?" Rita asked with a smile.

"No, no... I'm looking for alchemy books — ones that can teach me how to make pure mana," Leon said.

"Oh... Are you an alchemist?" Rita asked with a smile, continuing to stare at his face.

"Yeah," Leon replied with a neutral expression.

'Is there something on my face? Or do I look funny?' Leon wondered, unable to understand why Rita was smiling while looking at him.

"That's good... Very good," Rita said, drifting into her own thoughts, her smile growing wider.

"So... Can you show me the way to...?" Leon asked, waving his hand in front of her face.

"O-Oh, yes! Please come with me," Rita said, snapping out of her thoughts and walking towards the right side of the first floor.

"So... Why is it so crowded today?" Leon asked, confused.

"All the magic academies except Velthorne Academy decided to move their final exams to next month, because of the unfortunate deaths of soldiers on the battlefield last night.

These students are getting ready for their exams so they can pass and serve the kingdom," Rita explained.

"Ah... I see," Leon replied with a neutral expression as they stopped in front of a bookshelf.

Rita looked through some books before pulling out a small one.

She opened it and flipped through its pages carefully before stopping at one page and pointing her finger at it.

"This is the one. Here you go, Mr. Leon — this should help you," Rita said, handing him the book.

Leon looked at the page carefully and saw the heading:

[Making of Pure Mana]

"Well, enjoy your reading and if you need anything, just wave your hand and I will be here," Rita said, smiling as she left.

"Thanks," Leon replied as he started reading.

---

[• Pure Mana is often used in making potions and enchanting weapons. Although it is a powerful chemical, it can be easily made by anyone.

• In order to make pure mana, one must know how to feel and control their own mana.

• The most efficient way to gain control over your mana is to close your eyes and feel it around and inside your body.

• Once you feel the mana, start concentrating a large amount at your fingertip.

When concentrated, the mana will automatically turn into a shiny blue-colored liquid and drip from your finger.

• The stronger your concentration, the more pure mana you can generate.]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 99: Mana Expulsion
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After carefully reading through the process described in the book, Leon closed his eyes and focused.

He took a deep breath, letting the air slowly fill his lungs, and emptied his mind of everything else.

He concentrated solely on the sensation he was looking for — the elusive presence of mana within his body.

One Minute passed.

At first, there was only silence within him. But then, faintly, he began to feel something stir — like a hidden current travelling through his body.

'I think... I can feel it,' Leon thought.

'There's something... moving through me. It's like a river flowing smoothly, gentle yet powerful... and yet, it feels strangely familiar, like blood circulating through my veins.' Leon remained still and started controlling the mana within his body slowly.

A group of noble kids from Pheonix Academy of Mages, were sitting on a bench near the shelf and one of them spoke up as he pointed towards Leon.

"Hey, Look at that idiot."

"Hmmm...? What is he doing ?" Another one asked with a confused expression as a smile appeared over his face.

"I think he just fell asleep... Let's wake him up."

"I know how to wake him up." A guy with blond hairs spoke as he raised his hand towards Leon and started casting a spell.

Leon slowly opened his eyes and this feeling that he felt right now was something else,

'I can feel it... I can feel the mana within me and the mana around me.

Now, let's try to make some pure mana.' Leon thought to himself He carefully began guiding the flow, urging it to move toward the index finger of his right hand.

The mana was moving slowly towards his finger, Leon felt it.

Suddenly, A small water ball appeared over his head.

"Stop casting..." A student in the group who was wearing glasses over his eyes, focused on Leon's finger as he removed his glasses and placed them on the table.

"Oh come on Justin... Let us enjoy a bit. You are-"

"I said stop the fucking casting or I will kill you." Justin spoke as he looked at him with a serious expression.

"Y-Yes." The blonde haired guy immediately stopped casting and the water ball disappeared.

As the mana pooled into Leon's finger, he noticed it growing heavier.

The sensation was odd — not painful, but not comfortable either, it was like pulling out splinters out of his finger.

Still, Leon gritted his teeth and maintained his focus.

Bit by bit, the weight intensified.

Then, finally, a small, shimmering drop of blue liquid formed at the very tip of his finger, glowing faintly.

*Tap*

The droplet detached, falling onto the ground.

As soon as it touched the floor, a crisp notification blinked in front of Leon's eyes.

> [-1 M.P.]

'Hah... Looks like I misunderstood something.' Justin thought as he closed his eyes in disappointment and started picking up his glasses.

'Just one mana for—' Before Leon could even finish the thought, a violent jolt shot through his entire body.

[+35798 M.P.]

Every muscle in his body tensed up like iron cables being pulled taut. His heart slammed against his chest, his vision blurred for a second — and then—

[-5000 M.P.]

*Srrrrrrr*

A brilliant blue beam of pure mana erupted from his fingertip.

It lanced downward with devastating force, punching a clean, smoking hole straight through the library's stone floor, as effortlessly as a hot knife slicing through butter and made almost no sound.

[+35820 M.P.]

"Oh shit..." Leon stumbled backward, his eyes wide in shock as he immediately broke the mana flow.

All the students of Phoenix Mage Academy immediately stared at him with utter confusion, like they weren't sure if they were seeing reality or a hallucination.

"D-Did he just created a hole in the ground ?" Justin asked his mouth wide open in shock.

"H-He did..." His partner replied.

Without wasting a second, Leon slammed the book shut, shoved it roughly back onto the shelf — and bolted out of the library.

'Fucking hell, what the fuck was that?!' His mind raced, completely unable to comprehend what had just occurred.

"Ouch..." Suddenly a sharp, stabbing pain flared in his finger.

He immediately pulled his hand out of the pocket and looked at it with wide eyes.

His finger has turned purple and swollen, as if it was crushed by a giant anvil.

"What the fuck? Heal!" Without wasting another second, he activated his healing skill.

Soft green light enveloped his injured finger as he started walking toward Vinceral Forest.

The healing process was slow.

It took nearly thirty continuous minutes of focused casting before the swelling subsided, and his finger returned to its normal size and color.

A small notification floated in front of him.

[Minor Heal (F5)]

Leon absently dismissed it, but his mind was still replaying the scene that happened in library over and over again.

He stood in front of the dungeon, deep in thought.

"Should I try that one more time?" Leon whispered, his voice neutral as he started thinking about it.

30 seconds passed and Finally, he shrugged...

'Eh... fuck it. What's the worst that can happen? My finger can get destroyed?

But if I don't try it again... the curiosity will eat my mind.' Leon thought as he immediately bent down and picked up a small, smooth stone from the ground. He placed it carefully on the dirt and holded his right hand's index finger above the stone abd began channeling mana into his index finger once again.

The familiar heaviness returned almost immediately, stronger this time, like a deep throbbing pulse at the tip of his finger.

Sweat beaded his forehead as he kept his focus razor-sharp. Bit by bit, a second droplet of pure mana formed.

*Tip*

The drop fell onto the stone. Another notification appeared.

[-1 M.P.]

And then, once again, Leon's muscles locked up violently.

[+38950 M.P.]

His chest felt like it was about to rip itself apart from the inside.

"FUCK..." The pain was sharp, electric, seizing his entire body in a vice-grip.

*Srrrrrrrrr*

[-5000 M.P.]

The beam of pure mana shot out once again, piercing through the stone with such ease that it almost seemed unreal — the stone crumbled apart, reduced to fragments in one second.

[+38950 M.P.]

Leon immediately broke the mana flow, breathing heavily.

*Huff-Huff*

He noticed his finger was already beginning to swell again, turning a sickly shade of purple.

"Fuck... Now, I understand what's happening. Heal." He muttered to himself, gritting his teeth as he casted heal on his finger.

'Every time I try to create Pure Mana... I force mana into my finger. But because my mana cap is only 500 right now, I have to focus incredibly hard to manipulate the mana in my body.

And as soon as I lose that 1 mana... because of the {Mana Return} skill, my mana is regenerated automatically in a very huge amount.

But the mana cap prevents me from absorbing all of it and if the mana is not absorbed by my body it will get expelled.

But, if I am already expelling it out of my finger... It will take that route.

Mana is acting like electric current and it's choosing my body as a medium to get out because my finger is already expelling it but why is it stopping at 5000 ? Is it my body's limit ?

It's like I am holding a wire of electricity in one hand and trying to conduct current... But, What if I give it a machine to work with ?" Leon thought as an idea came to his mind.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 100: I have to go to the toilet
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Leon pushed himself off the ground and raised his left hand toward a nearby tree.

'If I use two skills at once, there's a universal skill cooldown of one second between their activations— that's when my mana recovery kicks in.

If I use Mana Threads alongside any other skill, there'll be a one-second delay before the second skill activates, giving me just enough time to recover the mana I spent.

But... if I create pure mana instead of using it normally, the loss isn't magical —it's physical.

Meaning, within that one-second window, I could activate a skill, and if I'm right, all the recovered mana will surge into it.

If I'm wrong.. well, what's the worst that could happen? I'll just explode into pieces.' Leon thought as shivers went through his spine.

But Without hesitation, he focused his mana into the tip of his right hand's middle finger.

And a droplet of pure mana began to form on his fingertip.

*Tip*

[-1 M.P.]

As soon as the notification popped up, Leon immediately channeled his skill.

The moment the word left his lips, his entire body locked up. It was as if an overwhelming force crushed him into immobility. His muscles wouldn't respond; even breathing became a conscious effort.

I only help people who want to help themselves. Not cowards who run like little girls the moment it's time to act. So, Feel free to leave anytime you want."

"I-I'm sorry... It just seems really risky. I mean, I'm telling all this to someone outside the family, and I don't even know how much power you actually hold—"

Suddenly, Leon raised his index finger and pointed down toward the table.

"W-Wha—?"

Justin glanced down and his face turned pale, as the words didn't come out of his mouth.

Two sharp blades were floating just a few centimeters from his stomach. One of them tilted slightly—aimed directly at his heart and the other one at his dick.

"I saw the wand handle sticking out of your pocket when you walked up to my table. And when you lifted your hand off the table, it slipped back into your pocket—most likely to get a grip on it.

You were planning to cast either an offensive or defensive spell—silently and without my knowledge.

That's why you maintained eye contact so intently... to avoid looking down but you couldn't notice my blades either," Leon explained.

"B-But—"

"I'd suggest you stay calm," Leon interrupted, his voice cold.

"Because before you can even finish the first word of a cast, one of these blades will pierce your heart...

and the other will slice your dick off, maybe you will live after the heart one but that dick-cutting will really make you... Oh well, you know what.

That's why you should always pre-cast defensive spells before engaging in conversation with someone you suspect might be 'Dangerous' ," Leon said, making air quotes with his fingers.

"S-So… are you going to kill me?" Justin asked nervously, slowly pulling his hands out of his pockets and placing them on the table.

"No. You asked how much power I hold—this is your question's answer," Leon replied calmly.

"I-I get it now… P-Please, withdraw your blades. I don't want to become a man with no dick," Justin said seriously.

"I don't want to make you one," Leon smirked, then retracted the floating blades and extended his hand. "It'll be a pleasure working with you, Justin Weaver."

Justin hesitated, then reached out. The two shook hands.

"Now then, what's the status of your second oldest brother?" Leon asked with a smile.

"His class is swordsman, and he's 26 years old... He says that he is single, but secretly has a girlfriend. She's a knight in the Royal Army. He loves her more than anything, Her name is Amelia Ford. Anything else you want to know?" Justin replied, still a bit nervous.

"What about your third brother?" Leon asked, his face unreadable.

"Oh… he's not a problem. Just a drunk. Spends most of his time at the Windgrass Brothel. Spending our father's money on whores and stupid sluts."

"Well… that's an interesting character," Leon muttered. "You can go now. I'll gather the rest of the information myself. You don't stay in the academy dorms, right?"

"No. I travel from my house to the academy every day."

"One last question—why didn't you get into Velthorne Academy?" Leon asked, raising a brow.

"Because… they don't take students based on knowledge. They conduct aptitude tests… and I failed," Justin answered with a hint of shame.

"Inform your maid that I'll be in front of your house, day after tomorrow at 8 a.m. sharp. Tell her to teach me the routines and chores.

Don't talk to me in public, and don't give me orders in front of others unless necessary.

Also—starting tomorrow, you'll begin practicing real magic," Leon said, his voice sharp and commanding.

"Y-Yes… Umm, can I go now?" Justin asked hesitantly.

"Yes," Leon replied, already engrossed in his book once more.

'I have to make some buff skills in order to make him a good mage.'

'But his family is rather interesting… a brother with two wives and a son, yet he spends most of his time stationed at the northeastern border. Suspicious.

And the second brother… deeply in love with a female knight. Now, I can only wonder what might happen if she gets posted at the same border as the first brother…

What if their meals are spiked with a potent aphrodisiac? Or better… what if the second brother and the first brother's wife end up in a sealed room inside the castle, and the air starts to fill with that same aphrodisiac ?

Heh… now that would be a sight worth seeing. A brother might forgive an assassination attempt… but never fucking his wife or girlfriend.

Once both of them starts to fight each other, they should get eliminated from the successor's list.

That's a wicked thought. But such games aren't easy to pull off… I'll need to get inside that house and see what's really going on.' A devious smile crept onto Leon's face as more ideas began forming in his mind.

Helix Library — 3 Hours Later

Leon sat in silence, his eyes scanning the final book laid out in front of him, titled 'Ancient Alchemy Records.'

He flipped through the yellowed pages until something caught his attention:

---

• Moonrush Aphrodisiac Recipe

Moonrush Aphrodisiac is one of the most powerful lust-inducing substances ever recorded. Even a single whiff of its fumes is said to drive a dragon mad with desire.

Legends speak of a deranged queen who crafted this potion in an attempt to enslave her king through lust.

When she used it on him, the king became a frenzied beast, killing over a hundred virgin girls in a single night while ravaging them.

Eventually, he was slain—by his own daughter.

To brew this forbidden potion, only three ingredients are required:

1. Moonflower – A mystical flower said to absorb the light of the moon itself. Known for heightening sensitivity and inducing a euphoric mood.

2. Redleaf Plant Extract – A once-common plant famed for stoking the internal fire of arousal in target's body. Long believed to be extinct.

3. Succubus/Incubus Body Fluid – These demons of lust produce a unique sweat when aroused through their sweat. It exponentially enhances seductive power, but drastically lowers rational thought and intelligence in the target.

Brewing this potion is still considered mythical.

As no one has ever succeeded in creating it due to the extreme precision and total mana control required.

---

Leon leaned back, his eyes narrowed.

'Moonflowers… I've read about them. They bloom only at night in the Vinceral Forest, but they're incredibly hard to spot.

They're high-level demonic monsters and according to books, they only appears in B-rank or higher dungeons—and even then, finding them there is not completely guaranteed.

I'm nowhere near strong enough to solo one of those and taking a party there is suicide.

There is only one place on this planet that has succubus demons and that is Demon kingdom itself but that kingdom is far, far away from any of the other kingdom and going there is also suicide.

First of all, you can't carry enough food and water to cover the journey, but even if I fully filled my inventory with food and water, the demons will make me their dinner as soon as they see me.

The Redleaf Plant… that's the real problem. Even if I somehow find a succubus and moonflower, the Redleaf Plant is extinct…' Leon stared blankly at the page, his fingers tapping the table in a slow rhythm, lost in thought.

> [300 - Learning Stacks]

<Skill Crafter>

> [Please pick a name for the skill...]

Alchemist's Shop,

[Description: Gura Grandom, one of the greatest alchemists in human history, crafted this alchemy set.

It contains hidden pockets that can store up to fifty potions inside them, accessible only while wearing the matching gloves. Main Dueling Ground of Velthorne Academy

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Visit my room after the match, sweetheart. I'll show you how to enchant something real good," Jimmy said with a playful wink toward Ingrid.

"Then come at me. You've already enchanted my heart, beautiful," Jimmy grinned.

"Aldos Inferno!" Ingrid shouted, unleashing a barrage of twenty blazing fire bullets from her wand.

Swish-Swish-Swish-Swish

"There it is—Ingrid's flame barrage! But the bullets are flying at such irregular intervals! Can Jimmy dodge them all?" Tyron shouted, excitement filling his voice.

But Jimmy didn't even flinch. He stood still, smiling.

Boom-Boom-Boom

The bullets slammed into him, enveloping his body in smoke.

"They hit him—dead on! He didn't even move! Could this be the end for Jimmy?" Tyron asked as the crowd held their breath.

"D-Did I win...?" Ingrid asked timidly, her eyes focused on the smoke.

But then the smoke cleared—and Jimmy stood there, completely unharmed. Not a single burn or scratch on his clothes.

"What the—?" Ingrid gasped, utterly bewildered.

"Looks like Jimmy just gave us all a lesson in enchantments," Tyron laughed as he immediately understood what had just happened.

"His clothes are covered in defensive enchantments," Leon commented calmly.

"That's not possible... The uniforms are already enchanted. You can't just—" Malkof began, but Leon cut him off.

"They look like academic uniforms, but they're not. He's wearing fakes—replicas enchanted with his own magic." Leon spoke with a smile as he had already inspected his clothes.

"While the crowd's attention was focused on Jimmy, no one noticed the small pendant he had tossed beneath Ingrid's feet." Leon added.

"What!?" Malkof hissed, spotting the trinket.

*WHOOOSH*

A gust of wind blasted from the pendant—lifting Ingrid's skirt and revealing her white panties to the entire stadium.

"Kyaaah!" Ingrid screamed and dashed off the stage in embarrassment.

It's really easy." Malkof spoke with a serious expression as he looked at Leon.

Swish—Swish—Swish!

Leon calmly reached into his coat and retrieved a small vial filled with greyish liquid.

Psssh!

A thick grey mist erupted on impact, engulfing Leon in a dense fog. The wind blades sliced into the mist—yet none emerged from the other side.

[Name: Killer Mist]

[Type: Consumable]

[Description: Creates a thick, vision-blocking mist with a one-meter radius. Absorbs all breathable air within the cloud for ten seconds.]

Clink—

Stch—Puff!

*Clench*

Arthur's head exploded with a sickening blast—blood, muscle, and bone splattering across Veronica's face and all over the ground.

Chapter 136: Noble Status

He paused.

'Why the hell is he turning this into a npc monologue ?' Leon thought, his expression flat.

"When I faced the nobles for the first time, it was a war in silence—sneers, threats, politics. Every day was survival. I was terrified, but I never stopped climbing the stairs and-"

The headmaster blinked. Then, he smiled—thinly. "Impatient, are we? Fine. Let's get to the heart of it and you are definitely not getting expelled." He turned to face Leon again.

"What?" Leon's brows furrowed as he looked at him with a confused and shocked expression.

"This academy is built on my blood and bones. I've kept peace between noble lineages and commoners for years.

Then you walk in and begin thrashing the sons of dukes and daughter of a seven star household. I can't allow that kind of disruption. I'm willing to offer you a one-star noble status. I'll even cover the fifty gold fee myself and you can increase the status of your family by-"

Leon blinked. "I don't want it."

The headmaster's brows shot up. "What?"

"I said I don't want the noble status." Leon replied with a flat expression.

The headmaster's voice grew firm, perplexed. "Do you understand what I'm offering? Respect, protection, status. People will treat you differently from Commoner because you will become noble."

"How would they even know I'm a noble?" Leon asked, an amused smile twitching at his lips.

"You'll receive a certificate signed and stamped officially by the king himself—carry it, show it. It'll open doors for you to bigger opportunities.

You will able to do stuff without any Commoner interrupting you." The headmaster explained.

Leon tilted his head. "Did you carry yours around too?"

The headmaster snorted. "No. I was awarded my title after slaying a dragon in the northeast wood valley.

I built this academy alongside the best adventurers of that time kid. But even I knew better than to antagonize seven-star noble households."

"Then expel me if you're that worried about it," Leon said coolly. "I'm not interested in studying here that much."

The headmaster stared at him for a long moment. Then, he let out a tired sigh.

"I've tried my best with you, Leon Luster. My offer will remain, should you ever change your mind.

Come find me—if you survive that long, but I am not expelling you, Derisa has made that clear in her letter that you will ask for this, Hohohohoh..." He chuckled as his figure shimmered, vanishing with a swish of air.

Leon stood there for a second. 'Well… that was weird.'

He turned on his foot and started walking towards the exit gate of the academy.

'What the hell is happening today? Why is everyone so intrested in talking to me ?' Leon groaned internally.

"Follow me, Fast" The knight didn't wait for confirmation as he started walking.

Leon sighed and fell in step behind him. "Who the hell is Master Blain…?" he muttered under his breath.

They walked through long, echoing halls before stopping outside a heavy wooden door.

It was a meeting room just under the arena.

"Inside," the knight ordered.

Leon pushed the door open and stepped into a lavish room. Seated inside were Wander Blain and his wife, sipping tea in absolute silence.

"What kind of commoner walks into a noble's room without greeting them properly?" Wander asked, a mocking smile on his lips.

Leon gave a curt bow, his face unreadable.

'What kind of stupid ass wants to meet you ?' Leon thought in his mind as he checked out his wife and didn't find her much attracting as she was really skinny and had flat chest.

Wander leaned forward. "I'll be direct with this. I want you in my army. A knight serving at the southern border. Every three years, we promote talented soldiers to nobility. You've got the spark. Serve under me, and you'll have a real shot."

Leon began with a polite tone, "That's too great of an offer for someone like me, but I sincerely—"

"Cut the crap." Wander's eyes narrowed. "Your face is clearly not matching your words. Tell me the truth. Why are you refusing?"

Leon looked him dead in the eyes. "I'm lazy."

Wander blinked. "What?"

"I'm lazy. Doesn't matter how good your training is. I'll still be lazy. You don't want a knight like me. I will just get my comrades killed in the battlefield." Leon replied with a straight face.

For a moment, silence hung thick in the air.

Then Wander laughed—a deep, rumbling sound. "That's the dumbest but most honest answer I've heard in years. Fine. Go on. But don't forget… you just walked away from the chance to become a noble.

That's an opportunity that people die for kid."

Leon offered a sarcastic bow as he spoke in a low voice "I'll weep over it later in my room, Sir."

He turned and walked out after that.

Click.

Thud.

Chapter 137: Emilia
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

On Noble Street, commoners were strictly forbidden—unless they were servants or merchants with business. This street was the pride of the kingdom, cleaner than any other, lined with immaculate marble tiles and perfumed flowerbeds.

All the noble families above three-star status lived on Noble Street, and this street was cleaner than the other streets.

At the start of Noble Street, there were two huge iron gates protected by two knights in shining armor.

'Well, well... Talk about discrimination. These guys have built a whole fucking area only for themselves in the middle of the kingdom,' Leon thought as he walked up to the huge iron gates.

"Commoners aren't allowed in this area. Return at once," both of the knights spoke as they immediately stood up from their chairs and looked at Leon with serious expressions.

"I am here to visit the Weaver Household. Sir Justin Weaver has appointed me as his personal butler," Leon spoke with a confident expression as he looked at the guard with a neutral face.

One of the knights turned to look at his partner.

[Warning: You are under the skill {Truth's Judgement}.]

'Truth's Judgement? Is he using some kind of lie-detecting skill on me?' Leon thought with a confused expression.

"Let him through," the other knight spoke with a neutral expression.

"Very well," his partner said as he opened the gates and let Leon through.

'An interesting skill. I should make one of those—' As Leon was thinking that, an idea came to his mind.

'Why haven't I made this skill until now?' Leon thought as he immediately used his skill.

<Skill Crafter>

[Please pick a name for the skill...]

"Mimic."

[Skill 'Mimic' has been created.]

[Rank has been settled to 'F' by default.]

[State the use of skill.]

"A skill that can only copy the non-damage-dealing skills of a target in sight and convert it into an 'F-ranked' skill.

Only a single skill can be copied at once inside the copy slot. The mana consumption to mimic and store a skill is very high, and the copied skill can only be stored inside the copy slot for 24 hours.

The skill can be leveled up, and as it levels up, one copy slot will be added," Leon spoke with a smile as he continued to walk forward.

[Processing...]

[Error: Skill's description doesn't match the Rank. Please try again...]

"Oh, well... I knew that was bound to happen. Glitch Point," Leon spoke in a low voice as he immediately activated his skill.

<Glitch Point>

As soon as the skill was activated, two big red marks appeared over the screen, highlighting two sentence.

'24 Hours' and 'The skill can be leveled up.'

'I can take care of the time, but level up... If this skill doesn't level up, then... I will only be able to copy one skill and... If I think about it, it's better than nothing,' Leon thought in his mind.

"Okay... The skill can be stored for up to 12 hours."

[Error: Skill's description doesn't match the Rank. Please try again...]

"Fuck... 11 hours."

[Error: Skill's description doesn't match the Rank. Please try again...]

"10 hours."

[Error: Skill's description doesn't match the Rank. Please try again...]

"9 hours."

[Error: Skill's description doesn't match the Rank. Please try again...]

"8 hours."

[Cooldown has been calculated: 10 minutes after every use.]

[Would you like to learn the skill or destroy it?]

'Oh my god... Such a low cooldown. Fuck, I am in luck,' Leon thought with an excited expression.

"Learn it," He replied with a smirk.

[Congratulations! You have successfully learned the skill {Mimic}.]

Suddenly, Leon noticed a butler walking towards him with a bag full of groceries in his hand.

'Here comes the first test subject.' He thought in his mind as a smirk spread across his face.

[1%... 33%... 100%]

[Successfully copied the skill {Swap}]

[Name: Swap]

[Rank: C(F)]

[Description: Can exchange the positions of two objects—one you hold in your hand and the other you select as the target.

Only objects with almost the same weight can be exchanged.]

As soon as Leon read the skill's description, his eyes widened in shock.

'Fucking hell... Now that's one overpowered skill. I should take a good look at this butler if I want to copy this skill agai—' As soon as he turned around to look at the butler once more, Leon noticed that he was already gone.

"Where the fuck did he go?" He murmured with a confused expression.

Leon then turned around and started walking forward once again.

There were many flowers in the garden, magical and ordinary ones.

He was very muscular and wore a purple coloured dress, that had a 'W' imprinted on it's top left corner.

'Don't tell me the weaver Household has their own uniforms.' Leon thought in his mind.

"What do you want, Kid ?" The guard asked with a neutral expression as he looked at Leon.

Without any delays, Leon used his skill on him.

<Inspect>

[Name: Romas]

[Class: Swordsman]

[HP: 900/900]

[MP: 450/450]

[Description: Romas was an orphan when the leader of the weaver household adopted him from the streets and made him the guard of his house.

"What's this?" Leon asked, examining the small paper with a tiny magic circle drawn on it.

"It's a fire spell, sir. Tear it, and you'll learn it!" the boy explained with a smile.

"A spell?" Leon raised a brow, then tore the paper without hesitation.

[Congratulations! You have learned the skill {Lighter} successfully.]

[Name: Lighter]

[Rank: F]

[Description: Produces a small flame on the user's fingertip.]

[Mana Cost: 5 MP]

[Cooldown: None]

"Now just hold your fingertip up to the cigar and use the spell, sir!" the boy encouraged.

Leon placed the cigar between his lips, held up his index finger, and activated the skill.

'Lighter.'

*Bop*

A small, hovering blue flame burst to life on his fingertip, igniting the cigar in seconds.

"Huh...?" the boy stared in awe at the odd-colored flame.

"Well, isn't this convenient. Thanks, kid." Leon took a puff, smoke curling out from his mouth as he turned and continued walking.

"Was that a blue flame...?" the kid muttered, with a confused expression as he walked away.

...

"Come here, kid! I'll show you a good time!"

"No, no, come here instead!"

"Hey, gentleman! Want to strip me down ?"

...

Every brothel Leon passed threw waves of propositions his way—girls young and old, some even offering him money to sleep with them.

After a few more blocks, he finally reached his destination.

The building wasn't too big or too small, but it stood out. A large golden-lettered sign loomed above:

THE GOLDEN SLAVE TRADERS

*Puff*

*Pshhh*

Leon exhaled smoke as he eyed the place.

Two massive guards stood at the gate—lizardmen, not humans. Towering bodies covered in scales, gripping heavy metal spears in both hands and they had slave collars in their necks.

Leon immediately used his skill on the guards.

<Inspect>

[Name: Kol]

[Class: Spearman]

[Race: Lizardman]

[Level: 21]

[HP: 30,000 / 30,000]

[MP: 15,000 / 15,000]

[Description: Lizardmen are renowned for their exceptional physical strength and natural resilience. Their bodies are protected by dense, durable scales that offer powerful defense against physical attacks. As a Spearman, Kol combines raw brute force with precision, making him a formidable frontline warrior.]

'Lizardmen... Huh.' Leon thought with a smile as he walked upto the gate.

Suddenly, both of them crossed their spears as they looked at Leon.

'Well... Time to act cocky.' Leon thought in his mind.

"I want to buy another one like her." He spoke with a smile as he released smoke on their faces.

*Pshhh*

The guards looked at him for a moment then removed the spears and bowed down to him.

"We are sorry, Sir. Welcome..." They spoke, letting Leon enter inside the house at once.

Chapter 155: Slave Trader
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As soon as Leon stepped inside the slave trading firm, a sweet, intoxicating scent filled his senses.

"Welcome, sir," a soft voice greeted him.

A young, strikingly attractive girl approached with a smile, her short red dress clinging tightly to her curves.

"Hi," Leon replied casually, returning the smile as his eyes swept over her.

"Are you here to buy a slave, sir? Or sell one?" she asked, her gaze drifting briefly to Fruela.

Fruela's expression turned cold instantly, shooting the girl a hateful glare.

"I'm here to buy," Leon said, still smiling. "And not just any slave—I want the most expensive ones you've got. Females, of course."

"O-Oh... Th-that—" she stammered, but before she could finish, a voice rang out behind her.

"Jennifer... I think I'll take it from here. Why don't you go and deal with other customers ?" said a man descending the staircase.

He looked to be in his forties, sharply dressed in a crimson suit. In his hand, he carried a sleek cane with a wolf's head carved into its handle.

"Y-Yes, Sir." Jennifer spoke as she immediately walked away.

'Jackpot,' Leon thought, smiling as he activated his skill.

<Inspect>

[Name: Richard Henson]

[Class: Slave Trader]

[Race: Human]

[HP: 10,000 / 10,000]

[MP: 5,000 / 5,000]

[Description: Richard comes from a long bloodline of slave traders. His father, grandfather, and even great-grandfather worked as the proud owner of Golden Slave Trading Firm. He views slaves solely as profit-generating commodities. Skilled in crafting slave contracts and collars.]

'Well… that's an interesting class,' Leon mused, smirking.

"Please, follow me to the V.I.P. room, sir...?" Richard asked for his name with a practiced smile.

"Rubin," Leon replied with a smile.

"Ah, Mr. Rubin. This way, please," Richard gestured with his cane toward a side door.

"Sure," Leon said, nodding as he, Fruela, and Richard walked toward the private room.

---

Inside the V.I.P. Room

The room was spacious and elegantly furnished—gilded walls, plush carpet, and three oversized couches that looked as if they'd swallowed kings.

Richard took a seat, but Leon remained standing.

"Please, have a seat. Let's discuss the type of slaves you're looking to purchase," Richard offered. "We have such a vast inventory that sometimes even I forget what's in the vault—"

"Dark Elves," Leon interrupted, his voice calm and deliberate. He walked toward the window, staring out with a subtle smile, both of his hands inside his pocket.

"Ah... Excellent taste," Richard said, adjusting his posture. "Unfortunately, Dark Elves are only sold through private auctions. Very exclusive, very high-end. Judging by the one you're traveling with—and how well she's dressed—I'd say you've developed quite the appreciation for the product."

He gave a sly smile. "You'll need to return for tomorrow's auction, though—"

"I have a very profitable, one-time deal for you, Mr. Richard Henson," Leon cut in, still facing the window.

He reached into his pocket, pulled out a cigar, placed it between his lips, and raised a finger to its tip.

"Lighter."

*Boop*

A sharp, blue flame sparked from his fingertip, igniting the cigar with a soft hiss.

*Puff*

"A deal for me ? Well, I will be more than intrested in listening to this deal. Please tell me." Richard asked with a smile as he placed one leg over the other and looked at Leon.

"Release all the dark Elfs you have caught in the forest this morning." Leon spoke with a neutral expression as he released smoke into the room.

"Your Life." Leon replied with a smile as he turned around and Fruela immediately pulled out her blades.

"Well... Those are some nice toys you have bought for your slave but I don't think it will be a good time and place to joke about such things, Mr. Rubin." Richard spoke as the smile disappeared from his face and he immediately stood up from the couch.

"Oh... Look at her face. Do you think she is joking ?" Leon asked with a smile as he pointed towards Fruela.

"You are a human, how can you take the Elf's side ?" Richard asked with a serious expression.

"I am not taking sides, Richard. I am just giving you a chance to correct what you have done." Leon spoke with a cold smile as he placed the cigar down inside the ashtray.

"They are my products... I have captured them. I can now sell them however I want. You have no right to tell me what to do with my product.

What do you think ? You are first ones doing this ? I will now call the guards in and both of you will be lying dead on the floor. GUARDS." Richard shouted loudly as he pointed his cane towards Leon.

*step-step-step-step-step*

Leon slowly turned to look at Fruela.

"Three... Humans." Fruela spoke with a serious expression as she tightened her grip over the daggers.

*Click*

One of them was wearing a black mask on his face.

All they saw was Richard pointing his cane towards Leon and all three of them ran towards him.

*Swish-Swish-Swish*

Slowly blood flowed out of their necks and eventually their heads rolled down on the ground.

*Thud-Thud-Thud*

He slowly rotated his neck and noticed Fruela standing right behind him pointing her dagger at his neck.

The blade's tip slowly piercing into his neck a single drop of blood coming out of it.

"You have seen my face Richard... How can I trust you that you won't tell the royal guards about me ?" Leon asked with a smile as he looked at Richard.

*Stab*

Fruela immediately stabbed the dagger all the through Richard's neck, killing him in one swift motion.

*Thud*

His corpse hitted the ground and blood poured out like water.

"Hah... Time for Plan B." Leon spoke with a neutral expression as he walked upto the room's door and noticed that the girl from earlier is still standing outside.

"Umm... Jennifer, can you come in here for a moment ?" Leon asked with a smile as he looked at her.

"Oh, sure." Jennifer replied as she immediately started walking towards the room.

Chapter 156: Keyholes
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

*Creak*

"How can I hel—" before Jennifer could finish her sentence, Fruela yanked her into the room and clamped a hand tightly over her mouth.

"Mnnnnfff! MNNNNNNFF!"

The desperate cries were useless under Fruela's palm.

Jennifer's gaze darted around in panic until it landed on Leon—calmly standing by the window, puffing on a cigar with a completely indifferent look on his face.

*Puff*

Outside, snow had begun to fall.

'It's snowing already... I need to be home before both of them get worried. I have to get done with this fast,' Leon thought as he exhaled smoke and turned his gaze toward Jennifer.

She was drenched in sweat despite the cold air, her body was shaking like a leaf caught in a blizzard.

"For the next one minute," Leon said, walking toward her, "I want you to listen very carefully to the words coming out of my mouth, Jennifer."

He placed the cigar back in the ashtray and looked her dead in the eyes.

"If you miss even a single word... you might end up on the floor like the others. Do you understand, Jennifer ?"

*Nod-nod-nod*

Jennifer nodded frantically, tears spilling from her eyes. Her mascara ran in dark streams down her cheeks.

*Nod-nod-nod*

"Well then," he said with a smile, "let's not waste any time, right Jennifer ?"

"Y-Yes..." Jennifer said quickly and turned toward the silent hallway, leading them toward the basement stairwell.

Leon followed with Fruela by his side. As they entered the next room, Leon pulled the Nyxter from his inventory and kept it in hand—just in case.

The room had a trapdoor embedded in the floor—a heavy metal hatch with five different keyholes in it.

Leon raised an eyebrow. "Only two keys... but five locks?"

Jennifer looked terrified. "I-I was never allowed to go down there... M-Mr. Richard always brought the slaves up. He told me about this door though. H-He said if someone places a key in the wrong hole, poisonous gas will be released... and everyone down there will die."

"Looking at your facial expressions, I can safely say that he never told you which one is the right keyhole, right ?" Leon asked with a smile as he looked at Jennifer.

"Y-Yes..." Jennifer replied with a scared expression.

Leon then turned to look at Fruela.

"I can only sense a bottle attached to the gate, nothing else." Fruela replied with a neutral expression.

"Very well, Glitch Point." Leon spoke in a low voice as he activated the skill.

<Glitch Point>

The skill came into effect immediately. Five of the six keyholes turned red.

"That one," Leon said, pointing to the upper-right keyhole.

"H-How do you know?" Fruela asked, her voice tight with concern.

"I'm guessing," Leon said casually.

"W-What?!" Fruela snapped, her eyes wide with panic.

"Place the key in Jennifer," Leon ordered.

"Y-Yes..." Jennifer nodded and slowly inserted the key into the instructed keyhole.

"There are children down there. If they die—this is all for nothing!" Fruela's voice was strained, barely holding back emotion.

"Turn it," Leon said calmly as he ignored Fruela.

"Y-Yes..." Jennifer twisted the key and Fruela immediately closed her eyes as she started praying.

*Click*

Fruela's eyes snapped open as soon as she heard the clicking sound.

"I-It's open," Jennifer whispered, her voice full of disbelief.

She lifted the hatch. The door creaked open without resistance.

"H-How did you—" Fruela began.

"Lady Luck," Leon said with a grin. "Sometimes she just loves me."

Fruela immediately descended the stairs with a confused expression on her face.

Leon gestured to Jennifer, ordering her to go down before him.

She obeyed without hesitation and went down the stairs.

As Leon stepped onto the stairs, something caught his eye.

Attached to the trapdoor's internal mechanism were several vials filled with a faintly glowing purple liquid.

He immediately used his skill on one of them.

<Inspect>

[Name: Auqalic]

[Type: Poison]

[Rank: C]

[Description: Auqalic is a dangerous airborne toxin capable of erasing all life in an enclosed space within seconds. It fully dissolves in air after three hours.

Mostly used in sleeping assassination and camp wipe outs.]

With delicate care, he removed the vials of Auqalic posion from the trigger assembly and slid it into his inventory.

Chapter 157: Master, Oh Master

*Boop*

Leon didn't respond. He simply stared at the rising smoke, lost in thought.

The other elves looked at her in confusion as if trying to figure out why she was bowing down to Leon.

In return, I, Fruela—daughter of Ralf and Eria—swear my loyalty to you. If danger comes your way, I will face it, even if it costs me my life."

Then Fruela took Leon's hand, brought it to her lips, and...

...kissed it slightly.

*Teeeeaar*

*Click*

As soon as the slave contract was torn apart. The slave collar around Fruela's neck unlocked and hit the floor. She smiled as she looked at Leon.

Leon stepped forward, flicking the burnt cigar aside.

"Ladies, you can argue later. We need to get out of here and if you want to move them to the dungeon, well... there will be guards on the exit gate of the kingdom and there is a chance that people will see us in such a big group."

"I-It should be closed by now... B-But as you said, if so many elves leave at once, they'll definitely be seen,

And the heavy snow outside will make it difficult for the children to walk." Jennifer replied nervously.

Everyone turned.

Chapter 158: Rees Krambelbar
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"I can get you out of here," a voice echoed from another cell.

Everyone turned to the right. A shadowy figure cloaked entirely in black sat motionless inside. No face, no hands, no legs—nothing was visible beneath the fabric.

Without hesitation, Leon activated his skill.

<Inspect>

[Name: Rees Krambelbar]

[Class: Magi]

[Race: Lich]

[Level: 97]

[HP: -1 /970000]

[MP: 0 / 485000]

[Description: Rees Krambelbar, once a noble who craved for knowledge but as his age closed in on him, he found the best way to continue his research and studies.

He became a Lich, an undead who holds terrifying powers, enormous amount of knowledge and thirst to know more about the world.

But being seprated from his necroheart Rees was left powerless as he can't collect mana without it and when he was searching for it, Richard found him and enslaved him.]

"An undead, huh ?" Leon muttered, smirking.

"Huh ? You can tell ?" the figure responded curiously, rising and stepping forward.

As Rees stepped into the light, even Leon flinched slightly. A white skeleton, draped in black robes, with three glowing red eyes—one in the center of his forehead.

Although the eyes looked more like red orbs just floating in his skull.

"Fucking hell..." Leon murmured under his breath.

"People usually react like that," Rees said calmly. "But you're holding your composure well. Not scared of the undead ?

Any specific reasons for that ?" He asked.

"I've seen worse," Leon replied with a grin.

'In movies and mangas, I have seen things like him so much that I only felt a little surprised when I first saw him but now I don't feel much. It's a magical world after all and undead beings here is not a new thing.' he added in his mind.

"So… how do you plan to get us out of here ? And what do you want in return ? Freedom ?" Leon asked with a curious expression.

"I overheard your conversation that you were having with this dark elf girl. You mentioned 'Your Dungeon'. Did I hear that right?" Rees asked as he looked at Leon.

"You did... What about it ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"T-That's impossible. To own a dungeon, you need to bind with the core. To do that, you have to be—"

"A monster ?" Leon asked with a smile as he cut him mid sentence once again.

"Yes." Rees replied as he looked at Leon.

"Not necessarily. I bound with it as a human. Now, what's your angle ? And how do you plan to help us ?" Leon asked with a straight face.

"When I looked at you… I saw knowledge. You're hiding it well, but it's there in your mind, I can tell the face of a scholar.

That's why your eyes weren't filled with fear when you saw me, because religious people most often referred to me as the work of devil and always ran away from me." Rees said as he looked at Leon.

"You are a walking skeleton... What do you expect ? Now get to the point." Leon replied with a flat expression.

"Let me stay in your dungeon. Just for a while, until I find what's mine. In that time, we can trade knowledge. I know almost every dark art, and I can teach you things that'll crush your enemies. What do you say ?" Rees asked his voice laced with greed.

Leon grinned. "How do I trust you?"

"W-What?"

"Your words are a bit too sweet. And since you're without your necroheart, you're powerless. Why should I even bother with you in the first place ?" Leon asked with a smile.

"H-How do you—how do you know about the necroheart ?" Rees stammered as he looked at Leon.

"W-Wait!" Rees panicked.

"I was captured while searching for materials to craft a new necroheart in the Vincerel Forest since my old one got stolen while I was working in my dungeon. If I stay here, I'll run out of soul essence soon and die.

Help me, and I'll give you every last piece of knowledge I know about dark arts and this world!" Rees spoke as he offered another deal to Leon, this time desperation clear in his voice.

'He broke that fast... pathetic liar,' Leon thought in his mind as he looked at Rees.

"Hmm… hard to decide. What if you tried to-" Before Leon could've completed his sentence, Rees interrupted him.

"Take this," Rees said quickly. He reached up and removed the glowing red orb from his forehead, holding it out.

Leon narrowed his eyes and casted his skill on it...

<Inspect>

[Name: Life Essence Crystal]

[Rank: B]

[Description: Found in high-level intelligent undead, this crystal stores life force and preserves the user's sanity.

Once bound, it becomes the undead's core lifeline. Any damage to it causes severe, irreversible harm. An undead can possess upto five of them but as they are connected to each other, even if one of them get's destroyed the undead will suffer near death pain and maybe even loose his sanity.]

[Bound to: Rees Krambelbar]

[Life Force: 7 Days]

"It's a life essence crystal—"

"That stores your life force, yeah. Only seven days left, huh ?" Leon said, smirking.

"H-How do you know that?! Only I should know that!" Rees gasped with a confused and worried expression as he hesitated now.

Leon took the orb out of his hand immediately and slipped it into his inventory.

"Jennifer, open the cell." He ordered.

'He might be useless now, but can be useful in future and he has information on dark arts. I will just give him some floor on the dungeon.' Leon thought with a smile.

"Y-Yes..." she stammered, unlocking the gate.

*Click*

Rees stepped out. The elves and children clung to each other in sheer terror—some even crying, trembling, others hiding behind their mothers. Only Leon remained calm, unfazed.

"So," Leon said, "that plan to get us out of here you mentioned—was that a lie too, or…?"

"N-No, it's real. I know a teleportation spell. But..." Rees trailed off, placing his bony hand over his skull. "There's a small problem."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 159: The Magic Circle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"What kind of problem?" Leon asked, suspicion written across his face as he narrowed his eyes at Rees.

"Umm... you see," Rees began, rubbing the side of his skull. "The teleportation spell I intend to use... it consumes a massive amount of mana. On both ends."

"Both ends?" Leon repeated with a confused look. "What do you mean?"

"Aha... well, that’s another part of the issue," Rees said with a nervous chuckle. "This spell works using two magic circles—one drawn where we are now, and one drawn at the destination where we want to teleport."

Leon crossed his arms and raised an eyebrow. "Something tells me the problems aren’t over yet."

"T-There might be... one last, very, very small issue," Rees said sheepishly.

Leon’s voice sharpened. "What is it?"

"If mana isn’t supplied to both circles at the exact same time... the spell fails. There’s only a one-second window to ignite both circles and if-"

"...And if it’s not done simultaneously, it won’t work," Leon finished the sentence with a sigh.

’These aren’t just problems—they’re conditions we have to fulfill in order to use the motherfucking spell,’ he thought, now wearing a serious expression.

"I can draw the circle here," Rees continued, "but I can’t feed mana into it. If all of these elves supply mana at once, it should be enough to activate this side."

"And the other side?" Leon asked, gaze unblinking.
