Leon smirked. "You can start by teaching me everything you know—Voodoo magic, necromancy, all of it."

"I will pass on every scrap of knowledge I possess and make you the best scholar of humans." Rees vowed, his voice carrying an unfamiliar weight.

"Good. Now, let’s see this Necroheart of yours, how it works." Leon said, crossing his arms.

Rees reached beneath the counter and pulled out an aged wooden box, its surface unmarked save for a single name carved into the corner: Rees.

Leon’s eyes flickered with recognition. "You got this from Lisa’s shop, didn’t you?"

"Yes. I crafted it years ago when I made my first Necroheart, but in order to keep it safe... I gave it to Lia." Rees explained, lifting the lid. Inside, nestled on a cushion of deep red velvet, was a spherical casing of polished silver.

"This is the shell of a Necroheart," Rees said, lifting it carefully. "Silver is the standard, but the most potent necrohearts are forged from gold or platinum. Back then, I could only afford silver—costed me a hundred coins to make this one. I had gold coins afte that... But i didn’t bothered to change it." Rees explained.

Leon leaned in, examining the intricate engravings over it.

"What are these ? Runes ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Enchantments... Engraved by the finest Enchanter in the whole elf kingdom. Alkari." Rees spoke as he admired it’s beauty.

"So, what’s the next step?" Leon asked with a curious expression as he looked at it from all the angles.

"The Mana Complexer goes inside, the necroheart." Rees said, pressing a small button on the sphere’s surface.

***Click.***

A series of mechanical whirs echoed as the silver casing split open, revealing an inner chamber lined with delicate gears. Rees poured the water like liquid from the bowl into the hollow core, and despite its small size, the chamber swallowed every last drop without overflowing.

Another *click*, and the Necroheart sealed shut, just like it was before.

Then, Rees turned to Leon with sudden urgency. "You have infinite mana, correct?"

"Yeah. Why?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"I need you to channel some of it into the heart, Mr. Leon. You have to do it fast in order to activate it." He handed the Necroheart to Leon immediately.

"How? Should I just directly supply mana to the circle or Focus on the-

"Just focus your mana into it. A steady, low stream is ideal—too much at once, and the temperature will spike dangerously." Rees explained as he looked at Leon.

Leon frowned. "Wait, *dangerously* ?"

"Yes If it overheats, the reaction could detonate with enough force to erase the entire Vinceral Forest—and us along with it." Rees spoke in a neutral voice as if it’s nothing big.

Leon’s grip on the sphere tightened. "So I’m holding a fucking nuclear bomb in my hand."

"What’s a nuclear bomb ?" Rees asked with a confused expression.

"Nothing... I meant, A fucking magic bomb." Leon corrected.

"Essentially, yes." Rees replied.

"The fuck do you mean by yes ? If this thing explodes, I will kill you." Leon spoke with a serious expression.

"Don’t worry, Mr. Leon. It won’t explode if you keep giving it a small amount of mana continuously but you will need to do it fast... If the activation ritual didn’t take place fast, the necroheart will be left useless." Rees spoke with urgency.

Leon exhaled sharply. "Fine. But the second this thing gets too hot, I’m cutting the flow."

Rees nodded. "A wise precaution. I should have thought of that .... I wonder why I didn’t." Rees thought as he placed his skeleton fingers on his skull.

"Because you’re an idiot," Leon muttered under his breath before focusing.

[ -700 MP ]

[ +345,897 MP ]

[ 700 MP ]

[ +345,897 MP ]

...

The cycle repeated, mana pulsing into the Necroheart in a rhythmic surge.

"How long does this take for this to activate or whatever ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Once it activates, it’ll glow green, and—" Rees cut himself off as the sphere suddenly radiated a deep violet light.

"Uh. That’s not supposed to happen." Rees spoke as he pointed towards the necroheart.

Leon stared at the pulsating purple glow.

"What the hell does that mean?" He asked with a shocked expression as he looked at Rees.

Rees scrambled for his books on the slabs as he tried to find out what was wrong with the necroheart. "I-I don’t know! This has never happened it can happen that the heart is not getting enough mana or maybe it’s not—"

Before he could finish, a translucent screen flickered before Leon...

[ Would you like to bind with the Necroheart? ]

[ Yes / No ?]

[ Note: Once bound, the Necroheart will obey your commands. You may transfer ownership to an undead subordinate if desired but the necroheart will still acknowledge your command as priority.]

Leon’s lips curled up as soon as he readed the notification.

"Yes." He spoke without any hesitation.

[ Necroheart has been bound to Leon Luster. ]

The violet light faded, replaced by a steady emerald glow.

Rees turned back, relief in his voice. "Ah! It stabilized. The mana flow must have resolved the anomaly. I don’t understand why it showed such bright colours... Phew, I thought it was about to explode for a second."

"So it’s ready?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Yes. And thanks to your infinite mana, the process was exponentially faster.

Your infinite mana is a very nice gift. Can you teach me how you achieved it ?" Rees asked took the Necroheart back, admiration in his tone.

"No. Even if I did... You won’t be able to do it." Leon replied with a flat face.

"I understand." Rees replied with a neutral tone.

"You know... I can show you some really good spells that no one even dares to use because of their absurd mana usage.

Once you learn them... You will become one of the strongest beings in this world." Rees replied with a neutral expression.

"Sounds useful, but I can’t supply infinite mana to spells remember that." Leon replied with a neutral expression.

"I can give you spells that use continuous amounts of mana for a long time. But most of them are class restricted spells." He explained.

"Although if you can become a magic class... I can teach you all the spells." Rees continued.

"Why magi class ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Magi is the only class that can allow you to learn every class based and restricted spells, Mr. Leon." Rees replied with a neutral expression.

’What !? I didn’t know that.’ Leon thought as his eyes widened a bit.

Rees then removed his tattered robe, exposing his skeletal frame.

Leon raised an eyebrow. "What are you doing?"

"To fully integrate the Necroheart, I must revert to my base form—just as all undead as I was upon the creation after i died." He laid flat on the ground, placing the glowing necroheart within his ribcage.

"Two questions," Leon said. "How do you plant to die since you are already an undead, and how do you plan to come back if you died?" He asked with a confused expression.

"An undead under a necromancer’s control can perish at their master’s will. Since I am my own master... I can simply choose to die at will. The Necroheart will revive me." Rees explained as he looked at ceiling.

With that, the crimson balls in Rees’ eyes hitted the back of his skull.

*Thud-Thud*

Suddenly a new prompt appeared before Leon’s eyes.

[ Would you like to raise the undead with the Necroheart ? ]

[ Yes / No ?]

’I didn’t plan on binding it to myself in the first place, but I can’t trust Rees completely either. Better be safe than sorry.’ Leon thought in his mind as he selected yes on the screen.

The Necroheart flared, its verdant light spreading through Rees’ skeleton like liquid fire. Then—

*Crack. Crack. Crack.*

Fissures spiderwebbed across his bones.

"What the hell?!" Leon stepped back.

Rees’ voice echoed, calm. "Do not worry, Mr. Leon. This is the reinforcement process done by the necroheart. Look closely, you will see something interesting."

The cracks deepened, revealing tiny, luminous runes etching themselves into the bone.

Leon leaned closer, eyes widening. "Holy fucking shit."

"The Mana Complexer will flow through these runes," Rees explained as his skeletal form reassembled, the fractures sealing with a faint glow. "Just as blood flows through living flesh."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 179: Exiled
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Rees slowly rose from the ground, his movements deliberate. Dust clung to his tattered coat, and as he straightened his back, he stretched his arms wide into the still air.

Leon frowned, watching him. "What are you doing?" he asked, his voice laced with confusion.

Rees closed his eyes, inhaling deeply through his imaginary nose. "It’s been so long since I felt mana," he murmured, his voice calm yet filled with quiet wonder. "I forgot how alive it makes you feel."

’You are dead motherfucker.’ Leon thought in his mind.

Leon stood a few feet away, his expression unreadable. "Now that you’ve re-created your necroheart," he said evenly, "how about you teach me some Voo-Doo magic?"

Rees chuckled. "Ah, of course. Just give me a moment—I’ll go grab the materials we’ll need—"

"Not today." Leon’s voice cut in flatly. "It’s already evening. I’ve got somewhere to be. We’ll start tomorrow."

Rees nodded. "As you say," he replied, already turning toward the stone slab. "Then I’ll begin setting up the death traps instead."

Without another word, Leon turned around.

"Take me out," he muttered—and vanished immediately.

---

*Swish*

A gust of cold air hit him as he reappeared at the dungeon’s exit. The world outside had shifted into twilight. Shadows stretched long over the snow-flecked ground, and the bitter wind carried a promise of more snowfall.

Leon narrowed his eyes toward the horizon. "Finally... time to go home," he said quietly, placing his hands in his pocket as he began walking out of the Vinceral Forest.

’It’s been a long day,’ he thought as he trudged through the woods. ’I spent all of it inside the dungeon. But it wasn’t a waste... Now that I’ve reinforced it with traps, I won’t need to check on it constantly anymore. I can finally focus on leveling up and building up my physique.’

His lips curled into a subtle smirk.

’And those wooden bombs—if Kai can craft small enough spheres, I could inscribe them with six hundred seals each. If I get even one fo those inside a monster... nothing of it will survive that blast and I will gain tons of exp.

I have to try this tomorrow and I should make a throw skill that can throw things with increasing speed.’

His mind buzzed with the possibilities, and by the time he reached the edge of the village, ten minutes had passed.

That’s when he noticed it.

The guards weren’t at the gate.

Leon slowed his steps. "Where did thses two idiots go ?" he muttered, his eyes narrowing.

As he entered the village, a commotion ahead caught his attention. A large crowd had gathered outside the village chief’s house. Tension rippled through the air.

He approached, and all at once, eyes turned on him—cold, hostile, accusing.

’What the hell is wrong with these people ?’ he thought, his brows furrowing.

At the front of the crowd, he spotted Elaine and Selene. Both stood with their heads lowered in shame. Julia stood nearby, calm and poised, her butler Charles fidgeting nervously beside her.

Leon stepped forward. "What’s going on herr?"

Kael Klen, the village chief, turned toward Julia. "Is he the one ?" he asked grimly.

"What—?" Leon barely got the word out before—

SLAP!

Kael’s hand cracked against Leon’s cheek, echoing through the silent crowd.

*Crack*

Leon’s fist clenched involuntarily, his knuckles whitening, bones straining with fury. He opened his inventory, preparing to draw the Nyxter, but—

"Yes, Father," Julia said, pointing at Leon with unwavering calm. "He was the one who tried to rape me."

The words froze Leon in place.

He stared at her, his eyes wide with disbelief, rage simmering beneath the surface. "What the fuck are you talking about?"

Kael surged forward, grabbing Leon’s collar and pulling him close. "Listen to me, you filthy trash bastard. How dare you touch my daughter ?"

"She’s lying," Leon growled, locking eyes with Kael. "I am not even attracted to her body."

Kael turned to Charles. "You saw it too, didn’t you, Charles ?"

Charles hesitated. His gaze fell to the ground. "Y-Yes... Sir. I saw him. He tried to force himself on Madam Julia and I saved her.

He then ran away."

The crowd erupted in furious murmurs.

Kael’s voice boomed over them. "This man is a threat—to your wives, your daughters. He’s like a wild animal.

That’s why I have decided to exile Leon Luster and his entire family from this village. Pack your things. Get out. Now."

"Out with him!" shouted one villager.

"Monster!"

"Rapist!"

"Get lost!"

...

Elaine trembled, her voice cracking. "W-We’re very so—"

"Mom," Leon interrupted firmly his face looking at the ground with a serious expression. "We’re leaving. Pack our things."

"But—"

"I said we leave. Now." He turned without another word.

Elaine and Selene followed behind, their faces stained with grief.

...

As they walked, Leon muttered under his breath, "I didn’t rape her."

"We know," they said in unison from behind.

He stopped with a shocked expression and turned around immediately, "What?"

Selene’s face was tight with frustration. "That bitch Julia came to our house after you left this morning. She was furious—rambling about making you her personal slave or something. Then she offered Mom a deal."

Elaine nodded, reluctantly. "If you became her butler, she said she’d have her father exempt us from taxes—for life."

Leon’s jaw clenched. "And you turned it down?"

"I turned her down immediately," Elaine whispered. "I thought she’d leave after that. But instead she started threatening us—said she’d accuse you of rape and have us all exiled from the village if we didn’t agree."

Before Leon could respond, another voice rang out from behind him.

"She still refused, Can you believe that? Your mother still refused even after i gave her such a huge threat."

They turned to see Julia, standing behind them with Charles at her side, a smug smile on her lips.

Leon’s gaze sharpened. He stepped forward, with a serious expression on his face.

"P-Please stay back!" Charles stammered, lifting his wand in defense.

Julia waved him off. "Calm down, Charles. He’s just a poor, broken boy now. Don’t be cruel to him. It’s not like he is going to kill me, right ?"

Leon didn’t blink. "I never laid a hand on you."

Julia tilted her head, her expression warped into a twisted grin. "I know. But you were supposed to be mine. The moment I saw you from my carriage, I wanted you—under my shoe, working for me. I gave you chances. So many chances. But you clung to your ego like a fool. Now your family will pay for it."

She stepped closer.

"I could still help, you know. Right here, right now. Just kneel. Kiss my boots. Accept me as your master... and I’ll let you and your pitiful family stay in this village. Otherwise—well, I doubt you’ll survive a night in this weather."

’This psychopath bitch.’ Leon thought in his mind.

"If I can’t have you, Leon... I’ll make sure no one will have you." She spoke as she placed her fingers over Leon’s lips.

Leon raised two fingers.

Charles flinched, his eyes locked on the gesture.

Julia raised a brow. "What’s that supposed to mean ?"

Leon’s voice dropped to a razor’s edge. "Before the midnight bell rings... your family will be the ones outside this village."

Julia sneered. "Are you insane? What nonsense—"

"If I fail..." Leon said, walking past her, "I’ll crawl back to you. And become your slave.."

He didn’t wait for her reaction. Elaine and Selene followed behind, silent but resolute.

---

Back at the house, they packed quickly.

"What now?" Selene asked, her voice tight with panic. "It’s already snowing. We won’t make it to the kingdom without a wagon."

"Leo..." Elaine whispered with a worried expression. "What do we do?"

Leon looked at them with a smile, "Don’t worry... I will take you guys somewhere that you will completely forget this house."

"Want to tell us ?" Selene asked with a curious expression.

"Nope. You will have to see for yourself."

---

30 minutes later...

As all four of them walked out of the gates, villagers watched them with hollow eyes, as if watching ghosts disappear into the night.

"Come. Follow me, we have to get there before the snow settles down." Leon said, his voice steady.

"Where are we going ?" Selene asked again.

"I’ll explain when we get there."

He didn’t turn back and continued to walk forward.

In his mind, many ideas were hovering and mostly all of them were related to killing Kael and Julia.

’It’s good we’re out of that stupid broken wooden box. Now I can keep them inside the dungeon, a more safe place.’ Leon thought in his mind.

’But, that bitch Julia... She will pay for this. I could’ve killed her and her father right on the moment he slapped me but that would’ve been the stupidest move.

What fun is in killing someone if you can see them suffer for the rest of their stupid lifes and I am going to make her life so miserable that she will regret ever fucking with me.’ Leon thought with a serious expression as he continued to walk towards the dungeon.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 180: Loosing Power
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Snow fell heavily, blanketing the Vinceral Forest in white silence. Leon walked ahead through the thickening woods, his boots crunching the frost-covered ground. Elaine followed a few steps behind, carefully maneuvering through the low brush. Selene trailed at the rear, eyes darting through the trees, every sound putting her on edge.

"Leo... why are we going into the forest at this hour?" Elaine asked, brushing snow from her clothes as she jumped over a fallen branch.

"You do know this place is crawling with monsters, right?" Selene added, her tone sharp as she gripped the hilt of her dagger with one hand and holded her daughter in other hand. "Don’t get us killed just because we trust you, Leon."

"As long as you’re following me," Leon replied, not turning back, "there’s a 99% chance you won’t die. So don’t worry."

Selene narrowed her eyes. "And the other 1%? What about that ?"

Leon finally cracked a smile. "No one knows."

"You are not even telling us where-" Before Selene could’ve completed her sentence, Leon stopped walking.

Elaine tensed as she looked at Leon with a confused and scared expression. "W-What is it? Something wrong ?"

Leon shook his head. "No. We’re here."

"Selene," Leon said calmly, "come here."

"Okay..." she muttered, cautiously walking up to him.

Selene stepped up beside him, staring at the massive tree towering above.

"Are we... going to live under a tree now ?" She asked with a confused expression as she looked at Leon.

Without warning, Leon lifted his leg and thrust it deep into the snow, his foot striking solid stone underneath it.

’Yep... That’s the first step of the dungeon.’ He thought with a neutral expression.

"Why would you do that ?" Selene asked, her expression twisted in confusion as she looked at Leon’s leg.

"You ask too many questions," Leon replied.

"Both of you—place your hands on my shoulders." Leon spoke as he looked at them.

Elaine and Selene exchanged glances but obeyed without protest, placing their hands on his shoulders.

Leon whispered, "Take me to the last floor."

In an instant, all four of them vanished.

---

Dungeon – Seventh Floor

*Swish*

They reappeared in a warmly lit stone chamber. Flickering torches lined the walls, casting gentle shadows over the walls.

"Whoa..." Selene breathed, her eyes wide in wonder as she looked around the huge room.

"W-Where are we? How did we get here ?" Elaine whispered, clutching her chest and grabbing Leon’s hand in confusion.

"It’s... warm in here. Is this a dungeon ?" Selene asked, turning to Leon with a smile.

"Yes, How do you know ?" Leon asked with a neutral expression as he looked at her.

"Saw it in a book... Looks really cool in person." She replied as her eyes continued to scan the room.

"What !? This is a dungeon ? The place where monsters live ? Oh my god... we have to get out of here it’s so danger-" Elaine cried out in disbelief but before she could’ve completed her sentence, Leon interrupted her.

"Calm down, Mom. It’s my dungeon—and you’ll be sleeping here tonight." Leon replied with a calm expression as he looked at Elaine.

Both women shouted in unison, "What, Your Dungeon !?"

Leon raised a hand. "Look, It’s a long story. I don’t have time to explain it all and—"

Before Leon could’ve completed his sentence, A voice interrupted him.

"Welcome back, Master. Please accept my greetings." a calm voice said from behind them.

Elaine and Selene spun around—and screamed as soon as they saw Elis’ translucent body and a small blue ball hovering inside it.

"G-Ghost!" they shouted, diving behind Leon and grabbing him tightly.

Leon sighed. "She’s not a ghost. That’s Elis—the dungeon core. She maintains this place. She’ll take care of you while I’m away."

Elis bowed deeply. "I will, Master."

"Make some beds for them, Elis. I’ll be—"

"Master. We are back," another voice interrupted him.

’FUCK... Why is everyone coming now ?’ Leon thought with a serious expression.

He immediately turned toward the entrance, where Dusk walked in beside Fruela.

Elaine held her head immediately loosing her balance and her eyes closing."Oh my god... Monster, We are all going to die."

"Dusk, change into your cat form immediately," Leon ordered.

"Y-Yes, Master," Dusk replied and instantly shifted into a small black cat.

*Poof*

Selene’s jaw dropped. "T-The monster turned into a cat... holy fucking shit."

"So that’s where I learned it from." Leon spoke in a low voice as he looked at Selene.

"Should we run ?" Selene asked with a serious expression as she looked at him.

Leon sighed again. "Look, everything in this dungeon is under my command. You’re safe here. Fruela, take care of them while I’m gone."

"Yes, Master," Fruela said, bowing respectfully.

"W-Wait—you’re leaving ?" Elaine asked gaining her balance again.

"I told you already, Mom. Nothing can hurt you here and I have a very important work that I have to do." Leon explained.

Elaine stepped forward, placing her hand on Leon’s cheek. "Then go. But come back safe, Leo."

"I will. Don’t worry." Leon replied with a smile.

"And once you do," she added, her voice becoming scary and serious but her smile remained the same, "you will owe us some answers."

"Y-Yeah s-sure... Take me out." Leon gave a hesitated smile as he immediately disappeared.

Selene turned to her mother, still processing everything. "Did you see that ? Our Leon knows magic... and he owns a dungeon! That’s insane!"

A voice from below cut in. "Hi! I’m Dusk—Master Leon’s familiar."

Elaine blinked down at the cat. "I’m Elaine... Leon’s mother."

"I know," Dusk said, smiling. "I’ve been to your house with Master before."

"Wait, what? When?" Selene asked, with a suspicious expression.

"You wouldn’t have noticed. I can go invisible." Dusk spoke as he vanished immediately.

"Okay, this place is full of surprises..." Selene muttered, looking around.

"It is..." Dusk spoke as he re-appeared.

"Your beds are ready, You can sleep on them if you want. I can also make a bed for that human, if you eant." Elis said softly, pointing to Selene’s daughter.

"No... She sleeps with me." Selene replied.

"Shall I make your bed bigger than ?" Elis asked with a neutral expression.

"Sure." Selen replied.

"It’s done... Your Beds are there." Elis replied as she pointed toward the room’s corner.

"Thank you, we will use them when we need." Selene replied politely.

She then turned to Fruela. "So... who are you to my brother ?"

Fruela smiled. "I am his servant. Sir Leon rescued me and my sisters from a slave trader. I’ve served him ever since."

Elaine’s eyes widened. "He did what!?"

Selene nudged her. "Alright, Fruela. You’re telling us everything. From the beginning."

"Of course," Fruela nodded with a soft smile.

Meanwhile, Dusk quietly padded out of the room, his tail flicking.

"Master’s about to get into a lot of trouble," he spoke with a smile.

---

Weaver Household – Justin’s Room

Ten golden lamps lit Justin’s desk, casting a soft glow over his spellbook. He flipped a page, his eyes scanning words efficiently.

"So... that’s how you conjure fireball," he murmured.

*Click.*

Suddenly the door lock turned.

"Who dares to enter my room without—" he began, then froze.

"Mr. Leon ?"

"Hello, Justin," Leon said with a faint smile, stepping into the room.

"You didn’t show up this morning. I thought that elf did something to you."

"Don’t worry about her. She’s gone."

Justin stood up from the table and walked upto Leon immediately. "You were right. Ever since I stopped eating that poisoned food, the mana’s flowing properly again in my body. It’s like a miracle. I actually have a shot at becoming my family’s successor now.

Are you here to teach me magic Mr. Leon ?" He asked with an excited expression.

"No," Leon said. "I need a favor."

Justin tilted his head. "A favor?"

Leon’s tone was steady. "How much power do you have over a village chief?"

"You mean villages outside the kingdom walls?"

"Yes."

"Well... I can terminate them from their post. But I can’t kill anyone, if that’s what you’re asking."

"If I wanted him dead," Leon said coolly, "I wouldn’t be here. I want him removed. Write me a termination letter."

Justin considered. "If you want to get back at him for something... how about an AST letter?"

"AST?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"All Services Termination. It strips a noble of their title, their property, their job—everything."

Leon blinked. "You can do that ?"

"For one and two-star nobles, yes—especially if they have prior offenses. And Kael Klen? That guy is begging to lose his status. He nearly lost it when he tried to steal from the state funds about 2 weeks ago. The king exiled him outside the walls as punishment."

Justin smirked. "With an AST letter, no one will question it. He’ll become a commoner and without a job he and his family will be decreased to a slave pretty soon.

Because no one gives jobs to nobles who have turned to commoners."

Leon’s smile turned cold. "Perfect. Write it. I want to throw it in his face before midnight."

"Yep... I will write it immediately." Justin sat down and began writing.

’Let’s see who ends up exiled now. Losing power and status? That’s worse than death motherfuckers. Once she becomes a Commoner, I will make that bitch my slave.’

"Should I also write that he getting kicked out of the village ?" Justin asked.

"Yes... Write that in big words." Leon replied with a smile.

"Okay."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 181: Stayin’ alive
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As always, two guards were seated at the entrance, idly chatting beneath the snowy overhang.

"I still don’t believe it," one of them muttered.

"Hmm? What now?" his partner asked, frowning.

"That Leon tried to rape the chief’s daughter. I mean, come on—he’s smart, the only student from Velthorne Academy from this village. He had a bright future. Why would he throw it all away like that ?" He asked with a serious expression.

"Lust can be dangerous. Or maybe it’s because she’s the chief’s daughter. You know how some people feel about nobles." his partner replied with a neutral expression.

"I think he was framed," the first guard said, his eyes narrowing.

"Framed? Why would the chief do that?"

"I don’t know. But something stinks about this wh—"

*Clip-clop. Clip-clop*

They both went silent as the sound of hooves echoed faintly through the snow-covered road.

"What’s that?" they said in unison, rising from their chairs and peering into the snowy night.

*Clip-clop. Clip-clop*

"It’s getting louder," one guard said. "But where is it coming from?"

"Hey—look!" the other one pointed.

A massive white horse—no, a Snow Walker—was racing toward them at full speed. And on its back there was someone riding. It was none other than Leon.

"That’s Leon! We have to stop him!"

Both guards grabbed their spears and pointed them toward the charging rider.

Leon’s voice rang out with wild laughter, "Get outta my way, motherfuckers ! Hahahaha! Daddy’s here to deliver a message!"

He jerked the reins and tapped the Snow Walker’s side twice.

"Hienggggg...!"

The beast let out a shrill cry and leapt over the guards in a single, majestic bound as it’s legs made way for him in the air, landing clean inside the village.

*Thud!*

The guards turned in shock, watching Leon gallop away.

"What the-!?"

"Good boy," Leon muttered, patting the Snow Walker’s side as it trotted toward Kael’s house.

---

In Front of Kael’s House...

Clip-clop. Clip-clop.

The Snow Walker stopped at Leon’s signal.

Thud.

Leon leapt off and strode to the door, rolling his neck and flexing his arms as if preparing himself for fighting.

*Knock. Knock*

From inside, Kael’s irritated voice echoed, "Who the hell comes at this hour...? Damn villagers, and where’s that useless butler?"

"Well... Not what I was expecting." Leon spoke in a low voice as he gave him arm a rapid jerk.

Click.

The door opened mid-rant. "Can’t even open a fucking doo—"

Before he could’ve completed his sentence,

CRACK!

A heavy punch smashed directly into Kael’s nose.

Thud.

"Owwww—FUCK! My nose! My fucking nose is bleeding! HELP SOMEONE." Kael shrieked, crumpling to the floor, clutching his face.

Leon stepped inside, emotionless. Then—

KICK!

A brutal boot to Kael’s gut pushed him a little backwards.

"Have you used spatial magic on her in order to to shrink it like that ?" Leon asked with a smile as he looked at the scythe.

"No. Runes. A friend engraved them into the handle. They shrink the weapon and until I speak a specific phrase, she can’t come back to her original form." Rees explained.

"I see..." Leon turned back toward the forest. "Let’s move then."

"Ah yes."

Both of them began to tread through snow-dusted underbrush, wind rustling overhead.

"So... any target in mind? Like a specific monster you want to kill first." Rees asked as he looked at Leon.

"Whatever crosses my path, I will try to kill it." Leon replied with a plain face.

He then reached into his pouch and withdrew one of the wooden beads.

"Seal." Leon spoke as he activated the skill.

<Blast Seal>

[-20 MP]

A black circle materialized over the bead. Leon ran his thumb along its surface and suddenly, a series of notifications started popping in front of his face.

[-20 MP]

[-20 MP]

[-20 MP]

...

Layers of seals stacked one upon the other with incredible speed. After a few seconds another notification popped up in front of him:

[Maximum Seals have been applied.]

He pocketed the bead, pulled out the second one, and began repeating the process.

Rees watched silently. He had learned not to interrupt Leon when he was preparing something... because if he asked question right now, Leon’s answer will only make more questions in his mind.

They continued walking until a low, guttural growl broke through the howling wind.

Grrrrr...

"Hm?" Leon stopped. "Did you hear that?"

"I did." Rees pointed to a thick patch of bushes. "It’s coming from there."

"Glitch Point."

Three red dots appeared over the bushes—clustered together.

’The dots are awfully close that means it’s a single target with three weak spots.’ Leon thought in his mind.

Rees pulled his scythe from his back and began casting buffs. Green runes glowed softly along the blade.

<Inspect>

[Name: —]

[Race: Wild Agile Boar]

[Level: 9]

[HP: 900/900]

[MP: 600/600]

[Description: Wild Agile Boars are known for their strength and relentless speed. They prey on human children and weaker flesh, preferring easy kills.]

’It’s hesitating,’ Leon thought. ’Maybe because of Rees’s aura.’

He turned. "Rees, step back."

"What?! Are you sure? We don’t even know what we’re deal—"

"I know." Leon’s tone left no room for argument.

"...Yes." Rees stepped back but kept casting buffs.

Leon crouched, holding three exploding beads in one hand, his thumb ready over one.

"Come on... come out."

Suddenly, the bushes rustled.

Oink-oink-oink!

The boar lunged forward with shocking speed, tusks aimed for Leon’s legs.

"Mr. Leon, it’s a Wild Agile Boa—"

Leon flicked the bead toward it and whispered, "Kaboom."

BOOOOOOOOM.

A deafening explosion shook the earth. Snow scattered. Blood painted the white ground crimson.

Leon looked down.

At his feet, the massive boar lay still—half its head obliterated, the rest twitching in its death throes.

[Congratulations! You have Leveled Up!]

Rees approached, stunned. "W-What was that?!"

Leon smiled, brushing soot from his coat. "You’re not the only one with hidden magic weapons, Rees."

He turned, walking deeper into the forest.

"Let’s keep going."

"W-What kind of magic is this guy even using ? Even I haven’t seen such a spell that can be activated this fast and without any chants as well." Rees murmured in a low voice as he started following Leon.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 192: Being Slick huh?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vinceral Forest, Midnight

The forest was deathly still, cloaked in a heavy darkness that swallowed all light.

Snow blanketed the ground in a cold hush and it is falling down from the sky non-stop. Nothing was visible—but Leon walked forward without hesitation as his skill was activated.

<Adaptive Body>

His breath didn’t fog in the freezing air. His body, unnaturally warm. His vision, sharper than any other humans should be. He was studying his status screen as he walked forward:

---

[Name: Leon Luster]

[Class: Alchemist]

[Race: Human]

[Level: 11]

[Mana: 2300/2300 (+100)]

[HP: 1100/1100]

Skills:

[Skill Crafter (F)], [Inspect (C)], [Adaptive Body (S)], [Minor Healing (F2)], [Trap (F)], [Inventory (F3)], [Blind Spot (F)],

[Touch of Lust (F4)], [Loose Grip (F)], [Adrenaline Rush (F)], [Lustful Thoughts (F)], [Potion Crafter (F {S-Cap})], [Alchemy (F)]

[Glitch Point (F)], [Instant Craft (F1)], [Reverberate (F1)], [Mana Thread (F)], [Life Steal (F)], [Leeching Aura (F)], [Mana Return (F)]

[Efficient Breathing (F)], [Core Bonding (F)], [Dungeon Master (F)], [Silent Steps (F)], [Blast Seal (F)], [Focused Reading (F)], [Data Manager (F)], [Mimic (F)], [Bluff (F)], [Lust Bite (F)], [Pain’s Recovery (F)]

Title: Dungeon Master

____________

’Wow... I made so many skills? And why is my mana double my HP? Wasn’t it the other way around before?’ Leon thought for a moment, then dismissed the screen with a sigh.

’System really works in a mysterious way. Oh well... I just remembered a new skill and I should make it before I forget it again.’ He thought with a smile as he activated the skill.

<Skill Crafter>

[Please pick a name for the skill...]

"Sixth Sense."

[Skill ’Sixth Sense’ has been created.]

[Rank has been settled to ’F’ by default.]

[State the use of skill.]

"A passive skill that remains active all the time and whenever there is danger around the user, this skill will let the user’s mind know where the danger is coming from. Other than that... this skill is completely useless, deals no damage, and doesn’t give any battle advantage to the user," Leon said in a low voice.

"Did you say something, Mr. Leon?" Rees asked with a confused tone as he looked at Leon.

"Huh...? Oh, nothing. I was just talking to myself, I do that sometimes... You don’t have to pay attention to it." Leon replied calmly as he looked at Rees.

"Ah, okay. As you say Mr. Leon." Rees replied.

[Processing...]

’I don’t know about this skill. It’s like I’m shooting arrows in the dark.

From one angle... it’s useless, but in reality, it’s really useful. If I can know where the attack is going to come from, I’ll have an easy time dodging. I should also make an auto-dodge skill and a skill that can keep me alive while I sleep and—’

Before he could finish the thought, another screen popped up in front of his face.

[Description has been accepted.]

[Estimated mana cost: 400]

[Cooldown: 60 minutes after the user intentionally shuts down the skill.]

[Would you like to learn the skill or destroy it?]

’Oh... the system got really specific with the notifications, huh?’ Leon thought for a second before whispering the word.

"Learn it."

[Congratulations! You have learned the skill {Sixth Sense} successfully!]

<Sixth Sense>

As soon as the skill was learned, it activated automatically and a very sharp pain struck Leon’s head.

"Fucking hell..."

*Thud*

Leon dropped to one knee and closed his eyes, grabbing his head with one hand... He placed his other hand on a nearby tree.

"What happened, Mr. Leon? Do you feel cold or tired? Should I take us back to the dungeon?" Rees asked, placing his skeletal hand on Leon’s shoulder.

Suddenly, black-and-white images began flashing through Leon’s mind.

His surroundings became visible, not through sight, but through perception. Everything felt alive. Every fluttering insect, each snowflake brushing his cloak and even the breeze of air—he could feel it all.

"Mr. Leon... can you tell me what happened ? So that I can help you ? Please you are scaring me... If something happened to you, Fruela and Dusk will litreally kill me. Please tell me." Rees asked again in a worried tone.

Leon slowly opened his eyes. The world looked normal again.

"I am alright." Leon replied with a flat expression.

’Was that because of the skill? For that one moment, it felt like I was a part of the forest itself.’ He thought standing up once again.

"S-Should we move forward or go back? I think going back would be a better option since you are-" Before Rees could’ve completed his sentence Leon interrupted him.

"We go forward and don’t worry about me." He said without hesitation.

"Y-Yes."

After walking for about ten minutes, Leon suddenly stopped.

"What is it, Mr. Leon? Do you feel sick again ? Should I-"

"Something’s here..." Leon spoke with a serious expression as he felt it in his brain.

It was as if an alarm went off in his brain... Telling him that something is wrong.

"I see."

Rees pulled out his scythe and began chanting immediately.

"Kekeke..."

A familiar laugh slithered through the darkness and Leon grinned as soon as he recognised the language.

"Goblins..."

He drew Nyxter from his inventory and bound five invisible mana threads to each blade in that very moment.

"Mr. Leon... look, over there on the tree, there is something standing there." Rees said, pointing with his scythe.

Leon looked up and saw a taller, more muscular figure.

<Inspect>

[Name: Unknown]

[Class: HobGoblin]

[Race: Monster]

[Level: 13]

[HP: 5000/5000]

[MP: 300/300]

[Description: A HobGoblin is an evolved form of a normal Goblin. They are taller, stronger, and more intelligent. HobGoblins don’t like to stay in groups; they prefer to rule inferior goblins.

But if found in groups, they are likely working under a superior monster. Who is commanding them to do his bids.]

"A HobGoblin ?" Leon asked with a little bit confused expression.

"Kekekeke.... Kekekeke... Kekekekekekeke..."

"Keekekekek..."

"Keke kekek kekekeke.."

More HobGoblins stepped out of the bushes, wearing leather armor and wielding swords and shields.

"We’re surrounded, Mr. Leon," Rees stated as he stepped back towards Leon and watched the HobGoblins coming towards them slowly.

"I know." Leon spoke with a neutral expression as he counted the number of HobGoblins around him.

’1...2...7, 8 and 9. Total nine of them.’ he thought in his mind.

"I haven’t seen this many HobGoblins together unless they’re working under—"

"A superior monster?" Leon finished.

"Yes. Should I use a light spell to distract them so we can run away?" Rees asked as he was ready with the chant.

"Why would we run away ? So much experience points just walked upto me and we should just run away ?" Leon asked, smiling as he grabbed two blades from Nyxter and let three hover around him.

"W-Wait... you don’t mean to fight them, do you ?" Rees asked in a worried voice as he couldn’t understand why Leon wanted to fight them.

"You’re holding a fucking scythe and you know dozens of spells. Can’t you fight off some HobGoblins ?" Leon asked with a smile as he slowly tightened the grip on his blades and selected the target HobGoblin that was walking right in front of him.

"I-I haven’t really fought anyone before. Neither monster nor human. I’m a scholar, Mr. Leon, not a fighter. But I can cast buff spells if that helps or Teleportation spells too." Rees spoke in a worried tone.

"Then get to casting the buff spells and keep the teleportation spells ready as well." Leon ordered.

Rees began chanting immediately.

"Al frendlle... Shlic Grutosrus." As soon as he stopped the chants messages started popping in front of Leon’s face.

[Undead’s Buff has been activated.]

[HP increased by 5000 (Temporary)]

[Defense increased by 30% (Temporary)]

[Speed increased by 50% (Temporary)]

[Stealth increased by 60%(Temporary)]

"Hehehe... this is going to be even more fun now," Leon grinned as a black fog swirled around him.

"You’re dead, motherfucker. Loose Grip..."

<Loose Grip>

The HobGoblin that was walking in front of Leon suddenly felt his sword and shield slip from his grip.

"Kekekeke...?"

*Thud-Thud*

They hit the ground and the HobGoblin’s eyes widened immediately but Instead of retrieving them, it leapt backward.

The HobGoblin understood that if he bent down, Leon will kill him immediately and that’s why he jumped two steps back.

*Jump-Jump*

"Kek—"

*Stab*

But in the middle of the second jump, a dark blade pierced out from it’s chest. As it looked back in horror, the HobGoblin saw Leon’s smiling face.

"Trying to be slick, huh, motherfucker ?" Leon asked with a smile as he slowly twisted the blade.

*Chck*

"Keke-" Before the Goblin could’ve done anything, Leon brought another blade down, decapitating the HobGoblin in one blow.

*Thud*

Its head hit the snow. The others stepped back, visibly shocked from what happened to their partner.

"What happened? Why are you stepping back? Don’t tell me you’re scared already, if you don’t kill me... What are you going to eat tonight.

I am really tasty you know." Leon said, yanking his blade free. The HobGoblin’s body crumpling at his feet.

*Thud*

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 193: Brothers Kill
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As soon as Leon killed the first HobGoblin, he turned sharply to the left, his eyes locking onto another target stalking the treeline.

"Well... here goes nothing," he muttered with a smirk, confidence radiating from every inch of his posture. He stepped forward with calm precision, the snow crunching beneath his feet.

The HobGoblin ahead twitched nervously, it’s eyes wide. It planted its feet and raised its weapon, preparing to lunge the moment Leon came within reach.

As Leon walked forward... He noticed that the Hobgoblin’s sword was twitching in his hand.

’Ah... He is about to use his sword as soon as I get in his attacking range. Well... This should be fun.’ Leon thought with a smile as he activated his skill.

"Blind Spot,"

In the blink of an eye, his form disappeared from the HobGoblin’s vision.

"Kekek...?!" The creature spun on its heels, swinging its shield in a wide arc, expecting an ambush from behind as he has seen with his friend.

But it was already too late,

*Stab*

Nyxter’s blade erupted cleanly through its chest and as soon as he felt his heart getting punctured by it... His eyes widened in fear.

*Chop*

Before he could’ve turned back... Another dagger decapitated him clean in one blow.

*Thud*

Leon stood behind him, calm and composed, tightening his grip on the Nyxter’s handle. With a single, efficient motion, he twisted the blade and yanked it free from it’s body.

Blood sprayed into the cold night air as the HobGoblin’s lifeless body collapsed to the snow.

Leon shook both blades, flicking crimson droplets to the ground. "You know what...? I should try something else," he said casually.

He released the two blades that he was holding in his hands and all five blades floated above him, spinning silently in formation.

The remaining HobGoblins exchanged hesitant glances. Their discipline cracked as fear slithered into their ranks. Seven of them remained, but none dared stepped forward.

As they were being confused wether to fight or run, one of them roared loudly using his skill.

[Rage Buff]

[All Ally attack damage has been increased by 30%.]

"Keeeekekekekek...!"

It charged forward, his shield raised high in order to protect from any straight forward attacks.

"Now you’re charging head-on ? Bold move, but you see... Even after being so intelligent, you still have the mind of a goblin when in fear.

You think your partners will charge alongside with you but they aren’t." Leon said with a chuckle as he raised his hand, palm open.

Swish—Swish—Swish—Swish—Swish!

The blades shot forward with incredible speed. One skewered the HobGoblin’s foot, pinning it to the forest ground. Another struck its shoulder. Two more pierced its chest and skull. The final one shattered its kneecap.

"Kekeke.!!!! Kekekekekekeke !!!!! Ke-" He shouted in pain and misery as if calling his partners to help but none of them dared to step forward.

Stab-Stab-Stab-Stab-Stab!

Thud.

[Congratulations! You have leveled up!]

The monster died before it could even complete his last scream. Its blood steamed in the snow.

Leon tilted his head, inspecting the result with analytical calm. "Still imprecise... I wanted only the foot pinned, rest of the blades were aimed to strike at the chest. They’re responsive, but I need better control.

I am just relieved that all of them hitted the same target."

Rees’s hollow voice cut in. "Mr. Leon... I believe they’re retreating."

Leon looked up. The HobGoblins were inching back into the shadows.

’Knowledge is really a curse huh ? When they were goblins, I am pretty sure they would’ve charged at me at the same time because they were dumb.

But after becoming intelligent... All they have learned is how to save themselves first. Some creatures are better left dumb. But that’s just to my advantage.’ Leon thought for a second as he looked at the HobGoblins.

"They are not retreating Rees... They are running. I wanted to kill all of them but looks like I can only kill one more." he said, disappointed.

With a snap of his fingers, the floating blades converged toward another target.

*Swish—Swish—Swish—Swish—Swish!*

The running HobGoblin lifted its shield—but it couldn’t block everything. Leon struck from every open angle and the HobGoblin’s shield couldn’t block them all.

*Stab-Stab-Stab-Stab-Stab!*

Another one dropped down, it’s blood spilling over the ground.

*Thud

Seeing another comrade butchered with surgical ease, the remaining Hobgoblins finally panicked and fled into the dense forest.

Leon recalled the blades, spun them once midair to shake off the blood, then reassembled them into Nyxter and slid the weapon back into its sheath.

"Let’s go forward," he said flatly.

Rees hesitated. "Mr. Leon... the superior monster leading these HobGoblins will be deeper inside. If we keep up with this path, a confrontation is very likely to happen."

Leon paused. ’Those HobGoblins were too easy... but what if the next enemy isn’t?’ he thought with a serious expression.

Rees pointed off-trail. "If we head this way, we can circle the forest’s perimeter. We’ll avoid the deeper zones—for now. What do you say ?"

Leon considered it.. ’He’s right. I shouldn’t underestimate whatever’s ahead. Even dusk says that the deeper zones of this forest aren’t very safe and I won’t let my arrogance fuck me up... let’s just stay out for now.’

"Fine," he said, turning to the right.

"A truly wise decision, Mr. Leon." Rees replied in a happy tone.

As he walked, he glanced down at his bloodstained hand. The sticky crimson blood reminded him of how difficult every battle truly was when he fought the hoblins. He wiped it off on a leaf and moved forward.

...

Meanwhile, in the Weaver mansion’s grand library, tension strangled the air. Justin and Althea sat in heavy silence across the table.

"This... this isn’t good," Althea muttered as she stood up and started walking around the room.

"What happened today will destroy our reputation. Your father entrusted this house to me while he’s gone—and this... this disgrace. He’ll never forgive me." she spoke with a worried expression as she sat down on the couch.

’Hmm... She is right. The reputation of the house will certainly go down if this news made it out.’ Justin thought but didn’t give it much time.

She stood up, pacing once again. "Should I kill all the servants? Silence them? Or... do I kill that shameless whore Emilia instead? What do I do, Justin? Help me you are the smartest kid I have."

Justin’s expression remained unreadable. ’You’re asking me for a solution? You, who ignored me all my life ? What a fucking joke.’

But outwardly, he simply nodded. ’I should say what Mr. Leon told me to say.’

"Mother, I’ve already sent word to Brother Jamie. He’s returning home." Justin spoke without any hesitation as he looked at Althea.

"You WHAT?!" Althea spun toward him, stunned.

"I said—"

*SLAP!*

Justin’s head jerked to the side as Althea slapped him really hard.

"Ouch... Fuck," he cursed, straightening up and looking at Althea with a confused expression.

"You fool! You think he’ll listen to any of us ? Don’t you remember what he did when you stole his toy as a child?!" Althea asked with an angry expression.

Justin’s eyes narrowed. Cold fury boiled to the surface immediately.

"I remember what he did... As clear as day. He stabbed me—with a fucking dagger. Through my fucking hand, Mother." He spoke with a serious expression.

Althea’s face froze.

"And you?" Justin said, his voice low. "You did nothing. You never even checked on me. Just kept pampering him and saving him from father.

Isn’t that what you did, Mother ?"

Althea tried to pull away, but Justin caught her wrist in a vice-like grip.

"Let go! You’re hurting me! Justin I said let go... I am your my mother." Althea shouted as she looked at him.

"I also remember when he killed a maid ten years ago. You helped him cover it up. Burned her body, slaughtered her family. I wonder who gave that order, Mother ?

I hope father forgave you for that incident." Justin spoke with a serious expression.

Althea’s face turned pale.

"You... How do you—?"

"I saw her burn. She was the only one who treated me like a son. Her ashes are still in my room, Mother."

Justin’s tone dropped, deadly calm. "They stay there until I take this house from father. And now... I have the perfect opportunity to become the successor since my dear brother is coming home."

"You... you did this on purpose?" Althea asked, horrified.

"What?" Justin tilted his head.

"Emilia and Jim. You planned it—to make your brothers kill each other! Didn’t you ?" She asked.

Justin smirked. "I didn’t plan it. But I’ll use it the opportunity to it’s fullest."

"You sick bastard. I’ll see to it you never become head of this house!"

Althea stormed out, her footsteps echoing in the marble hall.

Justin sank onto the couch, breathing hard. "Fuck... that took everything out of me. Why the hell did Mr. Leon want me to say all that? What’s his plan?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 194: Goblin Champion
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Vinceral Forest, northern outer edge.

Leon and Rees pressed forward in the snow-laced gloom, their boots crunching over frozen underbrush. Despite their alerted senses, the forest lay still—too still.

No monsters were in sight. Only the occasional sigh of wind whispered through the trees.

*Swish-Swish*

"Where did all the monsters go? It’s dead here." Leon muttered, his tone edged with confusion.

"The cold is forcing them into hiding, Mr. Leon." Rees replied.

"What ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Only a few monsters can deal with such a cold weather and even they only come out in the day.

We should return, Mr. Leon it’s worthless to waste anymore time in this place. It’s nearly midnight. I doubt we’ll find anything still roaming." Rees explained as he halted and looked at Leon.

Leon nodded slightly, his gaze scanning the treeline. "Alright... but we’ll walk back to the dungeon."

Rees tilted his skull slightly. "Why walk, when I can—"

"I feel like walking," Leon cut in with a faint smile. "Maybe we’ll find some of those HobGoblins on the way back. I just want to kill one more and then we will return tomorrow morning."

’If I killed one more HobGoblin... I will reach level thirteen and that would be really good.’ Leon thought with a neutral expression.

"As you wish," Rees said, and together they turned back, retracing their path through the frozen woods.

After a moment of silence, Rees spoke again. "Umm... Mr. Leon, may I ask you a question ?"

Leon glanced over with a confused expression, "What kind of question ?"

"Well... when I get bored, I tend to tell riddles. Would you mind if I gave you one? You don’t have to answer if—"

"Go on, ask it... I am also getting bored." Leon said with an amused smirk, cutting him off mid-sentence.

"Ah, very well... I was expecting a negative response, since most of the humans don’t really like riddles-"

"Just ask, Rees." Leon spoke cutting him off once again.

"Yes, The more you take, the more I grow. Invisible trails that always show. I mark your path, but never stay—What am I, that walks away?" Rees asked in a rhyming tone.

"Footsteps, the more I take the more they grow, invisible trails because they are my trails.... They mark my path but disappear after sometime." Leon replied almost immediately as a smile appeared over his face.

"Y-You got that way too fast... Even I couldn’t crack it that fast or—" Rees started speaking but he was interrupted in the middle of his sentence.

"I’m good with riddles, Rees. You choose wrong opponent to pass time with. If you want amusement from me... You will have to take another topic and you were looking at my footsteps when you asked the question." Leon said, smirking.

"I see, Then let’s see how you handle this Mr. Leon.

Born of silence, yet I break it. Seen by none, yet all have met it. I flee the light, embrace the deep, and cradle those who fall asleep. What am I ?"

Leon’s expression darkened. "Danger."

"Incorrect," Rees said. "The correct answer will be—"

But Leon held up a hand. "Something’s coming, Rees. And it’s not just any monster. Get ready."

"Wha-!?"

Suddenly, the forest shook with a deafening roar.

"GRAAAAAAAAAHHHHH!"

Rees’s tone sharpened. "Mr. Leon—above you!"

Leon’s gaze snapped skyward immediately. A massive shadow hurtled toward them, descending down with terrifying speed.

Without wasting another second, Leon jumped back twice.

Jump-Jump!

THUD!

The impact was cataclysmic. Snow exploded outward as the creature landed, leaving a small crater beneath its bulk.

Leon’s eyes narrowed as he spoke in a low voice, "Holy fucking shit..."

Before them stood a hulking figure—nearly nine feet tall, its skin a dark green. Veins bulged beneath its thick, muscular frame. In both hands, it gripped twin axes, each glinting with dried blood.

There was a necklace of HobGoblin heads in it’s neck and golden bracelets in both hands.

Leon wasted no time and used his skill on him.

<Inspect>

[Name: Gruder]

[Class: Goblin Champion]

[Race: Named Monster]

[Level: 20]

[HP: 20,000/20,000]

[MP: 50/50]

[Description: Gruder was named by a mage—after he slaughtered him. Known for his intelligence, strategic brutality, and overwhelming bloodlust, he fights with relentless rage when challenged by a worthy foe or when he is extremely hungry.]

"M-Mr. Leon... that’s a Goblin Champion," Rees warned. "He’s the one commanding the HobGoblins that we met earlier. And I think now he’s come to finish the job himself because I can see their heads hanging in his necklace."

Leon’s expression turned even more serious as he activated another skill.

<Glitch Point>

But to his disappointment, there were no weak points over the Goblin Champion’s body.

’Dammit. How does he not have any weak points ? I should’ve brought Dusk or Fruela.’ Leon thought with a serious expression.

Then the monster spoke.

"Which... of you... stronger?"

Leon blinked with an utterly confused expression.

’It can talk ?’ Leon thought as he tightened his grip on the Nyxter’s blades.

"Mr. Leon! I’ve prepared the escape spell. Please—we can still run! Just run over to me." Rees shouted from the other side, a glowing sigil forming under his bony feet.

’He is right... We should return back to the dungeon. Fighting this monster is going to be a very very risky thing.’ Leon thought in his mind.

"If it can speak our language, Rees... it can probably understand it too. Don’t you think, Rees ?" Leon asked with a neutral expression.

Gruder grinned, revealing sharp yellow teeth as he looked at Leon. "No waste time. I eat... you. Food."

’His speech is broken... limited vocabulary, that means he has hunted less but more than enough humans to learn the language.’ Leon assessed for a moment but dismissed the thought immediately.

"Sorry, motherfucker. I’m not on the menu tonight." Leon whispered in a low voice.

<Blind Spot>

Leon vanished from sight and bolted toward Rees—but he’d barely covered half the distance when something whistled through the air.

THUD!

An axe buried itself in the ground just inches away from Leon’s feet. He skidded to a stop, his eyes wide in shock. Across the clearing, Gruder’s gaze burned with crimson red.

’He can see through Blind Spot...!’ Leon thought with a serious expression as he looked at him.

"Mr. Leo—" Rees began but,

The second axe was already airborne.

"REEES! MOVE!" Leon shouted with all his might.

Rees tried to move but it was too late...

CRACKLE—CRACK!

The blade sheared through his lower torso, scattering the bones across the snow.

"Fucking hell." Leon spoke as his expressions turned grim.

But Rees’s bones quivered, reassembling almost immediately. "I—I’m alright. I am alright Mr. Leon."

"Motherfucker... I thought he died." Leon spoke in a low voice as he turned to look at Gruder.

"DIE, FOOD! DIE!" Gruder roared in a very deep voice, as he charged towards him with terrifying speed, both of his fists raised in the air and over his head.

Leon immediately jumped back—

THUD-THUD!

—barely dodging the double slam.

Then again, Gruder thundered forward.

"GRRRRAAAHHHHHHHHHHHHH..."

Same form. Same angle. Same wild fists.

Leon stepped back again, narrowly avoiding the next strike as well but this time... Leon gained some distance between them.

This time, Gruder grabbed an axe and re-aimed it towards Leon.

<Loose Grip>

[Opponent Level Too High. Skill will not function correctly.]

As soon as Leon readed the warning, it was already too late.

The axe spun like a comet toward him.

SWISH—

"Fuck..."

She waved it off casually. "It’s a long story, will tell you later. And it’s getting really cold out here. Where’s that place you were talking about ?"

Unseen, Fruela followed them from a distance, completely concealed in the falling snow.

...

After walking for about ten minutes, three of them finally reached the wagon station.

Despite the heavy snowfall, a few wagons were still making their rounds through the rough weather. Leon and Emilia climbed into one, while Fruela silently perched atop the wagon roof.

"Vernon Village," Leon told the driver.

Leon nodded with a neutral expression . "Okay, I will pay it, since you have already bargained so much from both the sides by yourself."

The driver cracked the reins, and the horses slowly began trudging through the snow-covered path.

"You live in Vernon Village? Isn’t that—" Before Emilia could finish, Leon pulled a handkerchief from his coat and held it out to her.

Emilia blinked. "Why ?"

"I have trust issues." Leon replied immediately.

"...Okay." Though puzzled, she didn’t argue. She took the handkerchief and tied it around her eyes.

Thirty minutes later, the wagon pulled up to the north edge of Vinceral Forest.

"We’ll get off here," Leon said as he looked at the wagon driver.

The driver stared at him, baffled. "Here ? In the middle of nowhere ?"

"You ask too many questions. Here is your money." Leon dropped six copper coins into the man’s hand and hopped off the wagon.

The driver stared at the coins. "Wait, aren’t they only two people? Why six coppers...? Maybe he saw how hard I was working, What a generous guy," he muttered as he turned the wagon around.

Fruela silently leapt down from the wagon’s top and rejoined them without a sound.

...

"Are we... inside Vinceral Forest?" Emilia asked softly, still blindfolded. "I can smell some familiar herbs."

"Yes," Leon replied calmly as the trio continued through the snow-covered woods.

After a few minutes, they arrived at the base of a massive oak tree.

Leon walked up to where the dungeon gate was hidden beneath the snow. He lifted his leg and drove his foot downward with full force, expecting to hit the familiar stone staircase. But what he struck wasn’t stone—it was metal.

*Thud!*

"FUCK! my leg. What the hell ?" Leon muttered, quickly pulling his foot back. A metallic clink echoed from beneath the snow.

"Is everything alright?" Emilia asked, her voice laced with concern.

"Yeah... everything’s fine." Leon crouched and brushed aside the snow. His eyes narrowed at what he saw—metal. Two reinforced gates now covered the dungeon’s entrance.

*Grrrrrrrrrrrrr...*

Suddenly, the metallic gates groaned and began to open on their own, pushing the snow aside with mechanical ease.

Leon stared in stunned silence as the entrance widened before him.

"What in the actual hell...?" he whispered, eyes locked on the now-transformed dungeon entrance. It was no longer the same passage he had left just hours ago—it had doubled in size and was fortified like never before.

Fruela guided Emilia to the dungeon’s entrance and all three of them entered inside the dungeon.

*GRRRRRRRRRR*

As soon as they were inside, The gates got closed on their own.

*Boop-Boop-Boop-Boop*

Torches mounted on the walls lit up on their own.

The hundreth recast will continue until the skill is stopped manually and mana consumption will go on."

But, I really don’t know why your hand stopped moving, Master. I-I am very very sorry master. Please forgive me." Elis apologized with a worried expression as she immediately went down on both of her knees and did a very respectful bow.

"Because mana flows in everyone’s blood... If you blocked my mana that means you blocked my nerves as well and if blood doesn’t get to my fingers... I won’t be able to use them." Leon replied with a smile as an idea came to his mind.

’That’s a very useful skill... I can use it in hand-to-hand combat against living beings. If I activate it with glitch point... I will be able to locate the acupuncture points of my enemy easily.’ Leon thought as he looked at his hand.

The punch hitted her and she immediately fell down to the ground.

’Ah... That’s a nice skill. I will make that as well, it will help me figure out the fighting patterns of an enemy in hand-to-hand combat.’ Leon thought with a neutral expression.

He then walked upto Elis once again, made his fist into a punch and aimed for her stomach, but this time before he could’ve touched her... Elis turned exactly ninety degrees to the left side and dodged the move.

Not only did she dodged the punch, but raised her hand, made it into a fist and tried to land a punch in Leon’s back.

<Sixth Sense>

But before the punch could’ve landed, Leon felt it coming because of the passive skill that got activated automatically.

*Roll*

Leon immediately rolled down to the other side and dodged the attack.

’Okay... She learned, dodged and countered. It’s like I am training a robot to fight and the more she learns... The more it will help me get better in combat.’ Leon thought as he removed his coat and dropped it down on the floor.

He then folded his sleeves and removed his boots.

"Well... Let’s get serious, Elis." Leon spoke with a smile as he rolled his sleeves.

"As you command, Master." Elis spoke as she changed her stance this time.

Elis turned to the left side once again dodging the attack once again, but Leon immediately opened his hand and grabbed her leg.

He turned around and made her fall onto the ground.

*Thud*

Elis immediately stood up once again and took a different stance this time.

Leon ran towards her threw the punch once again, Elis turned to the side but this time as soon as he grabbed her leg... Elis grabbed Leon’s hand and pressed it down with full force, breaking the grip at once.

*Jump*

Leon immediately jumped back and looked at Elis with a smile.

’That was an impressive way to break my grip over her leg.’ Leon thought as he started running towards her once again, but this time instead of punching... He raised his leg in order to land a kick on her left shoulder.

*Thud*

But, Elis immediately raised her hand in a ’V’ shape and soaked most of his kick’s force with her wrist.

*Grab*

But she didn’t stop there... using her right hand, she immediately grabbed Leon’s leg and spinned his whole body mid-air.

Chapter 220: Killian: The Bow
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Dusk thundered across the snow-covered plains, his paws kicking up white dust as he galloped toward the southern border of the Human Kingdom. Atop his back sat Leon, his expression calm and unreadable, his eyes lost in quiet thought.

Though the sky was growing brighter, a curtain of dark clouds still loomed over the twin suns, casting a dim shadow over the snowy expanse. The snowfall had ceased, but the world remained cloaked in cold silence.

Leon slowly lifted his hand and balanced a thin wooden stick upon his palm. Releasing his grip ever so slightly, he allowed the stick to begin falling southward—but before it could tumble completely, he caught it again.

’Still south.’ Leon thought for a second before opening the map.

’A forest in front of a white-dotted lake... But why is the forest drawn upside-down? And what are these white dots in the lake?’ Leon narrowed his eyes in confusion, studying the strange map markings with growing curiosity.

Screech—!

"What the-!?"

Dusk came to an abrupt halt, jerking Leon from his concentration.

Sniff-Sniff

"Master... Blood. A very strong stench—it’s coming from that direction," Dusk said, his voice grim as his gaze fixed on a distant wooden gate.

Etched in faded, weatherworn letters above the gate were the words:

"Truvale Checkpost."

Leon read the sign aloud with a flat tone as he tucked the map back into his inventory and slid down from Dusk’s back.

"I can feel it, Master... death. Thick and heavy in the air. Thousands have been killed here," Dusk muttered, his fur bristling as if gripped by invisible dread.

"I heard the rumors... A monster wave tore through this place days ago," Leon replied, his tone still neutral as he stepped toward the gate.

The moment he crossed the threshold, the foul stench hit him like a hammer.

*Sniff*

"Fucking hell... What is wrong with this smell?" Leon coughed, yanking out his handkerchief and pressing it against his nose.

"The cold’s preserving the bodies. That’s why the stench hasn’t spread further. Had this happened in another season, the entire kingdom would be choking on it," Dusk explained, his voice low.

As they entered deeper into the checkpost, rows of bodies came into view—lined up, covered in white cloth, stretching across both sides of the ruined outpost.

’This is a massacre... Just like Justin said, they lied about the monsters. Not a single monster corpse is in sight,’ Leon thought, walking toward one of the shrouded bodies. With a slow hand, he lifted the frozen cloth.

"Fucking hell..." he muttered, his eyes shutting briefly at the sight before him.

The corpse was butchered. Her stomach torn open, heart ripped out, limbs mangled, and her lower jaw crushed beyond recognition. She had once been a woman—now, she was nothing more than a horror frozen in time.

Leon gently pulled the cloth back over her, covering the grisly sight.

"What the hell could do something like this?" he asked, his tone tight as he turned to Dusk.

Sniff-Sniff

"I don’t know, Master... But I still smell fresh blood. It’s coming from over there," Dusk said, already padding toward the other side of the outpost.

Leon followed—and froze.

An elven corpse lay sprawled in the snow, a gleaming golden bow clutched in his hands. The sight triggered a flicker of recognition in Leon’s eyes.

"This guy..." Leon stepped forward, his eyes narrowed.

"You know him, Master?" Dusk asked.

"The third son of Freya Virellium... Serandor Virellium, if I remember correctly," Leon said, inspecting the mutilated corpse.

"The elf prince? The one you fought at Eagle’s Eye Checkpost?" Dusk’s tone was tinged with disbelief.

"Yes." Leon’s voice grew tense.

’What the hell could do this to an elf prince? And what was he even doing here?’ Leon wondered, his eyes scanning Serandor’s ravaged body.

"Master... more elf corpses," Dusk called from farther ahead.

Leon strode over and stopped cold.

Hundreds of elven soldiers lay dead, their bodies strewn in chaotic patterns. Blood stained the snow beneath them.

"We should leave, Master. Staying here isn’t safe," Dusk warned.

"I can’t agree more with you, Dusk." Leon spoke with a serious expression. "Just give me a second."

He ran back to Serandor’s body and knelt beside him, eyeing the exquisite bow.

"This won’t be of any use to you anymore," Leon said as he gripped the bow. But Serandor’s fingers were frozen tight around the string.

"Let go of the bow... motherfucker," Leon muttered, yanking with full strength.

Snap—!

The taut string sliced clean through the elf’s frozen hand as Leon pried the bow free.

The moment he did, the runes and embedded gems across the bow shimmered to life—glowing with ethereal light.

[Aren’t you ashamed of yourself? Stealing from a corpse?]

A voice echoed in Leon’s mind, stern and disdainful.

"Hmm...?" Leon turned, scanning his surroundings.

[What are you looking around for, stupid kid? Not like you can hear me anyway. Why am I even talking to you.

Just don’t sell me to some cheap—]

"Is that you talking?" Leon interrupted, staring at the bow.

[W-What?! You can hear me?!]

"I can."

[But... that’s impossible. No one has ever been able to hear me.]

"I’m hearing you and wondering, why can a bow speak ?"

[I am no ordinary bow, kid. I am a Divine Artifact. My name is Killian, the Fourth High Judgement.]

"That’s a long name," Leon smirked, and activated his skill.

<Inspect>

[Name: Killian]

[Type: Divine Artifact]

[Rank: SS]

[Title: {The Fourth High Judgement}]

[Description: Killian is one of the seven divine artifacts scattered across the world after the death of Goddess Ikola. Only those with pure and courageous hearts can wield the full power of Killian.]

[W-What?! Trade me?! For food?! I am a Divine Artifact! You should be groveling to please me! Try to amuse me and I might let you use me for—]

Leon cut the bow off mid-sentence—by stuffing it straight into his inventory.

[Description: Medina is the Fairy Mother of all the fairies and the caretaker of the Tree of Life. She knows many magical arts and she is very kind but after getting betrayed by her brother five years ago. She is now paying a serious price for her blind trust.]

"I am Medina... The Fairy Mother. Leon Luster." She spoke.
