Chapter 234: The Chief’s Prayer
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"How do you know my name ?" Leon asked with a confused expression as he looked at Medina.

"I can also tell that you are not from this... *Cough-Cough*..." Before she could’ve completed her sentence, blood started coming out of her mouth.

"You don’t look very good... This parasite really fucked you up, huh ?" Leon asked with a neutral expression.

"You are not from this world, Leon Luster." Medina spoke as she looked at Leon with a serious expression.

As soon as Leon heard this, his eyes widened in shock and surprise.

’How does she know ?’ He thought with a very confused expression.

"Don’t think too much about it... I, myself am a Demi-God. I can tell that your *Cough-Cough* soul doesn’t belong to this world." Medina spoke.

"You were the one in my dream that night... I remember now. It was your face." Leon spoke with a serious expression as he looked at her.

"I was the one who brought you here, Leon. I was the one who sent that steelweb spider your way and gave you that armour skill.

I was the one *Cough-Cough* who showed you the way to my brother’s village." Medina spoke but the more she spoke, more blood came out of her mouth.

"Why ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Because you are the only one who can save me and the tree of Life. If this tree dies... This forest will die, me and my children will die and these demonic vines will become too strong to stop.

I beg you, Please save this tree... Please save my children, Leon Luster and I will pay you an equal *Cough-Cough* price." Medina spoke as she looked at Leon.

"How do I save you ?" Leon asked with a curious expression.

"Five years ago... Orion was one of us. He was happy and very brave, but on a very unfortunate day, his wife ’Olin’ was captured by some humans and they killed her after using her body.

When Orion saw her body, he broke down completely and transformed into a ’Mungel Fairy’.

We tried to comfort him and for a moment we thought he was ready to turn back into a normal fairy but my faith was broken.

Orion s-stole the ’Soul Stones’ of h-his sisters and turned them into cursed fairies using this Demonic Parasite.

This parasite sucks life f-force out of their bodies during the day and uses t-them at night to hunt for food. Without my children... I don’t have any power and the last bit of power that I was saving was used in bringing you here." Medina explained as her voice started breaking and more tears started coming out of her eyes.

"What’s a soul stone ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"A soul stone is the life essence of a fairy... It holds all of their magical powers. Orion holds all of their soul stones right now and you have to get them back from him.

If you can get the soul stones from him, I will able to bring back one of my children back to her senses and she will handle the rest.

Please get those stones, Leon." Medina explained.

"Say... These soul stones, is there anyway that they can be used to stop the cursed fairies from attacking the guy who is holding them ?" Leon asked with a curious expression.

"Yes, Yes... They will never attack the one who holds all the soul stones." Medina replied.

"Why ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"They are cursed and their bodies are being controlled by this Demonic Parasite, but as soon as they get near the soul stones, their consciousness starts to take over their bodies and it diminishes the effect of the Parasite.

That’s why the parasite immediately makes them step back." Medina explained.

"Ah... So, that’s why. Then I know where he keeps those soul stones." Leon replied with a neutral expression.

"You do ? Really ?" Medina asked with a serious expression.

"Yeah, he keeps them in a bowl and he was using them to- Wait... Your brother came here last night, right ?" He asked with a confused expression.

"I don’t know *Cough-Cough* I didn’t see him, but he comes in one or two weeks to make sure I am dying.

Sun is going down... Parasite will release them for hunt soon, you have to get back to the village and get the soul stones.

Leon, I never thought that I will fell down to such a disgraceful sight and ask a human for help, but you are different.

You don’t fear death as much as the other humans. Please help me, Leon and you will not be disappointed in the rewards." Medina replied as she looked at Leon with a serious expression.

"I hope so too." Leon replied with a neutral expression as he started walking towards dusk, who was constantly shaking his legs.

"Master... Should we leave ? I-I am not very comfortable here." Dusk spoke with a worried expression as he continued to shake his body.

"Yes. Let’s go back to the village." Leon replied as he immediately jumped on his back.

*Hop*

Dusk immediately started running at full speed.

’If all the fairies have soul stones that means Orion also have one of them. If I can get a hold of his soul stone... I will be able to get the other stones from him easily.

Fuck, I should’ve asked her where the fairies keep their soul stones.’ Leon thought with a serious expression.

...

After running for about 30 minutes, both of them reached the village as a cold blanket of darkness covered the sky.

"Master... We are here." Dusk replied as he stopped behind some bushes near the village.

Leon immediately jumped down from his back and Dusk transformed back into his cat form.

"No snow tonight, huh ?" Leon asked as he looked up and noticed that the sky was cloudy but there were no signs of snowfall.

Leon and Dusk slowly sneaked inside the village from the backside and noticed that all the villagers are gathering firewoods to make a campfire.

Dusk and Leon stood behind a house and noticed that Orion was going into his house with a smile on his face.

’I have to find out what this motherfucker does in these three hours. I am pretty sure he is not doing any kind of prayer in there.’ Leon thought with a serious expression.

"Where the hell are you doing ?" Suddenly, Mia’s voice came from behind.

Leon immediately turned around and noticed that she was standing behind him with a bunch of firewood in her hands.

"What are you doing here ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"I just asked you that question." Mia replied with a neutral expression.

"Yeah, I don’t have time for this. Tell me... Do you an extra key to the chief’s house ?" Leon asked with a serious expression.

"What ? No. Why would you want a key to-"

"Keep your voice down first of all." Leon spoke as he placed his hand over her mouth.

"Fine... But why do you want to get inside the chief’s house ?" Mia asked with a confused expression.

"Look, that guy is not a human. He is a fairy and that’s all I can tell you for now.

I need to get inside his house right now, so can you help me ?" Leon asked with a serious expression.

"A-Are you crazy ? You are saying that chief is a fairy ? How can chief be a fairy... Those things are monsters and chief is-"

"Chief is the one who turned them into monsters, Mia. You have to believe me or you will never be able to get out of this village." Leon replied with a serious expression.

"O-Okay... Fine, Fine... I will believe you. Just don’t make me regret this decision. There is a secret door in his house.

My father built that house and the chief asked him to keep that door a secret. When I was little... I used it to hide from the other kids while playing.

You can used it to get inside the house." Mia replied with a serious expression.

"Where is it ?" Leon asked with a serious expression.

"It’s in the back of the house. You have to look down at the bottom left corner and you will notice a small gap between the wooden logs there." Mia explained.

"Okay, Thanks." Leon spoke as he immediately started walking towards the chief’s house.

"Wait... Come back." Mia spoke as she grabbed his hand and pulled him back.

"What ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"I want to sleep with you tonight. I heard what you did to Lea... I want to feel it too." Mia spoke with an embarrassed expression.

"Fine, Fine... You will sleep with me tonight. Now let go of my hand. I don’t have time for this." Leon replied with a serious expression.

"Okay." Mia spoke as she let go of Leon’s hand.

Without wasting another second, Leon sneaked upto Orion’s house and after looking around for a while, he finally found the secret door on the left bottom corner but it was very small.

So small that only a cat or dog can pass through it.

Chapter 235: I also have some tricks
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Leon is now looking at the small door behind Orion’s house with a confused expression.

’Why would he make such a small gate ? Even for him, this is too small to run from.’ He thought with a confused expression.

"Master... Shall I go inside and take a look ?" Dusk asked as he looked at Leon with a neutral expression.

"Go inside and remove the curtain from that window a little. I want to see him with my own eyes and use invisibility to hide yourself." Leon ordered.

"As you command, Master." Dusk replied as he immediately turned invisible.

Leon slowly placed his hand over the gate and pushed it in slowly.

As soon as the gate opened up a little bit, Dusk squeezed his way in and Leon slowly walked upto the window that was on the right side of the chief’s house.

This spot was dangerous as all the villagers can easily spot him but the due to the darkness, he was barely visible to them.

Leon slowly looked inside the window and noticed that curtains were still there.

’What the hell is Dusk-’ Before he could’ve completed the thought, the curtain moved slowly and a bright light came from inside.

"What the-!?" Leon immediately closed his eyes as the light was too bright.

As the light dimmed down, Leon opened his eyes and noticed that Orion is sitting on the floor his knees bent and the same bowl that we was carrying yesterday was in his hands.

He was in his original fairy form and the bright light was coming from inside the bowl.

"Mllkiaoo Oliyui Pouyen, Iiwoy Se Risoua." Orion was chanting some kind of spells in ancient language as he looked at the bowl.

’What the hell is that spell-’ Before Leon could’ve thought of anything else, seven brightly glowing stones started flying out of the bowl.

All the stones were glowing brightly white and they had a majestic Aura around them.

’Soul Stones.’ Leon thought with a serious expression as he continued to observe.

"So, beautiful..." Orion spoke as he placed the bowl down and stood up to admire their beauty.

’These soul stones... They have some kind of attraction force. I can feel it and for some unknown reason... I want them.’ Leon thought with a serious expression.

"...Become mine." Orion spoke as he slowly raised his hands towards the soul stones but as soon as he tried to touch them, he felt a very unpleasant burning sensation.

*SsssssS*

"Shit..." Orion spoke as he immediately withdrew his hand and noticed that it was burnt really bad.

"You don’t want to come to me huh ? Don’t worry, in the next three days... Your hosts will die and then I will forcefully take you.

You will become mine and then, I will become the strongest fairy in this world. I will destroy all the humans and turn them into my fear producing toys." Orion spoke as a menacing smile appeared over his face.

<Inspect>

[Name: Orion Ikol]

[Class: Maze Master]

[Race: Mungel Fairy]

[Level: 40]

[HP: 80000/80000]

[MP: 1500/100000]

[Description: Orion is a Mungel fairy, one of the evil fairies that exist inside the forest and feed on other’s fear and death...]

"Please..." He spoke as he grabbed the door and started pushing his body in forcefully.

While Orion was busy talking to the soul stones like a crazy maniac. Leon squeezed in through the small door and entered inside the house.

As soon as he entered inside the house, Leon closed the small door, but as soon as the door was closed... It made a small clicking sound.

*Click*

’Fuck...’

"Hmm...!?" Orion immediately turned back and noticed Leon standing there.

"Hello, Mr. Orion Ikol." Leon spoke with a smile as he looked at him.

"Now, Now, we don’t want to raise our voices right ? Because the skill that you are using right now to hide your real form from everyone can easily be cancelled if the villagers get to know about your truth.

Just a little suspicion and your form will be revealed to everyone. Now, do you want that Orion ?" Leon asked with a smile as he looked at him.

"Ho-How do you even know about that ? Who the hell are you ?" Orion asked with a serious expression.

"Oh me...? I am just a normal guy, who knows somethings about you. Like, how you are using everyone in this village as your fear producing machine and how you have killed their parents before they could’ve taught them anything about the outside world.

You raised them and made them believe in a fake god so that they will fear those cursed fairies and you can live on it.

You didn’t let them learn magic, so that they can’t become stronger than you." Leon spoke with a smile as he walked closer to Orion.

"Ah, I understand now... You are with my sister, that bitch sent you here to get them, isn’t that right ?" Orion asked with a serious expression as he looked at Leon.

"Oh no, no, no, no.... Hell no. You think I will just give them to you ? You are a human, a lesser race, a small big in my eyes... You don’t have any idea of my powers.

I can kill you right here and right now. No one will heard a single voice." Orion replied with a serious expression as he raised his hand and the wooden floor beneath him started growing like a tree.

Sharp stakes formed out of the growing wood and started hovering behind Orion.

"Each of these stakes will go through your heart and kill you before you can make a single sound.

Now, do you want that Leon ?" Orion asked with the same maniac smile as he looked at him.

<Bluff>

<Steelweb Armour>

[+45000 HP]

As soon as Leon activated the skill, his body got covered in the dark armour made out of steelweb spider’s web and without wasting another second, he raised his hand and grabbed Orion’s neck.

Chapter 236: Don’t play God

The blades immediately flew out of their sheath and silently went behind Orion’s back.

"Don’t even think of making any sounds, this house is covered in a special spell that can stop any sound from going-" Before Orion could’ve completed his sentence, all the blades came at him from behind with full speed.

Without wasting another second, Dusk jumped over Orion’s back and used his skill.

<Mana’s Gravity>

As soon as he used the skill, a very heavy gravitational pull was applied over Orion’s body, pushing the Nyxter’s blades even deeper into his back.

"AHHHHHHHHHH... THE BLADDESSS PLEASE STOPPPPP... I-I BEG YOU, PLEASE STOPPPPP." Orion shouted in pain as he couldn’t lift a finger because of the extreme gravity.

"How did you get powerful all of a sudden ? Tell me." Leon asked as he sat down on the floor and looked at him with a serious expression.

"I-I will... Please the swords... They are very painful and T-They are penetrating through my fucking bone-" Before Orion could’ve completed his sentence, Leon picked up a small dagger that was lying inside a fruit basket on the table.

"You are going to answer me or..." Leon spoke as he slowly brought the dagger over Orion’s head.

’Fuck... The force is so strong. The swords should’ve pierced through his body by now but his bones are still holding them even under this pressure. He is far stronger than I thought.

It’s good that I came back with a back-up plan in case he pulls something like this. Although I didn’t expected dusk to have such a powerful skill.’ Leon thought with a serious expression.

"W-Wait... I-If you kill me the magic runes around the village will wear off and everyone will be dead." Orion shouted as he looked at the dagger closing in on his head.

"What !? You have drawn those runes ?" Leon asked with a confused and shocked expression.

"T-The runes are being powered by these soul stones and if you kill me. The power supplying link will break and those runes will be nothing more than stones." Orion spoke as he tried to raise his hand but dusk immediately stopped him using his claw.

*Thud*

"Are you trying to buy time to pull something like before ? Because I am holding this dagger with all my strength. I let go and you will be fucking dead in a second.

I don’t give a shit about villagers, I will run away with the soul stones because those fairies can’t attack you when you hold them right ?" Leon asked with a smile as he brought the dagger closer to his face.

"I-I am not... I-I am telling you the truth. W-When I raised my hand, I casted a strong illusion spell that made all the villagers see their worst fears forming in front of their very eyes.

As soon as their fear spiked, my mana started regenerating rapidly. I-I don’t use normal mana like you, I use-"

"Negative Mana." Leon completed the sentence as he looked at Orion.

’So those runes only stop normal mana from regenerating, but negative mana can be regenerated.

I should’ve considered a possibility.’ Leon thought with a serious expression.

"Yes. Yes... I use negative mana. H-How do you know about-"

"Those soul stones... You took them out of the village. But the runes were still working. How is that possible ?" Leon asked with a serious expression.

"I-I am the link between them and the runes. They will work as long as I stay alive and I am really dying right now... What the hell are these blades even made out of ?

They are piercing my body unlike any other human weapon... If you don’t stop them now. One of them will pierce through my heart and kill me.

The sun is already down and I know you don’t want the blood of so many innocent people on your hands.

So, just remove your blades from my back and my face, w-we might be able to come at some kind of conclusion, right ?" Orion spoke as he looked at Leon.

"Oh... I am not stupid enough to leave you like that." Leon spoke with a smile as he immediately pulled out two wooden beads out of his pocket and showed them to Orion.

"These beads explode when I want them to explode and when they do... Oh, believe me... They make quiet an explosion.

One of them is enough to annihilate you but I am still using two just in case. Now... They will explode as soon as I command them to explode but, they will also explode if you try to take them out.

Do you understand ?" Leon asked with a smile as he showed them to Orion.

"Y-You can’t do that... W-What if you accidentally exploded my body ?" Orion asked with a scared expression as he looked at the beads.

"Well... We have to play the game of luck on that and believe me... If I felt that you are trying to play a trick on me, I will explode your body immediately.

You can enchant your body with magic from outside to get stronger but believe me, on the inside... You are also weak." Leon spoke with a menacing smile as he looked at Orion.

Since the bluff skill was still active, Orion had no choice but to believe in what Leon was saying.

Leon looked at the blades and tried to pull them out using the mana threads but even though he tried his best... The blades weren’t budging from their location.

’The gravitational pull is too strong... I have to use physical force as well.’ Leon spoke as he immediately grabbed one of the blade and pulled it using both mana thread and his hands.

"Fucking... Hell." Leon spoke as he pulled out the blade successfully and placed it aside.

He then placed one of the bead on the cut that the blade had left and the bead went inside the cut, instantly.

"Good." Leon spoke as he immediately pulled out one more blade from his body and planted another bead inside.

He then pulled out all the other blades and walked upto the front.

"I will be taking these from your hands and remember... don’t forget that if you tried to play some trick, your body will go boom in just a second." Leon spoke with a smile as he grabbed the bowl and collected the hovering soul stones into it.

"Dusk... Leave him once I get out of the house." Leon ordered as he looked at Dusk and started walking towards the exit door.

"As you command master." Dusk replied with a neutral expression.

"D-Don’t help h-her...." Orion spoke in a low voice.

"Hmm...?" Leon looked back with a confused expression.

"She is weak and too trusting. Her trust was the thing that got her entrapped by that Demonic Parasite.

Her trust was the reason why humans entered inside this forest and killed her. Join me... Leon and I will give you powers beyond you can think.

We will remake a better world... Where no one will fight and everyone will live in peace." Orion spoke with a serious expression.

"Ah... really ? How do you plan on doing that ? By killing every human ?" Leon asked with a neutral expression.

"This village is an example that I can make a world that lives in peace and harmony. I can make the humans right... They will forget everything about wars and murders.

They will-"

"Are you calling this village some kind of paradise for humans ?" Leon asked with a neutral expression as he walked upto him.

"No... But, I can make one if you support me. This village can be the foundation of a new beautiful world full of-" Before Orion could’ve completed his sentence, Leon placed his foot over his face.

"This is what it feels like living inside this village after knowing that you are the one controlling it.

I hope you are liking it." Leon spoke with a disgusted expression as he removed his foot from his face.

"Y-You will die one day and you will die like the other pathetic humans and you will regret not taking-" Before Orion could’ve completed his sentence, Leon exited through the main gate.

*Thud*

Dusk immediately went Invisible and turned off the gravity spell. Leaving Orion alone once again.

Chapter 237: Look before you land

Three Days Ago — Human Kingdom

On the northern outskirts of the Human Kingdom stood God’s Grace Orphanage.

The building was secluded, surrounded by wide fields and forests, far from any village. It was quiet, beautiful, and almost too peaceful—as if the world had forgotten it.

Inside, children from ages one to fourteen ran about, chasing chickens, feeding cows, or laughing as they played in the snow. Others helped in the kitchen, lending small hands to the caretakers and the sisters of the church.

---

"Yuna, could you help me with these clothes?"

A woman in her thirties, Sister Kole, bent down and struggled to lift a heavy bucket filled with dirty garments.

"Yes, Sister Kole!" Yuna, a cheerful girl of twelve, hurried over and grabbed the other side of the bucket. Together they carried it across the courtyard.

"Where are your friends today? Aren’t they always by your side?" Kole asked, smiling warmly.

"Ah... Klause is scribbling something with pen and paper again. And Rio—" Yuna hesitated, rolling her eyes a little, "—he’s lying on the snow, smiling at nothing, with that black cloth tied over his eyes. Honestly, I don’t understand him... but he’s not crazy. I’m sure of it."

Kole chuckled. "Let them play around a little. Keeping them tied to your hip won’t be good when they eventually leave."

"Sister, we’ve been together since we were little. I can’t just let those stupid heads wander off without me," Yuna replied with a playful smile.

They reached the wash area and set the bucket down. Yuna dumped the clothes onto the ground with a dull thud.

Sister Kole pressed a hand to her chest, fingers tightening around the star-shaped sigil of her necklace. Her lips moved in prayer.

"Oh Mother of All, bless us with your grace. Let your tears cleanse these garments."

At once, the water in the nearby tub rose into the air, shimmering as if alive. The garments floated inside it, swirling gently as the water scrubbed them clean.

Yuna’s eyes sparkled. "I hope someday I can learn spells like that. Then I could help the orphanage too."

Kole’s smile deepened. "You will, child. I’ll make sure you learn every basic spell you need. One day, you’ll be a fine caretaker."

"Thank you, Sister Kole!" Yuna bowed deeply.

"Come, let’s fetch the rest of the clothes," Kole said, and the two left together.

Chapter 270: Moon Rise
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Floor Fifteen, Dungeon...

Leon stood alone in the silent room, his bare upper body glistening faintly under the dim, cold light. His breath was steady, measured. The Nyxter rested firmly in his hands — its blackened edge humming with restrained mana.

Before him floated a translucent blue screen, glowing faintly in the air.

---

[Name: Last Rise]

[Rank: ??]

[Stance 1: 02 / 10,000]

[Description: A mysterious sword technique with no confirmed origins.]

[Mana: None]

[Cooldown: None]

---

’Hah... Only twice,’ Leon thought as his eyes trailed across the data on the screen. ’I’ve only used this technique twice since the day I got it. When that old man used it, he killed that Parasite in a single strike... and even cut down that massive tree of life behind it.

If I’m ever going to stand at that level, I need to understand it — no, learn it — and grasp the essence of swordsmanship as well.’

Leon raised his sword slightly. The Nyxter gleamed under the faint light as the air around him grew heavier with focus. At that instant, the ground trembled faintly, and a stone dummy rose up from the floor, assembling itself piece by piece until it stood before him.

’Elis made this chamber specifically for my training,’ Leon thought, glancing at the regenerating construct. ’No matter how many times I destroy it, it rebuilds itself — never in the same position twice. A perfect opponent to practice attack.’

He exhaled slowly.

"Last Rise..."

The words left his mouth in a whisper — and instantly, the dungeon vanished from his vision.

---

Leon opened his eyes again. He stood at the base of a vast, emerald-green plain stretching endlessly before him. In the distance rose a tall mountain bathed in silvery light. Above it, a brilliant full moon began to climb the horizon, glowing with haunting beauty.

"Oh my... You’re really trying to learn it, huh?" a familiar voice echoed behind him.

Leon turned around, his expression neutral. The old man stood there again, his white robe fluttering softly against the nonexistent wind.

"You again?" Leon asked, narrowing his eyes.

"What can I say?" the old man smirked. "My soul’s trapped here. It’s not like I want to see your face. Honestly, I thought you’d just give up and let me take over your body."

Leon tilted his head. "Why the hell are you even interested in my body?"

"You have money, women, and plenty of booze to waste your life on," the old man said dryly. "What else could a dead man ask for?"

Leon sighed. "I thought your wish was to free your soul from this technique."

"I do want that," the old man replied, folding his arms. "But if you’re going to fail anyway, might as well have some fun before I wait again."

"Fuck that," Leon muttered, his tone flat. "You’re not taking my body. Now answer my question — why does my consciousness keep getting dragged here every time I try to use the technique?"

The old man’s smile faded slightly. "Every form of Last Rise has a unique name — and each represents a trial. The one before you now..." He pointed at the mountain. "...is to climb and reach the top where the full moon shines. That’s the trial. Hence, the name of the first form— Last Rise: Full Moon."

Leon crossed his arms. "So every time I use the technique, my consciousness will be pulled here?"

"Only when you’re in safe places," the old man replied. "Like now. You’ll need to perform the technique-"

"Ten thousand times. Yeah, I know." Leon’s voice carried no surprise.

"Nothing less expected from you," the old man chuckled softly.

"What’s your name, anyway ?" Leon asked after a moment.

The old man blinked. "I don’t remember. It’s been a thousands of years since I became a soul fragment. I remember nothing of my past — only this technique."

Leon studied him for a moment, then said calmly, "I’ll call you White Fox — you’ve got those sly eyes, that stupid white robe, and you smile like a fox too."

"Hmph." The old man smirked faintly. "Call me whatever you wish. But for now, listen carefully."

He gestured toward the glowing moon. "Look at it, Leon. What do you see?"

"A beautiful full moon," Leon said, his tone even.

"Once you start staring at it, you can’t stop, can you?" White Fox asked, a knowing smile on his face.

Leon’s eyes didn’t waver. "I can. It’s just... I don’t want to."

"Exactly," White Fox said softly. "That’s the key. When you raise your sword, it creates mirrored afterimages behind the blade — hypnotic to the eyes. For a few seconds, the enemy becomes entranced by the motion. You have exactly three seconds to take their head off. Fail, and the trance breaks."

Leon frowned slightly. "I tried it once — on a Goblin Lord — but the technique collapsed midway."

"Of course it did," White Fox said sternly. "This technique doesn’t rely on mana, stamina, or agility. It’s forged through focus and discipline. Move too fast or too slow, and the illusion shatters. The rhythm must be perfect — your will must be absolute.

It’s like looking at a serpent with calm expression and moving him to your will."

Leon’s gaze sharpened. "And how am I supposed to know when it’s perfect?"

"The higher you climb that mountain," White Fox replied, pointing again toward the moonlit peak, "the clearer the moon will appear to you. When the moon shines brightest — when its form stops wavering — that’s when you’ve mastered the first form. Then, your sword will no longer cut flesh or stone... it will slice through the very air itself."

"How-"Leon’s mouth opened to speak, but before he could utter a word, his surroundings shattered.

"Oh yeah ? If you can tell me from which side this blade is off-balanced... I will become your slave right here and right now." Gerald replied with a smile as he looked at Leon.

"From the handle side...?" Leon spoke with a confused expression.

"Nope... Now check out the skill that you got." Gerald spoke as he sat down on the wooden chair and picked up the jug that was full of booze.

<Inspect>

[Name: Nyxter]

[Type: Weapon]

[Rank: Rare]

[Crafter: Gerald Lon Kiggester]

[Description: A special sword crafted by legendary blacksmith Gerald Lon Kiggester. Forged from Travertine — a sacred, near-indestructible material from the Dwarf Kingdom. Don’t be fooled by its dark look. The sword houses five distinct razor sharp blades, capable of slicing through nearly any surface.]

[Skill slots: 5]

[Skills: {Blade Of Artimes}]

[Enchantments: None]

’Huh...’ Leon thought as he immediately clicked on the skill.

[Name: Blade of Artimes]

[Rank: Rare]

[Description: Upon activation, enchants the sword’s edge for 10 minutes, increasing its sharpness tenfold.

Each successful strike on a target applies a stackable Poison debuff, dealing 100 damage and reducing regeneration speed by 80% every second until the skill ends. Regeneration speed reduction does not stack but Poison debuff does.]

[Mana: 50000]

[Cooldown: 120 seconds]

’My, My... Would you look at that ? What a great skill it is.’ Leon thought as a big smile spread across his face.

"Looks like it’s a really good skill, since you are smiling like that." Gerald spoke with a smile as he looked at Leon.

"It’s good." Leon replied with a smile.

"What is-"

*Swish*

Before Gerald could’ve asked his question, Elis appeared in between them and bowed down to Leon immediately.

"Master... Sir Rees is standing on the dungeon’s entrance. Should I let him inside ?" Elis asked with a neutral expression.

"Rees is back ?" Leon asked with a shocked and surprised expression.

"Yes, Master." Elis replied.

"Is he alone ?"

"There is a lady with him as well, Master."

"I will go and see them myself." Leon spoke as he immediately placed the nyxter into his inventory and disappeared from the floor.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 292: Always Die strong
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

*Swish*

In a ripple of spatial energy, Leon materialized on the first floor of the dungeon. The moment his feet touched the cold stone, the massive gates groaned open on their own, ancient mechanisms grinding to life as if welcoming their master home.

"Why the hell aren’t these doors opening u—Ahhhhhhh..."

*Clank-Clank-Clank-Clank*

The heavy doors had barely parted when a skeletal figure came tumbling through, rolling down the short flight of stairs in a chaotic clatter of bones. Rees came to a halt mere inches from Leon’s boots, sprawled in an undignified heap.

"Hello, Rees." Leon’s lips curved into a faint, amused smile as he gazed down at the lich.

"Sir Leon... I greet you." Rees scrambled to his feet with surprising grace for a being made of bone, then bowed deeply, his empty sockets fixed respectfully on the ground.

"It’s been a while... Where have you been?" Leon’s expression shifted to neutral, though a hint of curiosity flickered in his eyes.

"Haha... You see, there was—"

*Step-step*

The sound of light footsteps echoed from beyond the gates. A woman stepped into the dungeon’s dim light, her presence carrying the faint scent of blooming flowers and ancient forests.

Leon’s eyes widened in genuine surprise. Standing before him was none other than Medina—the former Mother of Fairies herself—her ethereal beauty undimmed, beautiful hair cascading like moonlight.

"I greet the Dungeon Master." Medina offered a warm smile as she bowed gracefully to Leon.

"Well, well... If it isn’t the Mother of Fairies." He returned the smile, his tone laced with playful recognition.

"Not anymore..." Medina’s cheeks tinted with a soft blush as she straightened. "I have decided to give up that title. Now, I am just Mr. Rees’ wife."

"Eh...? EHHH...?" Leon’s composure cracked entirely. His gaze darted back and forth between the Rees and the ex-fairy mother, confusion etched across his features.

"It’s true, Sir Leon. I have married Medina, and now we are a married couple." Rees rubbed the back of his smooth skull in a remarkably human gesture of embarrassment, as if scratching an itch that no longer existed.

Leon stared for a long moment, processing the absurdity of it all. "I have so many questions... But I’m only going to ask two. When did this happen? And where are the people from the village?"

Medina’s smile brightened, her eyes sparkling with quiet joy as she explained. "When you left the forest, I had intended to give up my powers entirely to relinquish my title. But Sir Rees... he offered a better way. A way to step down without losing everything."

She glanced fondly at Rees who was now standing beside her. "A Fairy Mother cannot marry. The moment she does, the title is stripped away, but most of her powers remain. And so... we got married. We sealed our vows at the Pool of Life and Death, under the ancient waters that bind souls eternally."

"That’s why it took us some time to return," Rees added, his bony fingers intertwining with Medina’s delicate ones. "When we came back to the village, the people were happy. The fairies had begun teaching them magic, real magic. After long discussions, the villagers decided to stay. They’re now building a new home deep in the heart of the forest, together with the fairies. A place where humans and fairies can live peacefully."

Leon listened in silence, his face betraying none of the storm raging inside.

’Fucking hell... All those fuckable women, gone just like that. An entire village of opportunities went up in smoke. Damn it, I should’ve at least brought back that one girl... the one with no gag reflex. What a waste.’

His expression twisted into one of profound regret, brows furrowed and lips pressed into a thin line.

"Something wrong, Mr. Leon?" Medina tilted her head, concern creasing her flawless features.

"Can you still read my mind?" Leon asked quickly, a touch of alarm in his voice.

"Oh no, no..." She laughed lightly, shaking her head. "That ability was taken with the title. My thoughts are my own now and everyone else’s are safe from me."

"Good." Leon exhaled, relief washing over him. Then, a mischievous glint returned to his eyes. He raised both hands, making exaggerated, unmistakable gestures, the universal sign for having sex. "Say, now that both of you are married... How are you going to... you know..."

"Hmm...? What does that—" Medina began, genuine confusion clouding her expression.

"It’s a spiritual wedding, Sir Leon," Rees cut in smoothly, his tone firm as he looked at Leon, "We don’t require a physical relationship to prove our bond."

"Oh well... She’s your wife. Do whatever you want with her." Leon shrugged with a wicked grin, placing his hand over Rees’ shoulder. "But if she ever needs some physical satisfaction, you know where to find me. Just two floors down."

"Oh, do I still live on floor eighteen, Sir Leon ?" Rees asked.

"Yes... but for now, you’re coming with me. Elis will show Medina to the eighteenth floor." Leon’s voice returned to its usual commanding neutrality.

*Swish*

A soft distortion in the air, and Elis materialized behind him like a shadow given form. She bowed deeply, her movements precise and elegant.

"As you command, Master. But... for some reason, I can’t teleport her to the floor. She is protected with some kind of magic."

"Ah... Try now." Medina gave Elis a knowing smile, her aura shifting subtly as some invisible restriction lifted.

*Swish*

In an instant, both of them vanished, leaving only the faint scent of Medina’s floral magic lingering in the air.

Leon turned to Rees without missing a beat. "Take a look at this." He flicked his wrist, tossing a dark, pulsating core toward him. The core of the Gillian Hound, still thrumming with otherworldly energy.

*Catch*

Rees snatched it mid-air. He held it up, staring in silence for a long moment. Then, with a soft tap against his own skull, a thin red beam shot from his left eye socket, scanning the core like a machine analyzing data.

"Huh... This is not from this realm, is it?" Rees finally said, his voice low.

"Nope." Leon smirked as he started walking toward the dungeon’s exit, the cool night air already beckoning.

"May I ask what realm this is from?" Rees followed close behind, the core still cradled carefully in his bony palm.

"I don’t remember." Leon glanced upward as they emerged into the open. Dark clouds raced across the sky, driven by a restless wind that whispered through the trees. "All I know is that tonight, I’m consuming it. And we’re going deep into the forest for that."

"Sir Leon... The mana in this thing is abnormal." Rees’s tone carried genuine caution. "From what I can detect, it will flood your body and mind with violent bloodlust. Uncontrollable, perhaps."

"I know that as well." Leon’s smile didn’t waver.

"And... I suppose you’re still going to consume it?"

"Yes. And I want you there to watch." Leon replied with a neutral expression. "If I lose control or if I try to do something stupid, you will have to contain me. Hold me in a magic barrier or something until I’ve absorbed every last drop of its power."

Rees shook his head, a dry rattle echoing from his jaw. "Always doing something crazy... You are truly one strange human. It’s like you have no fear of death."

Leon chuckled softly, accepting the core back and slipping it into his inventory. "Sometimes it’s better to die strong than to die weak. And believe me, I’ve died weak before. It’s not good."

As they walked deeper into the shadowed woods, a warm, ethereal blue light suddenly bathed the world from above. Both of them stopped and looked skyward.

Two brilliant full moons hung in the clear night sky, their azure glow washing over the forest like a gentle benediction as the dark clouds vanished completely from the sky.

"Looks like we’ve survived the winters successfully..." Leon murmured with a smile.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 293: Mikael Malkovich
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Two blue moons hung high in the sky, casting an ethereal glow over the forest as slow, chilling winds rustled through the trees.

Rees and Leon trudged deeper into the woods, their weapons at the ready, senses sharpened against the encroaching shadows.

Rees gripped a long wand in his left hand, its tip humming faintly with one offensive pre-cast spell and one defensive barrier primed for instant activation.

Leon held Nyxter fully sheathed, invisible mana threads coiled around each blade like taut spider silk, ready to unleash the sword in a heartbeat.

"This area of the forest... it’s unnaturally silent," Rees murmured, channeling a scanning spell that pulsed outward, probing the surroundings for hidden threats.

"The crickets are still chirping, and those birds circling above suggest no predators are around," Leon replied in a hushed tone, his eyes locked on the winding path ahead, every muscle coiled for action.

"How deep are we going, Sir Leon?" Rees asked, his voice laced with hesitation.

"Deep enough so that no one can interrupt me," Leon answered flatly, pressing forward without breaking stride.

"Sir Leon... we’ve already delved farther than any adventurer has ever came," Rees pressed.

"I know. Now, stay quiet for a moment," Leon commanded.

’In case my bloodlust spirals out of control, I need a safeguard,’ Leon thought, his expression unchanging as he used the skill.

"Skill Crafter..."

<Skill Crafter>

[Please pick a name for the skill...]

"Calm Mind."

[Skill ’Calm Mind’ has been created.]

[Rank has been settled to ’D’ by default.]

[State the use of skill.]

"As soon as the skill is used, it will stabilize the user’s mind and calm it down from any overwhelming emotions."

[Processing....]

[Error: User can’t craft this particular skill.]

Leon’s eyes widened in bewilderment as the notification flashed before him.

"What the—" But before he could finish, an ominous shift gripped the air.

The crickets fell silent. The birds halted their flight, vanishing into the canopy. The forest plunged into an eerie, suffocating quiet.

"Mr. Leon..." Rees whispered, raising his wand and pivoting sharply.

"Get ready," Leon growled, his voice serious as he yanked Nyxter free in a fluid motion.

*Swish*

"But... I don’t sense anything. My skill shows nothing—"

"Hello."

The voice slithered from the left, cold and mocking.

Leon and Rees whipped around in unison, locking eyes on a figure emerging from the underbrush—a man in tattered rags, long black hair cascading to his knees, jagged nails glinting on his left hand. Blood smeared his lips and chin, a gruesome crimson mask.

*Step*

He stepped fully into view, and Leon’s blood ran cold. In his hidden right hand, he clutched two severed heads: one a hob-goblin’s, the other a crimson-skinned variant with twin horns jutting from its forehead.

Without wasting another second, Leon used his skill.

<Inspect>

[Name: Mikael Malkovich]

[Class: Chimera]

[Race: Vendigo]

[Level: 372]

[HP: 370002000/370002000]

[MP: 45000000/36486287]

[Description: Mikael Malkovich was part of an experiment done by the Lopez household’s leader in order to fight demons, but after fighting the Demon general Gurua and eating her meat, Mikael unlocked hyper regeneration and gained immense powers in exchange for his sanity.

Mikael is neither a monster nor a human, he is something else. He still holds his grudge for the Lopez household and wants to slaughter them once again.]

’Fucking monster...’ Leon’s mind raced, adrenaline surging as he activated every combat skill in his arsenal.

<Multi-Cast>

<Steelweb Armour>

<Leeching Aura>

<Reverberate>

<Life Steal>

<Adrenaline Rush>

<Blade of Artemis>

...

Power flooded his veins in an instant. His aura erupted like a storm, encasing his body in pitch-black metallic armor that gleamed ominously under the moonlight. His eyes darkened to abyssal voids, every sense heightened to razor sharpness.

"Here I thought the Red Ogre would be my meal... but it seems I’ve got dessert too," Mikael sneered, dropping the heads with casual disdain.

*THUD-THUD*

*Swish*

In a blur, Mikael vanished, reappearing behind Leon like a phantom. "This armor won’t save you..." His nail pierced Leon’s shoulder, slicing through the Steelweb Armour as if it were parchment, drawing a hot spray of blood.

Pain lanced through Leon’s arm, but he spun with explosive speed, Nyxter whipping through the air in a deadly arc—only to cleave empty space. Mikael was already gone.

’His speed... it’s inhuman,’ Leon thought, gritting his teeth as sweat beaded on his brow.

*Swish*

Mikael materialized inches from Leon’s face, languidly licking the blood from his nail. *Lick* "Hmm... tastes off. You haven’t been eating well. Oh well... time to hunt you.

Why don’t you start running ?" Mikael’s grin widened, predatory and feral, as his nails elongated into razor-sharp metallic claws, glinting with lethal promise.

Leon didn’t flinch. He shifted his stance, Nyxter humming with restrained power, his breath steady despite the pounding of his heart.

"That’s new... No one’s ever stood their ground after realizing they’re just a bug to me," Mikael chuckled, his voice dripping with amusement. Then, in a heartbeat, he blurred out of sight.

’Fuc—’ Leon’s thought cut off as instinct screamed. He whirled, predicting the strike—but Mikael wasn’t behind him.

<Hex-Mana Shield>

*Tang*

"Above you... Sir!" Rees bellowed, thrusting his wand forward. A shimmering barrier erupted around Leon, layers of hexagonal mana weaving into an impenetrable dome.

Leon glanced up just in time, Mikael hovered mid-air, claws descending like falling stars, slowly but inexorably piercing the shield with a grinding *crack* of fracturing energy.

"Fuck..." Leon cursed, diving right as the barrier shattered in a cascade of sparks.

*Thud*

Mikael slammed into the earth, craters forming under his feet. He inspected his claws with mild curiosity, then turned his gaze to Rees, a wicked smile curling his lips.

"Hah..." In a flash, he vanished again, reappearing behind Rees like death’s shadow, claws slashing towards his necro-heart.

*Swish*

But Leon was faster as he had already predicted his move. He pivoted with blinding precision, Nyxter singing through the air in a horizontal sweep that connected with Mikael’s wrist.

*Cut*

The hand severed clean, tumbling to the dirt in a spray of dark ichor.

*Thud*

Mikael didn’t scream or retreat. He stared at the stump, confusion flickering across his features as regeneration crawled at a snail’s pace, tendrils of flesh knitting far too slowly.

"What?" he muttered, eyes narrowing. The army that should’ve been repaired immediately is taking so much time just to stop the bleeding.

Leon seized the opening.

"LAST RISE: FULL MOON!"

His voice thundered as he channeled the skill, Nyxter whirling in a hypnotic circular motion. Moonlight seemed to amplify the blade’s edge, casting illusory foreshadows that danced like phantoms, drawing Mikael’s gaze into a trance.

The stance shattered. Nyxter exploded forward, a silver streak aimed at Mikael’s neck with velocity that warped the air.

Mikael’s instincts fired. His bare feet clamped the ground, toes and claws digging deep into soil like anchors. With a guttural snarl, he twisted his body at the last possible instant, the blade grazing his throat in a whisper of wind.

*SWISH*

*THUD-THUD-THUD-THUD-THUD*

Nyxter’s blades detached in a chaotic storm, hurtling outward like shrapnel. Trees splintered and toppled in rapid succession, bark exploding as the projectiles carved through the forest in devastating arcs.

*Haaaah*

Leon exhaled a steamy breath, sweat slicking his armor as he reset his stance, pointing Nyxter’s handle at Mikael.

"Nyxter..." he intoned, grip tightening. In a whirlwind rush, the blades reversed course, snapping back into place with metallic *clinks*.

Mikael’s casual demeanor shattered, replaced by a deadly glare. Carelessness evaporated; he now faced a true threat.

"I’ll hang your head in my lair..." he spoke, voice a low rumble of fury, claws flexing as tension crackled in the air between them.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 294: Billi-taash of Deadman’s Spell
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Leon and Mikael circled each other in the middle of the forest as Rees continued to cast defensive barriers around Leon’s body with all the buffs he had.

’He is fast... too fast... Even after so much concentration, I can only see some of his movements.’ Leon thought with a serious expression as sweat beads formed over his forehead.

His grip on the Nyxter’s handle tightened... As he used another skill.

<Glitch Point>

As soon as he used the skill, thousands of red dots appeared all over Mikael’s body...

’He is completely vulnerable and has no defensive spells... it’s his dexterity that makes him lethal. Only if I can make him a little slower-’ Suddenly Leon’s eyes widened as he remembered something crucial.

Without wasting another second Leon stopped circling and stabbed his sword into the ground.

*Stab*

"Hmm...?" Mikael looked at Leon with a confused expression as he couldn’t understand what was happening.

"Come on then..." Leon spoke with a smile as he placed both of his hands into his pocket and deactivated the steelweb armour.

"S-Sir Leon, W-What are you doing ? He will kill both of us." Rees spoke in a worried tone as he couldn’t understand what Leon was trying to do.

"Are you accepting defeat ?" Mikael asked with a confused and suspicious expression.

"Sure..." Leon replied with a smile as he looked at Mikael.

"KEKEKEKEKEKEKE... HAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHA... I THOUGHT YOU WERE BETTER THAN THIS... YOU ARE THE STRONGEST OPPONENT I HAVE FACED TILL THIS DAY BUT YOU HAVE ACCEPTED DEFEAT IN SUCH AN EASY WAY... HAHAHAHA..." Mikael shouted loudly as he laughed like a crazed maniac.

’Oh well... No matter how much power you have, it won’t matter if you don’t have a working brain.’ Leon thought with a smirk as he continued to look at Mikael.

"I shall consume you now..." Mikael spoke as he immediately disappeared from his position and re-appeared right in front of his face with his hand raised high in the air ready to kill Leon with one strike.

*Click*

But, before he could’ve even touched him... His whole body froze.

’1...’

Leon didn’t waste time and pulled out both of his hands from his pockets immediately.

’2...’

As soon as he pulled his hands out, a broken pocket watch fell down.

"Chitty Chitty, Bang Bang... Motherfucker." Leon spoke as he immediately grabbed the nyxter’s handle and pulled it out from the ground.

’3... 4...’

*Swish*

In just a split second, Nyxter’s blades sliced through Mikael’s throat and beheaded him.

’5.’

As soon as the five seconds were over...

*THUD-THUD*

Mikael’s head got separated from his body and fell down on the ground.

"W-What !?" Rees asked with a shocked tone as he couldn’t understand what just happened.

"Finally this came in handy... All the mana that I fed it was all worth it." Leon spoke in a low voice as he picked up the pocket watch from the ground and placed it back in his pocket.

"Sir... Leon... I-I don’t understand, how did you-!? When did you !?" Rees asked as he looked at Leon.

"Don’t worry... You will understand with time." Leon replied with a smile as he looked at Rees.

’Wait... Something is not right, I didn’t level up after killing him. He is a monster of such a high level and I didn’t get any experience points... That means...’

*Crack*

With cracking sounds, Mikael’s body started moving once again.

"How the fuck is that even possible ?" Leon asked with a serious expression as he raised the nyxter once again in order to end it but before he could’ve landed the hit, Mikael’s body moved rapidly to the side.

"Billi-taash of the Deadman’s Spell." Rees spoke as he looked at the moving headless corpse.

"What’s that ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"It’s a forbidden spell that can be used on chimeras and demons, since they have good regeneration abilities and more than one hearts.

If the head of the host is removed from the body... The body goes into a reflexive state, where the primary target of the body is to get back to safety and rejoin the head to itself." Rees explained in a hurry.

As soon as Leon turned to look at the body again, he noticed that it wasn’t there and neither was the head.

"Fucking hell..." Leon spoke with a serious expression.

"Should I cast a scanning spell to find the body, Sir ?" Rees asked.

’I can find it if I want with ’Compass Of Heart’ but for now I have to absorb that core. I used everything I had up my sleeve in order to kill him and yet he survived.

Ordanto’s watch will take around 24 hours to recharge once again and I have to give all of my mana to it while it recharges. I have to get stronger fast.’ Leon thought as he immediately sheathed the nyxter.

"Let’s go." Leon replied as he started walking deeper into the forest.

"Yes." Rees replied as he started following Leon.

30 minutes later...

Both of them came to a halt and looked around.

"This place is good..." Leon spoke as he sat down on the ground in lotus position.

He then took out the Gillian Hound’s core from his inventory and ate it without thinking twice.

*Nom-Nom*

"Hmm... Tastes like blue berry." Leon spoke in a low voice as he closed his eyes and started focusing in order to control his bloodlust.

But as soon as he closed his eyes... A tingling sensation started crawling throughout his whole body.

His heart started beating faster and faster as all the veins in his body started to pop up.

"This can’t be good..." Rees spoke in a low voice as he immediately casted a very strong defensive dome around Leon.

’Fuck... Fuck... It hurts... IT HURTS LIKE HELLLLL...’ Leon shouted in his mind but kept his mouth closed, keeping his concentration on one point in order to take his mind off of the pain but it didn’t work.

Suddenly, blood started flowing out of his hands, eyes, mouth and ears.

"Sir, Leon... You have to stop this is not-" Rees started speaking as he walked inside the protection barrier and tried to stop Leon but as soon as Rees’ hand touch Leon’s shoulder...

*Grab*

*Crack*

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 295: Rexian Bear
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As soon as Rees’ hand touched Leon’s shoulder, Leon’s hand shot up like a vice and clamped down on it with terrifying force.

Grab

Crack

"Shit-" Rees cursed as he instinctively tried to pull back.

*Crack*

The sound didn’t stop.

Bone fragments grinded against each other inside Leon’s fist as Rees’ hand was crushed mercilessly. Blood leaked out between Leon’s fingers, dripping onto the dirt below.

Leon’s expression twisted grotesquely. His brows furrowed, his jaw clenched so hard it looked like his teeth might shatter. Every muscle in his body trembled as if on the verge of tearing itself apart.

’It hurts like hell... no—worse than hell,’ Leon thought, his consciousness wavering.

’It’s like my body is being thrown into a fucking grinder again and again. It stops for a moment... just long enough to give me hope... and then it starts all over again.’

His breathing grew uneven.

’Thousands of needles... no—millions—piercing every part of my body at once. Inside my bones. Inside my veins. Fuck... how long do I have to endure this!?’

Rees staggered back, clutching the ruined remains of his hand, his expression pale despite his undead body.

’I don’t think Master Leon will be able to successfully absorb all the power of that core,’ Rees thought grimly as he forced himself to sit on the stump of a fallen tree nearby.

’But... if he somehow does... then nothing in this world will be able to stop him after this.’

Six hours passed and night passed...

"Hm...?" Rees slowly lifted his head.

A thin ray of sunlight pierced through the canopy of the forest, illuminating the ground in a soft golden hue.

’It’s morning already...’ His hollow gaze returned to Leon.

Leon was still seated on the ground, completely motionless.

Dried blood crusted his face, neck, and arms. His skin looked scorched, almost cooked, as if intense heat had been sealed beneath it. With every slow breath he took, white-hot steam poured out of his mouth and nostrils.

Shhhhhhh...

Fuuuuuu....

His chest rose and fell sluggishly. Sweat gathered across his body in thick layers, dripping down like condensation on burning metal.

’A normal human would’ve been dead long ago...’ Rees thought, his voice heavy.

’How strong is Sir Leon’s will... to endure such agony for so long without losing himself?’

Crack—Crack—Crack—Crack

Suddenly, the sound of breaking echoed through the clearing.

Rees’ eyes widened.

Leon’s skin began to ripple unnaturally, as if something beneath it was shifting and rearranging itself.

"What the- !?" Rees jumped back to his feet.

Crack—Crack—Crack—Crack

Leon’s eyes snapped open.

They were completely bloodshot.

"AHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH!!!"

His scream tore through the forest like a dying beast’s howl. The pain returned all at once, magnified beyond comprehension. Bones shattered inside his body one after another, twisting and snapping as if an invisible force was tearing him apart from within.

His limbs bent at impossible angles. His body convulsed violently, as though possessed by something monstrous.

"T-The final phase...!" Rees shouted, panic bleeding into his voice.

"It’s the final phase! SIR LEON—THIS IS THE LAST PHASE OF THE ABSORPTION! YOUR BODY IS RECONSTRUCTING ITS SKELETAL STRUCTURE! YOU HAVE TO SURVIVE THROUGH IT!"

CRACK—CRACK—CRACK—CRACK—CRACK—CRACK—CRACK

"IT HURTS LIKE HEEEEELLLLLLLLLLLLLLLLL—!!!" Leon screamed.

"IT HURTS—IT FUCKING HURTS—!!!"

Larger bones snapped violently. His legs twisted with sickening sounds.

Rees clenched his fists.

’He won’t survive like this...’

’Water. I need water—now.’

He slammed his wand into the ground.

<Undead’s Scan>

Mana spread outward in invisible waves.

"Fuck..." Rees muttered. "No river nearby..."

Gritting his teeth, he poured more mana into the spell.

"I’ll extend the range to the maximum."

It didn’t take long.

Ten kilometers away—a river.

"There."

"Alzeris Vom... Julan Revio... Telekenris... Roka."

Thud

The spell activated.

Leon’s broken, convulsing body slowly lifted into the air.

’The heat inside him is rising too fast,’ Rees thought urgently.

’If I don’t get him into water immediately... his body will explode.’

He quickly shoved his wand aside and pulled a piece of paper from within his robe. Dense runes covered its surface, an emergency spell.

"I’m sorry, Medina..." Rees whispered.

"But this is an emergency."

"Olmar Ilon Yumol Loruo... Des Tele Froun Porte."

Thud

The moment he struck the ground with his wand, all the mana inside his body was violently drained.

Both of them vanished.

...

Ten kilometers away...

Swish

Leon and Rees reappeared in front of a massive river connected to a towering, majestic waterfall.

Using the very last drop of mana in his body, Rees guided Leon over the river.

The spell collapsed.

SPLASH

Leon’s body crashed into the water.

His screams stopped instantly.

Thud

Rees’ body suddenly stiffened.

He fell face-first onto the ground like a discarded doll.

’Shit...’

’All my mana is gone... Those two spells took everything...’

’It’s fine... the necro-heart will regenerate some mana soon and I’ll be able to’

Shush

His thoughts froze.

’What... was that?’

Sniff—Sniff

’It’s near my legs...’

Sniff—Sniff—Sniff

The sound grew closer.

Rees’ hollow eyes slowly shifted upward as a massive shadow loomed over him.

"Gaww...?"

A monstrous creature stared down at him.

A Rexian Bear.

Sixteen feet tall. Ten feet wide.

Two massive heads.

One head sniffed Rees’ bony body.

The other sniffed toward the river.

Its scarred head was covered in deep cuts and old woundsmarks of countless battles it had survived.

’No... no no no...’ Rees’ mind screamed.

’Not one of these... Why does it have to be a Rexian Bear?’

Leon summoned Nyxter in a single fluid motion. The black blade materialized in his grip, edge shining from the sunlight.

"Assassin," he said lightly, never taking his eyes off Melare, "you can leave now."

Dredis didn’t need to be told twice.

"T-Thanks..."

He vanished from the branch like smoke.

Leon tilted his head.

"Yes," he answered simply. "I was the one."

<Flowbind Breathing>

*Fuuuuu—*

Melare’s lips peeled back.

"You are dead."

He vanished.

A heartbeat later he reappeared directly behind Leon, four massive fists already hammering forward.

<Iron Punches: Dimensional Hit>

Each punch turned metallic, the air screaming as they tore through sound barrier itself.

<Blind Spot>

Leon disappeared from Melare’s vision for exactly one second.

THUD—THUD—THUD—THUD!

Four craters exploded into the scorched earth where Leon had stood.

"Master..." Fruela breathed with a worried expression.

"His head..." Dusk said, already rising to his feet as his eyes focused on Leon completely.

Melare’s eyes widened.

"What the—"

A white flash spread across his neck.

Leon reappeared behind the demon, Nyxter’s edge dripping vivid blue blood.

*Swish.*

*Thud.*

Melare’s head toppled cleanly from his shoulders and rolled across the ash.

"Wow... he died that fast?" Rees asked in low voice.

Enid’s voice trembled.

"He’s not dead. Master, his head is regenerating—"

A brutal fist exploded from the side toward Leon’s face.

"I know," Leon said, smiling as he looked at Enid.

He ducked under the blow without hurry.

The punch whistled past his mask, close enough to stir the air.

*Swish—*

Leon straightened, turning slowly.

Melare already stood whole again—new head sprouting seamlessly from the stump, flesh knitting with sickening wet sounds.

"Well, well..." Leon murmured, smile widening. "Aren’t you regenerating fast?"

"Oh... I have seen this before." Rees spoke as he looked at Leon.

"Hmm...? What do you mean ?" Dusk asked with a confused expression.

"Didn’t I tell you about the Hob-goblin that sir Leon played with ?" Rees asked in return.

"Yes, what about it ?" Dusk replied.

"Well... He is doing same with the Demon. The movement of sir Leon’s body are lazy and slow, he is dodging the attacks slowly on purpose."

"You are telling me that you think master is playing with that demon ?" Dusk asked with a shocked expression.

"I don’t think that, I just know he is playing with the Demon." Rees replied.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 305: Demon Arts
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

’Ever since I came to this world, I have fought many monsters, and there was a very funny thing about every monster.

They do attacks in the same pattern again and again, like some kind of NPC who keeps repeating the same actions. I don’t know how, but I understand it every time I fight a monster, and once I understand it... I like to play with it a little.’

Leon thought as he stepped in front of Melare and looked at him with a mocking smile.

"You—"

Before Melare could complete his sentence, he disappeared from his position.

’Now he will appear behind me and try to punch me from all four sides with his metal punches.’

Leon thought with a smile as he felt Melare reappearing behind him.

’...And I will just use my skill. Since my skill Blind Spot has already bumped up two ranks, it feels good to use.’

---

[Name: Blind Spot]

[Rank: D]

[Description: Turns the user invisible for exactly one second. If the user is within a 3-meter range of the opponent, the user will be teleported behind the opponent.

The skill can be re-cast, but with every usage, mana consumption increases by +5 MP and cooldown increases by 30 seconds.]

---

"DIEEEEEE...!" Melare shouted as his fists turned into metal once again and slammed down at Leon with full force.

<Blind Spot>

*Swish*

Leon disappeared from his spot and reappeared directly behind Melare.

’That’s his first move... Now he will turn around and give me a side punch.’

Leon thought with a smile as he ducked to the side without even looking at the punch.

*Swish*

Melare threw the side punch exactly as Leon predicted, but it missed.

"Is that all you have?" Leon asked with a smile.

"YOUUUUUU... FINE! I WILL SHOW YOU MY TECHNIQUE BEFORE KILLING YOU!"

Melare shouted in rage as he immediately formed a lotus-like hand seal with all four of his hands.

<DEMONIC ARTS — THIRD FORM: BLOOMING LOTUS DOMAIN>

"Oh shit... it’s a domain technique," Rees spoke with a serious expression as he immediately tried to counter the spell—but it had already taken effect.

As soon as the skill activated, everything around them changed into a huge dirty pond with thousands of lotus flowers floating over it.

"W-What kind of technique is this?" Fruela shouted as the tree beneath her feet disappeared and she fell into the pond.

"These things... why are they sticking to my legs?" Dusk asked as he quickly moved away from the lotus flowers closing in on him from every direction.

’A demonic art... It’s a fucking demonic art.’ Enid thought as her expression turned grim.

"If you stay angry or think about violence, even in your mind, these lotus flowers will cover you completely and choke you to death."

She immediately plunged her nails into her right arm and let her blood drip into the pond.

The moment her blood touched the dirty water, all the lotus flowers around them began to wither and die.

"How did you do that?" Dusk asked with a confused expression.

"I have poison mixed in my blood," Enid replied seriously. "I can activate it at will and kill anything that breathes."

"That’s not a good ability for monsters who eat demons," Rees muttered as he looked at her.

"Look..." Dusk suddenly spoke, pointing his claw towards Leon.

...

The lotus flowers were crawling all over Leon’s body, covering him from every side in thick layers.

Yet, he stood completely still, doing nothing to resist.

"The more you think about killing me, the more you’ll be restrained by these flowers. Once they cover your entire body, it’s impossible to escape.

You can try to run, but you cannot outrun my domain... Hahahahaha! Why aren’t you saying anything now ? Scared ?"

Melare asked with a wide grin.

’My lower body is completely covered in these lotus flowers. Just like he said, I can’t move my legs even a bit. It’s like I’m trapped inside an extremely tight plaster.’ Leon thought calmly.

’Well... it’s a good thing I made this skill while waiting for Dusk.’

A screen popped up in front of him.

---

[Name: Multiple Control]

[Rank: D]

[Description:

When activated, this skill taps into a portion of the user’s unconscious mind, establishing independent connections with each {Mana Thread}.

Through this link and the support of {Sixth Sense}, the threads automatically move to protect the user from any detected danger.

When the user is actively focusing, the threads follow his precise will and intent.

When the user’s attention is elsewhere, the threads operate purely on instinct, reacting with split-second movements based on {Sixth Sense} warnings.]

---

’Turns out I can also write bigger and more specific descriptions now. It’ll be fun to make skills after this.’

Leon thought as a smirk spread across his face.

’Did he just smile...?’

Melare thought as his expression hardened.

<Multi-Cast>

<Multiple Control>

As soon as Leon activated the skill, all the blades of Nyxter separated and began floating in a circle around him.

Slowly, their momentum increased.

Within seconds, their speed became so fast that a visible black ring formed around Leon’s body.

Leon raised his hand—and slowly brought it down.

The ring of blades descended with it, shredding and annihilating every flower and vine wrapped around his body.

"Woah..." Enid muttered.

"Master has really gained terrifying control over those blades," Dusk said with a smile.

"Hm... let’s try this."

Leon raised his hand again and twisted it to the right.

Instantly, the blades began whirling around him from every direction at an absurd speed, forming a rotating barrier so dense that even air struggled to pass through.

Every lotus flower that approached was obliterated instantly.

Leon began walking toward Melare.

With every step, his bloodlust grew.

More and more flowers rushed at him—but not a single one broke through the blade shield.

"H-How are you doing that?" Melare asked, panic creeping into his voice.

"I’m closing the distance," Leon replied with a bloodthirsty smirk.

"And since you’re still holding your hand seals, that means your domain restricts your body movements."

Leon removed his mask and stored it inside his inventory.

"So... I am going to kill you now."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 306: BLACK DEATH’S GRAVEYARD
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"D-Don’t come near me..." Melare spoke with a hesitant expression as he looked at Leon.

"Oh, I am coming for you... Better get ready to die," Leon replied with a twisted smirk as he continued walking toward Melare.

’There is something wrong. He is not cancelling his domain. Even though he knows I’m close to killing him... why hasn’t he cancelled it ?’ Leon thought as he kept closing the distance while keeping his guard up.

...And then he felt it.

The aura around Melare suddenly changed—and so did his hand seal.

<DEMON ART : PUDDLE OF DEATH>

The moment the Demon Art activated, the pond beneath Leon’s feet began pulling him down.

The whirling blades of Nyxter stopped instantly as they too became stuck inside the pond.

"What will you do now ?" Melare spoke with a wide smile.

"The more you try to get out, the more it will pull you in. If you use even a shred of mana, it’ll drag you down even faster.

So... take your next step very carefully." He explained while looking at Leon with a grin.

"Haha... my body is continuously using mana, you stupid demon." Leon replied calmly.

"So that’s as fast as this puddle can pull me down." Leon spoke with a smile as he casually walked over the surface of the puddle.

"N-No... way! H-How are you doing that ?!" Melare’s voice trembled as he stared at Leon with a shocked expression.

<Feather Weight x100 stacks>

’The puddle can only suck in things using their own weight and gravity. Since I activated this skill before the fight even began, I already have a hundred stacks.

My weight is equal to air now. It can’t pull me in.’ Leon thought as a smirk spread across his face.

"H-How is this even possible? That shouldn’t be possible, no matter what spell you use, the puddle should pull you down." Melare muttered, his expression twisted with even more shock.

"Haa... I want to kill you right now," Leon said calmly.

"But I know you can’t be killed here. Your regeneration is already high—and once you activate this skill, it increases tenfold.

Killing you inside your domain is nearly impossible."

"How do you even know that?" Melare asked stiffly.

"She told me everything about you guys."

Melare followed his finger.

<Blade of Artimes>

*Swish*

Present time...

CRACKKKKKKKKKKKK

Melare’s head flew cleanly from his shoulders and it didn’t regenerate this time.

The staff was big, it’s lower part was made from a white wood, middle part was made out of a brown wood and on the top it had a black crystal ball.

Inside the Valley,

Valley’s entrance,

Chapter 308: The Blow up
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As Adwin rided forward and reached the middle of the valley, he noticed a tree stump—

Perfectly cut down by magic.

"HALT." Adwin shouted as he raised his fist into the air. Everyone immediately stopped behind him.

The generals also raised their hands and the armies halted all at once.

*Swish*

Dreulis appeared beside the stump and placed his bony hand over it.

"He was here..." he spoke in a low voice.

"Who? The undead you were talking about?" Gris asked, confusion written all over his face.

"No. No. No..." Dreulis shook his head slowly.

"That undead isn’t acting on his own. Someone stronger is with him. Someone who is ordering him around."

He paused.

"I can sense his aura."

The purple middle ring on his finger glowed, releasing an invisible wave of mana across the valley.

"He’s near... but I can’t pinpoint his exact location. It’s like he’s using some kind of—"

"Spell to hide." A calm voice suddenly came from above.

Everyone snapped their heads upward.

Leon was hanging upside down from the branch of a massive tree, his legs wrapped around it casually.

His red eyes staring at Dreulis through the mask.

"Sir, Michael Jackson... is that you?" Adwin asked as he immediately dismounted from his horse.

Leon let go of the branch.

His body began falling—but due to the zero weight surrounding him, he summoned Nyxter and forcefully regained mass.

*Thud*

As soon as he landed, Leon walked straight toward Adwin.

"Hm...?" Dreulis stared at him, the flames in his eyes flickering once again.

’It’s him.’

"Hello... Adwin," Leon said neutrally.

’His aura... it has changed. I can feel his bloodlust. Just like Dredis said.’ Adwin thought as he smiled and raised his hand.

"How are you?" he asked.

Leon shook his hand and met his gaze.

"I’m good. Just blew up half of this valley. Sorry for the damage."

"Yeah, I heard that from Dredis and you don’t have to say sorry, we are both kings. What happened exactly ?" Adwin asked calmly.

"I did a little research after that little meeting of yours," Leon replied, folding his hands behind his back.

"And I found out there aren’t only twelve demons in this forest." He explained and took a small pause.

"I don’t think... I follow, are you saying there are more than twelve." Adwin said, his smile stiffening.

"There’s an entire demon village hidden inside this forest." Leon replied with a neutral expression as he looked at Adwin.

"What !? A whole village ?" Adwin exclaimed.

"Sir Michael Jackson is telling the truth, Your Majesty," Gris said seriously. "We discovered the same thing using advanced magic."

"And you’re telling me this now?" Adwin turned sharply toward him, a pressuring aura leaking from his body. He was clearly angry.

"I—I wasn’t completely sure of the results, Your Majesty. I couldn’t present unconfirmed information," Gris replied hesitantly.

Leon interrupted.

"Here’s a little gift for you."

He opened his inventory and pulled out a severed head.

"Catch." Leon spoke as he threw the head of Melare towards Ruben.

*Catch*

Ruben caught it—then immediately dropped it, terror flooding his face.

*Thud*

"One of the twelve demons ruling that village," Leon replied indifferently.

"His name was Melare. He wasn’t that hard to kill." He explained.

"You killed one of the twelve rulers... alone ?" Dreulis asked, staring at Leon.

"So you’re the undead who was trying to breach the barrier," Leon said, looking at him with a tilted neck.

<Inspect>

[Name: Dreulis]

[Class: Lich]

[Race: Undead]

[Level: 399]

’His level is insane... I can’t even see his mana or HP,’ Leon thought with a serious expression.

<Frenhiz Shield>

Rees casted the spell instantly.

"I call it... The Blow up."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 309: Merlin
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

’Whenever an explosive magic spell is casted, mana is captured into the air as mana pattern. Those patterns can be analysed and found out what kind of spell has been used to cause the explosion.

But... This guy, he has so many passive skills active around his body and yet, I can’t possibly find out what each one of them does.

It’s as if... He is inventing his own spells. I can’t even nullify those spells without knowing their patterns. If I have to say it frankly... He is my natural enemy and an ideal test subject at the same time.’ Dreulis thought as he looked at Leon and thousands of thoughts popped up in his head.

"Don’t think too much about sir, Leon. The more you get to know him the more mysterious he turns out to be." Rees spoke as he walked behind Leon and started chanting.

"What ?" Dreulis asked as he couldn’t understand what Rees meant by that.

"Master... All of them are carrying magical weapons." Fruela informed from above.

"Obviously they are... They are fucking demons and I don’t know how they are still alive, even after that explosion." Adwin spoke as he holded the hilt of his sword tight and continued to stare at the shadows standing in the dust that was settling down slowly.

"Hyper regeneration." Leon replied with a calm smile.

As soon as the dust settled down, a Demon with green coloured skin came in view.

Two vertically rotated pitch black eyes staring directly into Leon’s eyes, his upper clothes were completely torn off by the explosion.

His body was lean and muscular at the same time with vines popping out from every single muscle.

Leon used his skill on him without wasting another second.

<Inspect>

[Name: Merlin]

[Class: Aura Master]

[Race: Demon]

[Level: 137]

[HP: 3000000/3000000]

[MP: 1500000/1500000]

[Title: {Dark Knight} {Last Stander}]

[Description: Merlin is one of most strongest Aura master in the Demon kingdom. He can manipulate mana around him to kill his enemies and once he activates his sacred treasure... Killing him becomes nearly impossible.]

’A demon with two fucking titles... Oh this is going to be fun.’ Leon thought as a smile appeared over his face.

"I take it that you are the one who killed one of my brother Melare ?" Merlin asked as he raised his hand and pointed towards Leon.

"Didn’t the red one tell you already ? I saw him watching the whole show from a distance." Leon replied as he took a step forward.

"Michael... Be careful." Adwin spoke with a serious expression.

"There are so many swords behind you and there are 10 of us here, now that’s not fair is it ?" A black demon spoke from behind with an evil smile on his face.
