Chapter 318: Dark Summoner
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Inside the dimly lit first floor of the dungeon, the air hung heavy with the scent of damp stone and wood.

Leon sat on a big wooden chair with a smirk on his face and a screen floating in front of his face.

"System... I want to change class."

A translucent blue panel shimmered into existence before him.

[Class Change is available!]

[Are you sure you want to change classes?]

[Yes / No]

"Yes," Leon answered without the slightest hesitation.

The moment his confirmation registered, three vivid crimson warning screens exploded into view one after another, each edged in pulsing scarlet light.

[Warning: Changing classes will remove all current class-based skills and reset class progression level to 1. Base HP and MP values will be preserved.]

[Warning: The class change option may be skipped at any time.]

[Warning: Class change can only be performed once per lifetime. Choose with utmost care and all current class related skills will be taken away.]

Leon let out a low whistle, eyebrows lifting as he scanned the aggressively flashing red messages.

"Wow... those are some really big, really angry-looking red screens," he muttered to himself, half-amused.

He read through the warnings again, more slowly this time, letting each word sink in.

"So my level resets to one... but the actual HP and MP I’ve built up stay the same?" A thoughtful gleam entered his eyes. "That means I’ll lose all the level-scaling perks tied to my current class, sure... but honestly? Who cares."

He gave a dry chuckle.

"My so-called ’Alchemist’ class is completely useless to me. I have zero interest in spending my days brewing potions or mixing herbs and I don’t really care about the spells related to this class. So yeah... I’m doing this."

With a decisive flick of his finger, he tapped [Yes].

[Class Change has been initiated...]

[Note: 50 class levels have been forcibly reset!]

[Please select your new class within the time limit: 30 minutes.]

"Time limit?" Leon blinked, the words catching him off guard.

Before he could process it further, a long vertical list unfurled in front of him.

[Swordsman]

[Archer]

[Alchemist]

[Mage]

[Dark Mage]

... [Click for more]

"More...?" he echoed, puzzled. He reached out and tapped the option.

Instantly the screen expanded outward in a dizzying rush, transforming into an enormous, semi-transparent wall of text that stretched far beyond his field of vision. Hundreds—no, thousands—of class names scrolled past in neat columns.

"Fucking hell..." Leon breathed, eyes widening in genuine shock.

Shadow Master.

Mind Flayer.

Necromancer.

Circus Master.

Blood Sovereign.

Abyssal Contractor.

Void Weaver...

These weren’t the safe, beginner-friendly classes handed out at starter villages. These were the real ones. Classes belonging not only to humans, but to every race, every nightmare, every monster this world had ever birthed.

He began scrolling slowly, scanning for names that stood out, drawn to the longer, darker, more evocative titles. For the next twenty-six minutes he read description after description using his fast reading skill, muttering to himself, weighing pros and cons, discarding dozens of tempting options in seconds.

Until only four remained floating prominently in his mind.

[Puppet Master]

[Malice Embodiment]

[Shadow Revenant]

[Dark Summoner]

Each one promised obscene versatility—access to virtually any dark spell tree, just like a high-tier Dark Mage, yet twisted into something far more unique and dangerous.

A sharp chime interrupted his thoughts.

[Warning: Less than 5 minute remaining until class selection window closes.]

[If no choice is made within 4 minutes and 23 seconds, the class change will be canceled and permanently locked with {Alchemist} Class.]

"Fuck—fuck—fuck!" Leon hissed under his breath, pulse spiking.

He stared at the four glowing class panels hovering before him, each accompanied by its neatly formatted description.

[Name: Puppet Master]

[Type: Monster]

[Description: The Puppet Master may summon lifelike human puppets whose appearances can be freely altered to the master’s desire. Their movements and actions are controlled remotely from any distance. Puppets grow stronger alongside their creator and, in turn, amplify the master’s own power.]

[Name: Malice Embodiment]

[Type: Demon]

[Description: An Embodiment of Malice draws strength directly from ambient negative mana, channeling it to reinforce the body and unleash devastating forbidden spells without suffering the usual backlash or penalties. Exceptionally powerful in environments saturated with malevolent energy like negative mana. Severely weakened in areas dominated by neutral or positive mana.]

[Name: Shadow Revenant]

[Type: Undead]

[Description: Shadow Revenant is one of the rare classes that no ordinary undead can achieve. Shadow Revenants are excellent in using dark or shadow related magic. They can control shadows to their will but without any shadow around to help them they become utterly uselss.]

[Name: Dark summoner]

[Type: Unknown]

[Description: Dark summoner is one of the most gruesome and powerful class, as one can use it’s powers to summon beings powerful enough to destroy kingdoms with just a flick of their fingers from the depths of the darkest purgatory.

But, everything comes with a cost.]

"Fucking hell... I am so confused. Which one of them should I pick ?" Leon asked as he placed both of his hands over his head and started thinking deeply.

[Warning: Less than 59 seconds left. Please select the clas or-]

Leon dismissed the warning immediately and closed his eyes as he started thinking carefully.

’Puppet Master seems like a perfect choice as I can summon puppets and make them look like anyone I want. It can help me in the politics but that is not enough.

Malice Embodiment is also a good class... But taking it will be stupidity. Negative mana can only be found in gruesome places and it’s weak without it. So that’s a no go for me.

The creature stood no taller than a child’s knee, its body thin and crooked like something that had grown wrong.

Its skin was a deep crimson, stretched tightly over sharp bones, with faint black veins pulsing beneath the surface as if heat flowed through them.

Two short, jagged horns curled from its forehead, chipped and uneven. Its head was slightly too large for its body, giving it a disturbing, almost goblin-like silhouette.

Its eyes were the most unsettling part—large, glowing yellow with slit pupils, constantly darting around with malicious intelligence. When it blinked, a thin black membrane slid over the eyes like a reptile’s.

A wide mouth split its face, filled with tiny needle-like teeth. When it grinned, which it did often, the smile stretched unnaturally far toward its pointed ears.

From its back sprouted tattered bat-like wings, leathery and dark, barely large enough to lift its body. They twitched constantly, as if the creature could never sit still.

Behind it flicked a thin, spade-tipped tail, curling and uncurling like a snake.

Its fingers were long and clawed, black nails clicking softly whenever it touched the ground.

And despite its small size, the creature carried a faint smell of smoke, sulfur, and burnt flesh, as though it had crawled straight out of a furnace deep in Hell.

Human Kingdom’s Church...

Inside the praying hall, hundreds of families are sitting on large wooden benches, their eyes closed and hands joint in worship, as an elder nun on the front pedestal readed something from an old book.

The environment was very calm and silent, a flowery smell covered the air and sunlight was shining through the church’s glasses.

Those who bring their children to the church tomorrow will be given access to the special feast that we are holding. Thank you." The elder nun spoke as she immediately stepped down from the pedestal.

As soon as the prayers ended everyone calmly started walking out of the prayer room and towards the Church’s exit.

Dinning Hall,

[Please input a name for the skill.]

"Electric Shocks."

[Skill ’Electric Shocks’ has been created.]

[Rank settled to ’D’ by default.]

[State the purpose and parameters of the skill.]

"Converts a continuous stream of mana into electrical current. Output voltage is manually regulated by the user. Sustained use drains mana at a steady rate, the higher the voltage, the greater the consumption. Cannot generate projectile discharge or area-of-effect attacks. Effective range: direct contact only."

[Processing...]

’I’d been meaning to make this one for a while,’ Leon thought, the corner of his mouth pulling upward. ’Funny. I wanted to invent a chocolate milkshake maker first and then build this skill. But I suppose this has its uses too.’

A quiet laugh escaped him... not loud, not theatrical. Just a small sound of genuine amusement.

’What the hell is wrong with him?’ Alren thought, watching Leon murmur to himself and smile at nothing. ’Why is he laughing like that ?’

[Description accepted.]

[Please select the activation chant for skill {Electric Shocks}.]

"Electro."

[Chant ’Electro’ selected.]

[Estimated mana cost: 50 mana per 1 volt of electrical output.]

[Cooldown: 5 minutes after skill deactivation.]

[Would you like to learn the skill or destroy it?]

"Learn it."

[Congratulations. Skill {Electric Shocks} has been learned.]

---

Leon stood, rolled his neck once, and walked toward Alren without hurry. He crouched in front of him and extended one finger, his right index and rested it gently against the nail of Alren’s pinky.

"...I think you’ve got the wrong idea about me," Alren said, glancing down at the contact with a mocking smile. "I’m not really into guys."

Leon didn’t acknowledge it. "Electro. One hundred volts."

<−5,000 mana>

*Trrr—*

*CRACK.*

"WHAT THE FUCK—"

The current punched through Alren’s finger like a hot needle driven straight into the bone. His entire body lurched shoulders, spine, legs all seizing at once in a single convulsive jerk, the restraints rattling hard against whatever held him.

In the corner, Fruela went very still.

"...Oh." Leon tilted his head slightly, studying his reaction."Nice."

Alren’s breath came out ragged. The smile was gone. "What did you just do?"

"Nothing, yet." Leon’s tone was almost conversational. "Do you know there are exposed nerve endings directly beneath a human fingernail? Completely unprotected. The nail exists, in part, to shield them." He paused. "Yours is about to stop doing that."

He separated one of Nyxter’s blades — smooth, practiced and pressed the edge beneath the edge of Alren’s nail.

"W—what are you—"

*Stab.*

"AHHHHHHHHH..."

The blade carved sideways in one precise stroke, splitting the nail away from the nailbed in a single motion. The sound it made was wet and small and somehow worse for it.

"Oh god." Fruela turned away, one hand pressed hard over her mouth.

*Tick.*

Leon pinched the separated nail between two fingers and peeled it back in a single fluid pull — not slow, not fast. Deliberate.

What remained was raw, glistening, weeping. The nerve endings beneath sat fully exposed to open air for the first time in Alren’s life covered in red blood, every micro-current of atmosphere against them registering as wrongness his brain had no framework to process.

"There." Leon held the nail up briefly, as though presenting evidence. "Those red fibers. Those are yours."

He placed his finger back against the exposed wound. The contact alone made Alren hiss through clenched teeth.

"Electro. One hundred and forty volts."

"You sick fu—"

*TRRRRRRRRRR.*

"AHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH—"

His whole arm thrashed uselessly against its restraint. Veins stood out along his neck. His teeth cracked together hard enough that a back molar chipped.

The current ran directly into the exposed nerve cluster and stayed there, every millisecond of it a white-hot signal screaming straight up into his brain with nothing to interrupt it.

Leon released the skill.

The silence afterward felt physical.

"Hehehe." The sound came quietly, almost to himself. "Now that is a proper scream." He looked at Alren with something that might have been appreciation. "I hope you’re enjoying this as much as I am."

Alren’s head hung. His chest heaved. When he finally lifted his eyes, they were wet, not from crying, his body had simply reacted on its own and burning with pure, unfiltered hatred.

"You’re a fucking monster."

"You’re tougher than I expected." Leon said it like a compliment. He stood, rolled his shoulders, and placed both hands flat against either side of Alren’s head, palms over his temples, fingers pressed firm against bone.

Alren’s eyes widened. Something in his senses recognized what was about to happen before his mind could catch up.

"W-What are you—"

"Electro. Two hundred and ten volts." A pause. "Charge."

*TRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRR.*

"AGGGGGGGGGHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH..."

The scream that came out wasn’t a scream anymore. It was something beyond language a sound Alren’s body produced entirely without his permission, torn from the chest and the throat and somewhere deeper than both. It traveled down the corridor and kept going, swallowed by the dungeon’s stone throat, and still it sounded loud.

His jaw locked. His eyes rolled. Every muscle in his face contracted simultaneously brow, cheek, jaw pulling his features into a rigid, grimacing mask he had no control over. The current arced between his temples through the soft tissue of his skull, lighting up nerve pathways that had no business being lit, sending signals cascading through his motor cortex like a circuit board catching fire.

His bound hands tore at the restraints with the full, panicked strength of a body that wanted desperately to survive.

Leon released him.

Alren slumped. His breath came in shallow, shaking pulls. A thin line of saliva ran down his chin.

"Phew." Leon stepped back and looked him over with mild satisfaction. "That was a decent preview." He settled back into the throne.

"We’re just getting started. So don’t you dare break."

---

*Swish. Swish.*

Two figures dropped silently into the room — Rees and Dreulis, materialized from nothing, drawn by the screaming sound.

Dreulis’s gaze went immediately to Alren. Then to Leon.

"I heard screaming."

"Small interrogation session." Leon waved a hand dismissively. "Nothing to concern yourself with."

Dreulis studied the ruined finger, the burn marks beginning to form at Alren’s temples, the glassy vacancy in his eyes. "...Isn’t he one of the Royal Castle’s personal guards?"

"He is."

"Are you extracting information about the King? The castle?"

"No, I want to know who he actually answers to." Leon’s voice cooled slightly. "Now be quiet and let me finish."

He rose again. Walked behind Alren’s chair. Placed one open hand flat against the back of his neck, fingers spread, palm against the cervical vertebrae.

Alren felt it. The warmth of the hand. The waiting.

"N-No— please— I don’t—I haven’t—"

"Electro." A quiet smile appeared over Leon’s face. "Three hundred and twenty volts. Charge."

*TRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRR.*

The current hit the cervical nerve cluster like a hammer.

For one full second, every muscle in Alren’s body locked simultaneously... jaw, neck, arms, chest, legs... a total systemic seizure, his body rigid as stone in the chair, every tendon pulled to its limit.

Then the shaking began.

His head snapped forward and back. His spine curved and straightened in rapid, violent spasms. His legs drummed against the floor.

*thud thud thud thud*.

The heels striking stone in a fast, helpless rhythm his body generated entirely on its own. The sound of it was somehow the worst part.

No scream came. His throat had locked shut with everything else. The only sound from him was a pressurized, high-pitched wheeze forcing itself out through a jaw clamped too tight to open.

White foam gathered at the corner of his mouth, crawling down his chin.

Leon removed his hand.

The shaking stopped. Alren’s chin dropped to his chest. The foam dripped.

"...Did I kill him?" Leon walked around to face him, genuinely curious, and slapped him once across the face, sharp and hard.

*SLAP*

Alren’s head snapped to the side.

Then—

"Haaaahhh—" A massive, drowning inhale, like a man surfacing from deep water. His eyes flew open, wild and unfocused, pupils blown wide.

When they finally found Leon’s face, every trace of defiance in them was gone.

What replaced it was something much more honest.

"N-No—" His voice shook so badly the words fractured apart. "Stay away from me— please— I’ll tell you— I’ll tell you everything—" The words tumbled out over each other.

"I work for the King’s third wife— Madam Luna R. Stark— I collect information and pass it to her, that’s all, I swear, I swear on everything, I don’t know anything else, please— please don’t—"

Leon straightened up. Let out a slow breath through his nose.

"Luna R. Stark." Dreulis said the name quietly, turning it over. "I never trusted that woman to begin with." A brief pause, then a small shrug, as though filing it away.

"Can you show me that spell again?" He asked, stepping closer, his eyes fixed on Leon’s hand with undisguised interest.

"Of course." Leon turned back toward Alren with a smile.

"No—" Alren’s voice cracked. "No, I told you everything— I told you everything—"

Leon’s hand closed over his mouth. Gentle. Unhurried. His fingers spread across Alren’s jaw and lower face, muffling the words completely.

He leaned down until his eyes were level with Alren’s.

"You did tell me everything," he said quietly. "Now... You are not use to me and as I said, I would make you regret to say those words."

He stood upright.

"Electro. Ten thousand volts."

*TRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRR*

What happened next didn’t look like pain. It didn’t look like anything human.

Alren’s body ignited from the inside.

For two full seconds he was luminescent — a deep, pulsing blue light bleeding through the skin of his chest, his throat, the thin flesh of his closed eyelids, the webbing between his fingers, as though something vast and electrical had replaced his blood and was burning its way outward.

Then his body stopped shaking.

The light died.

A thin column of black smoke drifted upward from between his lips.

Leon released him and stepped back.

Where Alren’s eyes had been, two dark, deflated things sat sunken in their sockets, burst by the pressure from within. A wide, branching pattern of black, like leafless winter trees, spread from the corners of his jaw outward across both cheeks, scorched deep into the skin.

The smoke kept rising, unhurried, dissolving into the ceiling and eerie silence covered the room.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 330: Just a tiny thing
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

*THUD*

Leon let go of Alren’s dead body and sat down on the throne with a neutral expression on his face.

"Well... Can I take his body for some research purposes?" Dreulis asked as he looked at Leon.

"Okay." Leon replied as he turned to look at Dreulis.

"Thank you..." Dreulis spoke as he immediately unstrapped his hands from the stone chair and disappeared with the corpse.

*Swish*

"Master... what should we do about the king’s wife? She knows where we all live." Rees spoke in a neutral tone as he looked at Leon.

"Should I go and kill her, Master?" Fruela asked with a serious expression.

"She is a queen, not some stupid girl walking on the streets, it’s not easy to kill her or even touch her until she is inside that castle. Fruela... You will go and sit outside the castle and keep an eye on her for the next few days.

If she gets out, follow her and silently observe. Don’t take any actions. Report back to me if you see anything unusual." Leon commanded.

"Yes, Master." Fruela spoke as she immediately disappeared from the room.

"Rees, I need you to read this and tell me if you find anything interesting." Leon spoke as he pulled out Dreulis’ grimoire and handed it over to Rees.

"As you command, Master." Rees spoke as he took the grimoire and disappeared instantly.

"I need to get stronger." Leon spoke in a low voice as he stood up from the chair and picked up the Nyxter into his hands.

’I have to summon more creatures from hell and for that, I need corpses and Mana cores. Although I have more than enough money to buy mana cores from the Adventurer’s Guild, but I doubt they will have anything above C-rank and if I bought so many cores at once, I will attract a whole lot of attention.’ Leon thought with a neutral expression as he disappeared from the floor.

...

Fourth Floor,

Dusk is sleeping peacefully on the floor without any worries.

*Swish*

*Clap-Clap*

"Dusk, wake up." Leon spoke as he clapped his hands together.

"Hmm...? Master? Are we getting invaded?" Dusk asked as he immediately woke up and started looking around.

"No, we are going on a hunt. Let’s go." He replied with a neutral expression.

"Hunt? As in monster hunt, Master?" Dusk asked with a confused expression.

"Yes... We are going deep into the forest so the returning duration can be long. So be prepared." Leon spoke with a neutral expression.

"Always prepared, Master." Dusk replied as he walked up to Leon.

"Good." Leon replied as he placed his hand over Dusk’s head and both of them disappeared immediately.

...

Human Kingdom’s Church,

A couple is standing inside the church and they are talking to Sister Miola.

The man is in his 30’s and the wife looks a bit young, in her 20’s, but her face is paled out and there is an apron tied to her head.

Both of them are wearing decent clothes, similiar to what a four star noble family would wear.

"Sister... She is getting worse day by day, no matter how much holy water I give her. Almost all of her hairs have fallen off and I can’t see her like this. Please let us meet Mother Carrie." Her husband replied with a worried and sad expression.

"I can understand your worries but Mother Carrie is busy in tomorrow’s preparations. Please come back tomorrow. I will make sure that once the festival ends, she will see both of you immediately.

Until then... Here, have some more holy water and wear this." Miola spoke as she took off her holy necklace and gave it to the sick woman.

"T-Thank you... *Cough-Cough-Cough* Thank you, Sister." She replied as she forced a smile on her face.

"We will take our leave now, Sister." Both of them spoke as they turned around and left the church at once.

Miola turned around and walked away as well with a sad expression.

...

Outside the church, the couple is walking back to their house.

"Darling, I will be alright. You don’t have to worry about me too much. I will get good once we meet Sister Carrie." The wife spoke with a smile on her face.

"Y-Yeah..." Her husband replied with a forced smile but he wasn’t happy at all.

"You won’t get good at all. Not from that condition." A voice came from behind.

"Hmm...?" Both of them turned around and noticed a nun standing behind them. She was small and had blonde hair.

This nun was none other than Terisa.

"W-Who are you, sister ?" The husband asked with a confused expression.

"Who even cares about me ? You should care about what I have on me." She replied with a smile.

"What?" Both of them asked with confused expressions.

"I have the cure for your wife’s condition." She replied.

"What!?" The husband asked with a serious expression as his eyes widened in shock.

"A-Are you serious, Sister?" He asked.

"I am pretty sure that once she consumes this... She will get back to normal immediately." Terisa spoke as she pulled out a black pill out of her pocket.

"T-Then... Please give it to us, Sister." The husband replied as he started walking towards the pill.

"We have to talk in private first." Terisa spoke as she immediately retrieved the pill.

"Y-Yes... Please come with us. Our house is not far from here." The wife spoke with a neutral expression.

...

Inside the house’s dining room,

"What are your names? And how many children do you have ?" Terisa asked as she pointed towards the man.

"I am Tinter Durden and this is my wife, Lisa Durden. As you can see we are just a small four star noble household.

I don’t have any children due to my wife’s condition, but I love her more than myself and I want to save her at any cost, Sister." Tinter replied instantly.

"Good... Now I have this pill that can heal your wife but I am not giving it away for free. You understand?" Terisa asked with a neutral expression.

"B-But... Aren’t you a nun? And we give big donations to the church every month. I-I just donated like 3 silver coins." Tinter asked, confused.

"Okay... What are you willing to do for your wife? My mistake... Dying wife?" Terisa asked with a smile.

"Anything... I will pay you with everything I have. Just give me the pill, Sister." He spoke with a serious expression.

"I don’t want gold or silver coins. I just want a tiny thing. I want you to change your house, your kingdom and your church." Terisa replied with a smile.

"W-What!?" Both of them asked with shocked and confused expressions.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 331: Take good care of him
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"We don’t understand, Sister... Don’t you work for—"

"Listen to me very carefully. I will put this in front of you one last time... Your wife is affected with huge amounts of negative mana and she is on the last stage of mana illness. If you don’t give her this pill in the next two hours, she will go into a deep sleep state that we all know as death.

Now, I can just get out of your face and you can take your chance with Mother Carrie, but if you really want to save her, change your church and embrace the real god." Terisa spoke with a serious expression.

"R-Real god?" Lisa asked with a confused expression.

"Yes, yes... The god that will help you instead of showing you the way to salvation. He will not lie to you like Mother Carrie, that you will have a great life if you continue to believe in him and just pray.

You are drinking that holy water for so many days. Do you feel even a little bit better?" She asked with a neutral expression.

"N-No... It’s gotten worse since they gave her the holy water." Tinter replied with a worried expression.

"Your god is dead. Our god is the only one that can save you from your ultimate doom. Here, have a taste of true divinity." Terisa spoke as she threw the pill towards Lisa.

Both of them looked at the pill that was rolling on the table in front of them.

Their faces had a very serious look as both of them stared at the pill.

Lisa slowly raised her hand and grabbed the pill in between her fingers.

"Lisa..." Tinter spoke with a worried expression.

Lisa slowly grabbed Tinter’s hand and ate the black pill.

As soon as she ate it... She felt something inside her body, an energy that was taking shape.

"I-I don’t feel—"

*Thud*

Suddenly she fell down from her chair.

"S-Sister... What is happening to her?" Tinter asked with a worried expression as he looked at Terisa.

"Wait for it." Terisa replied with a smile as she continued to observe Lisa.

*Crack-Crack-Crack-Crack*

"It hurtsssssssss..." Lisa shouted as her bones started breaking and blood started coming out of her mouth.

"Oh my god... What have you given her? I have to take her to a healer as soon as poss—"

"Shut the fuck up and watch. She is being re-born." Terisa shouted as she looked at Tinter.

"Haaaaah.... Helppppppp." Lisa shouted in pain as her bones shifted under her skin.

"I-I am sorry... I can’t see her like this. Please stop this right—" Before Tinter could’ve completed his sentence, Terisa struck him under his neck and he went unconscious.

*THUD*

...

1 hour later,

*Blink-Blink*

"Darling... Can you hear me? Darling?"

"Hmm...?" Tinter opened his eyes with a confused expression and noticed a beautiful woman’s face in front of him. She had long black hair, fair skin tone and a pair of beautiful blue eyes.

"What? Is that you, Lisa? Am I dead as well?" He asked with a confused expression.

"No, darling... We are both alive. Sister Terisa gave me the pill and now I am completely healed. I am better than before." Lisa spoke with a smile as she looked at Tinter.

"What? But you were crying and your bones—"

"You have to get through the pain in order to gain beauty, health and wealth."

As soon as Tinter turned his head towards the voice, he noticed that Terisa was sitting on the dining table and she is drinking tea elegantly.

"Sister..." Tinter spoke as he immediately stood up from the floor and went on his knees in front of her.

"Please forgive me for not trusting... I will do everything you say. I will believe in whatever god you want me to. Thank you for saving my wife, I will never forget this favour." Tinter spoke as he bowed down his head to the ground.

"Good... You will leave tonight for the Vergas Kingdom. Only take what’s important and abandon everything else.

Here, have these... They will take you in once you show them this." Terisa spoke as she pulled out two black pendants from her pocket and passed them to Tinter.

"Yes."

"Good... If someone comes finding you from the church, you will not make any conversation with them. Now I will take my leave. Where was the exit door again ?" Terisa spoke as she immediately stood up from the table.

"Please allow me to show you the way." Lisa spoke as she immediately started walking forward and Terisa started following her.

*Click*

"Thank you for everything once again, Sister." Lisa spoke as she opened the door for her.

Terisa stepped out of the house and looked at Lisa with a smile.

But as soon as Lisa smiled back, her eyes turned completely black. It was as if there was something evil inside her, that controlled her body.

"Take good care of him." Terisa spoke.

"Yes, Sister." Lisa replied as she closed the door with a creepy smile.

...

Terisa started walking back to the church with a smile on her face.

She slowly pulled out a necklace from her pocket. It was the same holy necklace that Miola gave to Lisa.

"Stupid." Terisa spoke as she crushed the necklace immediately and threw it away.

*Crack-Crack*

*Thud*

"You look more stupid to me, if you ask."

Terisa’s steps halted almost immediately as soon as she heard the familiar voice.

"What are you doing here?" Terisa asked without turning around.

"Don’t worry, you can turn around. I am in a disguise."

Terisa immediately turned around and noticed a woman in her thirties standing behind her, holding a basket of fruits.

"Hello, Sister... I have this for the church." The woman spoke as she handed over the fruit basket to Terisa.

"What do you want, Sister Yuli?" Terisa asked in a low voice while maintaining a smile on her face.

"Mother wants the name of the guy who killed Merlin and everything you can find out about him in two days or you will be called back. See you around, Sister." Yuli spoke in a low voice as she immediately turned around and left.

"Bitch." Terisa spoke while maintaining her smile.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 332: World Tree’s Grace
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As the sun touched down on the horizon, the clouds shifted their colour to a deep orange and red.

*Swish. Swish. Swish.*

Over the dense canopy of the visceral forest, a small red imp glided silently above the towering trees.

*Shh.*

"Hmm...?"

The imp’s eyes narrowed. Something had moved in the distance.

Without wasting another second, he shot towards it.

Below, a monster prowled through the undergrowth, lizard-like in shape, yet several times larger than any ordinary one. Long claws scraped against the earth. Three thick tails swept behind it. Thousands of old scars carved across its body like a map of past battles. But now, It was searching for food.

"Okay."

The imp touched the iron ring on his finger. In an instant, the skin of both his hands transformed — hardening, darkening, shifting into solid iron.

*Tang. Tang. Tang. Tang.*

He slammed his hands together, producing a sharp, ringing sound that cut through the forest air.

"Hissss...?"

The monster froze. Confused, it snapped its head upward, searching for the source of the sound.

But the moment it looked up—

*Swish. Swish. Swish.*

Three dark blades emerged from three different directions simultaneously, slicing clean through its body — head, tail, and torso.

*Thud. Thud. Thud.*

"...That was clean."

Leon stepped out of the bushes, his expression calm and indifferent.

[Congratulations! You have Levelled Up.]

"Master." Dusk deactivated his invisibility, materialising silently beside the monster’s torso. "This Vadizard has a mana core. Look."

*Bite.*

He tore open the torso without hesitation and pulled out a small, green glowing rock from within.

"Let’s see..."

<Inspect>

[Name: Vadizard’s Mana Core]

[Rank: D]

[Description: A Vadizard is a very fragile and agile creature. They can alter their body colour to blend seamlessly into their surroundings. It is nearly impossible to capture one alive for taming.]

"Another D-ranked monster..." Leon muttered, his brow furrowing slightly as he glanced toward Dusk. "How deep are we inside the forest?"

"I have no idea, Master. But I believe we are fairly deep — the fact that we are crossing paths with a Vadizard is proof enough for that. They are natural prey to my kind." Dusk replied, his expression neutral.

"We need to go even deeper." Leon turned his gaze forward. "Imp, continue searching for monsters."

"Yes, Master."

The imp’s hands reverted to normal. He rose back into the air and resumed his search.

Leon stared quietly at his inventory as he dropped the D-ranked core inside.

---

— Inventory —

73x F-Rank Mana Cores

52x E-Rank Mana Cores

25x D-Rank Mana Cores

500x Monster Body Parts

---

’One hundred and fifty mana cores in total, most of them F-rank. Around two hundred corpses cut down into parts. And I’ve levelled up to Level 20 in just one hour.

The progress is decent... but not good enough. I need to find higher-ranked monsters and get more experience points.’

Leon closed his inventory, his expression unreadable.

"Master... look."

Dusk walked up to a nearby tree and began dragging his paws slowly across its bark.

"What is it?" Leon asked, glancing over.

"A Worldroot Tree, Master."

Leon’s eyes snapped toward it. "What?"

Without wasting another second, Leon used his skill on it.

<Inspect>

[Name: Worldroot Tree]

[Race: Tree]

[Rank: S]

[Description: A mysterious tree that continuously wanders throughout forests. Cannot be spotted or identified by humans, elves, or demons.]

"I’ve seen one of these before..." Leon murmured, studying the tree. "Last time I crossed this forest with that slime, we passed through one just like this. But how did you even notice it? It looks completely identical to every other tree here."

"It’s difficult to explain, Master. It’s more like... a feeling." Dusk said. "When we draw close to one, we can sense its presence."

"I see." Leon folded his arms. "So where does this one lead?"

"That’s what I’m trying to determine, but..." Dusk’s paws stilled against the bark. "...something is wrong. The tree’s other side isn’t speaking to me properly. It’s as though something is preventing it."

"What’s preventing it?"

"I can’t quite tell. It’s communicating, but the words are... broken. Fragmented." Dusk paused, tilting his head. "It keeps saying — ’rapped in side geon.’ I don’t understand what a side geon is."

Leon stared at him flatly. "...Is it perhaps saying trapped inside a dungeon?"

"Oh." Dusk turned back to the tree and resumed scratching the trunk. "Are you trapped inside a dungeon?"

A short silence passed.

"It says yes, Master." Dusk replied.

Leon’s eyes narrowed. "Then why can’t it simply teleport itself out?"

"Under normal circumstances, no dungeon would be capable of confining a Worldroot Tree — they are far too powerful for that." Dusk’s tone grew heavier. "But this one is trapped. Which means the dungeon holding it, this is anything but ordinary."

He turned to face Leon directly.

"To restrain a Worldroot Tree, a dungeon must be at minimum Realm-Hold class — or as humans classify it, SS-rank."

Leon went still.

"...Did you say SS-rank?"

"Yes, Master." Dusk nodded. "These dungeons are capable of containing entire realms within themselves and can persist for thousands of centuries. When they overflow with mana, a bridge forms between the dungeon and a world such as this one. That is when the monsters sealed inside begin to cross over."

Leon was quiet for a moment. Then—

"Can we enter it?"

"Master." Dusk’s voice dropped. The usual neutrality in his expression was gone, replaced by something rare — genuine unease. "We should not go anywhere near a Realm-Hold dungeon. It would be certain death. Every monster within is sentient. Every single one of them is far stronger than either of us."

He paused, his eyes distant.

"I entered one once... out of pure curiosity. It took me three years just to escape. Every single day inside was a fight for my life."

"I see." Leon replied simply.

[Quest Found]

[Quest: World Tree’s Grace]

[Rank: S]

[Description: Enter the SS-rank dungeon and free the Worldroot Tree from within. Successfully rescue the tree and escape the dungeon — and the Worldroot Tree will grant you a reward befitting your efforts.]

[Reward: Unknown]

[Would you like to accept the quest?][Yes/No ?]

Leon stared at the notification for a moment.

Then, slowly, a small smile crossed his face.

"Hey, Dusk."

"Yes, Master?"

"Ask the tree to let us in."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 333: Hey look it’s a kingdom
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"U-Umm... What Master ?" Dusk asked with a confused expression.

"Ask the tree to let us into the dungeon so we can free it’s other side." Leon replied with a smile.

"Master... The dungeon is-" Before dusk could’ve completed his sentence, his paw sank into the tree’s trunk.

"Ah look... The tree opened up the path, let’s go." Leon spoke as he immediately jumped inside the tree’s trunk without wasting another second.

"Fuck." Dusk spoke as he also jumped inside the tree following Leon.

"Wooooohooooooooooo...." Leon shouted in joy as he slided down the tree’s trunk as if it’s some kind of amusement park ride.

Dusk followed behind him with a neutral expression on his face.

*Thud-Thud*

Both of them came out from the other side and a bright light shined on their eyes.

"What the fuck ?" Leon asked as he slowly opened his eyes and noticed that he was sitting on an open grassland.

The sun was shining brightly in the sky and winds were blowing naturally.

"Is this really a dungeon ?" Leon asked as he stood up and turned around only to notice the Worldroot tree.

It was the only tree on the whole grassland.

"It’s a realm inside the dungeon master... I think this is a resting area of some kind. These areas are common in these dungeons." Dusk explained.

’Wait... Wait... Wait... Maybe I can help you. I have a healing spell that can probably heal you.’ Leon replied instantly as a plan formed in his mind.

’Thou jests... There is no possible way that small vessel of thine holds mana enough to mend mine wounds, I, Lesrath the giant. Thou shouldst flee before mine end comes... For once I perish, this chamber shall no longer be fit for the living. Kumigano shall seize dominance and fill every corner of this place with his vile poison.’ Lesrath replied in a neutral tone.

’Trying doesn’t cost anything... If I were to be successful, you will be fully healed. If I were to fail, you are dying already.’ Leon replied with a neutral expression.

’Aye... thou speakest true. But what reason hast thou to help one such as I? What dost thou seek in return?’ Lesrath asked in a confused tone.

’How about we discuss that later... That Kumigano thing is waking up once again.’ Leon spoke as he pointed towards Kumigano.

Kumigano was coming back to it’s senses as it stood up from the ground and shaked it’s head furiously.

’Speak then... what wouldst thou have of me?’ Lesrath asked.

’Your hand extended it over here so I can touch it.’ Leon replied with a neutral expression.

Without speaking any further, Lesrath... Raised his hand and placed it near Leon.

Without wasting another second, Leon placed both of his hands over Lesrath’s diamond knuckles and used his skill.

"Heal."

<Minor Heal>

[WARNING: HOST HAS USED THE SPELL ON A VERY STRONG ALLY. HUGR AMOUNTS OF MANA EILL BE CONSUMED IN ORDER TO HEAL THE ALLY.]

[WOULD YOU LIKE TO CONTINUE ?][YES/NO ?]

"Yes." As soon as Leon selected yes, he felt all the mana in his body getting sucked out instantly and regenerating at the same time.

Very severe headaches started to occur in Leon’s head as his vision started going blank.

’N-No... Just a little more. If I can heal him. I will be able to achieve something much more bigger... This pain is nothing, Leon.’ He thought as his legs started shaking but his will wasn’t shaking even a bit.

*SHHHHHHHHHH*

Suddenly, Lesrath’s whole body started to glow brightly green and all of his wounds started closing. Even though the process was slow but they were closing at an incredible speed.

’Thou hast surprised me... thou art far stronger than thine appearance wouldst suggest. I, Lesrath, shall never forget thy kindness.’ Lesarath replied as he noticed that Kumigano has started walking towards him once again.

"KREEEEKKKKKKKKKK..." Kumigano shouted as if it had sensed that Lesarath was being healed.

*THUD-THUD-THUD-THUD-THUD*

Kumigano’s tail started glowing brightly purple as it started running towards Lesarath with full speed.

"Haah..." Leon took a long breath as he finally lost his consciousness from the severe headaches.

*THUD*

*SHHHHH*

Lesarath stood up from the ground as he was completely healed and started rubbing his diamond knuckles together.

"KREEEEEEKKKKKKKKKKK..." Kumigano stopped exactly 200 meters away from Lesarath and swinged it’s glowing tail towards Lesrath’s under abdomen.

"BHAAAAAAAANNNNNNNN...." Lesarath shouted as he immediately tightened his first and this time instead of blocking he threw a punch towards the Kumigano’s tail with full force.

*BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM*

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 339: Thirteen Damned Souls
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Leon touched Kumigano’s body and used his spell.

<Summons Of Darkness>

The moment the spell left his fingertips, Kumigano’s entire body dissolved from the floor as if it had never existed, swallowed whole by the darkness pooling beneath Leon’s feet.

*Swish*

Then the ground split.

A single black line tore open across the stone floor directly in front of him, wide and deliberate, as though something on the other side had dragged a blade through the fabric of the world itself.

*Crack — Crack — Crack — Crack*

The line fractured outward into jagged cracks that spiderwebbed across the cold floor. From deep within those fractures, a red light began seeping upward, not the warm red of fire, but something angrier, like the glow of something that had been burning for centuries and had long since forgotten how to stop.

The air around Leon thickened even more. A low, resonant hum vibrated through the soles of his boots, as if the stone itself was groaning under the weight of whatever was pressing against the other side.

The cracks widened.

Leon crouched slightly and looked down into the opening.

The pit beneath had no visible bottom. Only depth, an endless, churning dark that writhed with pale shapes. Thousands of damned souls pressed against one another in a suffocating mass, their translucent forms twisting and weeping, their cries reduced to a faint, distant choir that floated upward like smoke.

They were straining, clawing at something, pulling back against something, their hollow fingers wrapped around a silhouette that moved steadily, calmly, upward through them regardless.

Something was climbing.

It moved without urgency. Each pull upward was slow and deliberate, like a man ascending a ladder he had climbed a thousand times before. The damned souls around it wailed louder as it rose, their efforts to restrain it growing more frantic the closer it came to the surface.

*Thud.*

One hand, large, dark, and completely featureless, pressed flat against the edge of the cracked floor.

Then it stepped out.

Leon’s eyes went wide.

It stood upright. Humanoid in shape — two arms, two legs, a torso, a head — the proportions were correct, even ordinary at a glance. But there was no face. No hair. No skin. No texture of any kind.

Where a man’s features should have been there was only a smooth, seamless void — a silhouette carved from pure absence, wearing the shape of a person the way a coat rack wears a jacket.

The ambient light of the room bent slightly around it, as though the air nearest to the creature refused to illuminate it. Even the faint red glow rising from the cracks in the floor seemed to stop at the edge of its form rather than touch it.

It was less a creature and more the idea of one — a shadow that had learned to stand.

"What the fuck are you?" Leon asked, and without waiting for an answer, he used his skill.

<Inspect>

---

[Name: Malebolge (Thirteen Damned Souls)]

[Race: Spirit]

[Class: ??]

[HP: 800,000 / 800,000]

[MP: 400,000 / 400,000]

[Description: Summoned from the deepest abyssal pits, Malebolge is a volatile, walking oubliette containing thirteen damned Arch-Sinners who constantly tear and reconstruct their shared flesh to seize control.

Cloaked in deep, chiaroscuro shadows, this nightmare of shifting anatomy absorbs ambient light, emitting only a faint, low-light crimson glow from the cracks in its rusted, bone-plated armor. As the thirteen distinct, megalomaniacal wills violently cycle through the host body, wielding powers ranging from nerve-flaying strikes to soul-burning hellfire — the entity continually adapts its terrifying, hyper-realistic form to obliterate its enemies, all while quietly hungering for their sanity.]

---

"Malebolge?" Leon repeated, tilting his head slightly, the name sitting oddly on his tongue.

*Swish — Swish.*

The void rippled. Like ink dropped into still water, the featureless darkness of the creature’s form began to churn and reshape — flesh and stitching materializing from nothing, pulling itself together with a wet, rustling sound until a human male stood in its place.

He was broad shouldered and pale — deathly pale — and covered from his jawline down to his ankles in thick, black stitches that crossed his skin in uneven, overlapping lines, like a body that had been taken apart and reassembled by someone working too quickly in the dark. His eyes were colorless. His smile was not.

"Ah..." he said, his voice low and unhurried, carrying the ease of a man who had never once been surprised by anything. "So you are the one who summoned us."

"I did," Leon replied, expression flat, eyes already cataloguing every detail of the man in front of him.

"The name is Maverick the Third." The stitched man rolled his neck slowly, the sound of his joints cracking filling the stone room. "You can also call me the Butcher. I like chopping people, demons, monsters, anything built from flesh... into very, very small pieces of meat." As he finished speaking, his fingernails lengthened with a soft *schink*, stretching into long, curving blades.

He turned them over in front of his face with visible appreciation.

"So you are a butcher," Leon said. Same flat voice. Same flat expression.

"That’s right." Maverick grinned wider, and the stitches along his cheeks pulled taut with the expression. "Used to skin souls in hell. People, when I was still alive. Hehehe. Just give me the order and I’ll chop anything for you — since you’re my master now."

"Hey." A different voice. Sharper, feminine, cutting through Maverick’s sentence like a blade. "Change places. Now."

The stitched body dissolved.

*Swish — Swish.*

The void returned for a half-second — that same lightless, featureless silhouette — and then it rebuilt itself again, smoother this time, deliberate. A woman took shape.

She was tall. Her skin was a deep oceanic blue, and fine scales traced the lines of her cheekbones and the backs of her hands like natural ornamentation — the kind that looked beautiful and dangerous in equal measure. Her posture was impeccable. When she looked at Leon, her eyes didn’t blink.

Without a word, she stepped forward and lowered herself into a bow so composed and unhurried it bordered on theatrical.

*Bow.*

"My name is Lady Marla." Her voice was different from Maverick’s — smooth, measured, with a subtle resonance underneath it that made the air feel slightly heavier. "I am a siren master. I can sing songs so hypnotizing that the weak-minded cannot help but obey them. I can also reach inside your opponents and play with what they feel — fear, despair, obsession — as I see fit." She straightened, and a slow, deliberate smile crossed her scaled face as she held Leon’s gaze. "It is a pleasure to serve, master."

"Huh." Leon looked at her for a moment, then let his eyes drift back to the void form that briefly flickered at the edges of her silhouette. "Thirteen psychotic souls possessing a body that shape-shifts between them." A small smile pulled at the corner of his mouth. "Very, very interesting. I hope you are worth a ss ranked monster’s body and mana core."

*Swish — Swish.*

Marla dissolved.

The form that replaced her was slimmer than the others — narrow-shouldered and strange, with elongated legs that bent slightly backward at the knee like a rabbit’s hind limbs, the feet ending in thick, clawed pads pressed flat against the cold stone floor. Where a face should have been, there was only smooth, unbroken skin — no eyes, no nose, just a faint ridge where a brow line might have sat.

It was still. Completely, unnervingly still — the kind of stillness that made made the enviornment seem a little eerie.

"Master." Its voice was quiet, precise, and carried no inflection of personality. "My name is RXV-666. I am a chimera — constructed by Dr. Hofelworth. I have no eyes." A brief pause, as though allowing that fact to settle. "But I can locate any enemy, their position, their movement, their distance, with perfect accuracy, regardless of proximity or concealment. I can kill on your command. I can die on your command."

It bowed.

’Chimeras have souls?’ Leon thought, watching the eyeless face tilt downward. ’I always figured they were just body parts, different monsters stitched together to make something bigger and worse. Apparently not.’ He held the thought for a moment, then let it go with the casual ease of a man filing something away for later. ’Oh well. Whatever.’

He reached down without looking and picked up a small obsidian stone from the cracked floor.

*Throw*

Leon then tossed it into the air.

"Attack it..." He spoke while looking at RVX-666.

"Yes, Master." He spoke as he immediately disappeared from his position and re-appeared in the air directly above Leon.

*SWISH-SWISH-SWISH-SWISH-SWISH-SWISH-SWISH-SWISH-SWISH*

Before Leon could’ve looked up, RVX-666 disappeared and re-appeared on his original position, where he was standing from the start.

But as soon as Leon looked up he noticed that the obsidian stone has disappeared from the air and only black dust remains.

"Did you just crushed that stone into dust before it could fell down ?" Leon asked with a serious expression.

"Actually... I chopped it into dust, Master."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 340: Locust Albersum
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"You chopped it ? With what ?" Leon asked with a confused expression as he looked at RVX-666.

"I used-" Before he could’ve completed his sentence, Leon heard Lesrath’s footsteps coming back towards him.

"Yeah... We will discuss it later." Leon spoke as he immediately used another one of his abilities.

<Summoner’s Pocket>

The Malebolge disappeared instantly and desarath returned back with a huge piece of metal in his hands.

’Hmm...? Where hath Kumigano’s body gone?’ Desarath asked with a confused expression as he looked at Leon.

"Kumigano’s body... It disappeared as soon as you left, I don’t know how but it just disappeared." Leon replied as he pretended to be confused.

’What?! Kumigano’s body hath vanished of its own accord? But how canst such a thing be possible?’ Lesarath asked in a confused tone.

"How can I know that ? I am just a lesser Life form... I don’t know what happens to you guys when you die. Maybe your body will also disappear when you die." Anon replied with a neutral expression.

’Thou art right... thou art but a lesser life form. How wouldst thou know the reason behind Kumigano’s vanishing?’ Lesarath replied as he looked at Leon.

"What’s that you are holding in your hand ?" Leon asked with a confused expression as he looked at Lesarath.

’Ah... This is a very rare metal known as pure locust. If you use weapons made out of this material they will gain immeasurable powers but can you carry it ?’ Lesrath asked as he placed the piece of locust right next to Leon.

"Hmm...?" Leon looked at it with a neutral expression as he used his skill.

<Inspect>

[Name: Locust Albersum]

[Rank: S]

[Description: A very rare material that can only be found inside secret rooms of high level dungeons. Although it has a very high melting point, but the weapons made out of it are guaranteed to gain two or more skills based on it’s quality.

The material in itself is very light but when sharpened into a weapon properly, it can cut through mana patterns as well.]

"Well... Isn’t that something." Leon spoke as he immediately touched the Locust and noticed that it almost had no weight.

’I can pick this up with one hand, yet touching it feels as if it’s made out titanium or something. So this is what an S rank metal looks and feels like.’ Leon thought as he immediately placed the whole piece of locust into his inventory.

’Huh... Thou canst wield space magic as well? Thou art a most intriguing being. Wouldst thou wish to become a friend of Lesrath?’ Lesarath asked.

"Your friend... I don’t see any problems with that. So, okay." Leon replied with a neutral expression.

’So... What is it thou art doing here? What purpose hath brought thee into Lesrath’s lair?’ Lesarath asked in a curious tone.

"For now... I am trying to find out a way on how to get out of this Dungeon. Do you know any ?" Leon asked with a neutral expression as he looked at Lesarath.

’Hmm... Escaping this dungeon... that is no easy feat. Yet I have heard whispers from the seventh floor that some hath discovered a crack through which one might escape.’ Lesarath replied as he looked at Leon.

"A crack ? So... There is an escape route on the seventh floor, right ?" Leon asked with a smile.

’Thou art right... yet I, Lesrath, wouldst not counsel thee to venture unto the seventh floor. To go there is to invite death to dine with thee.

The Mongers make their dwelling upon that floor... vile creatures of the darkest nature — ones that couldst bring even Lesrath to his knees. The deeper thou dost descend into this dungeon, little one... the more wicked and fearsome the creatures thou shalt encounter.’ Lesarath explained.

"Then... What’s in the end of it ?" Leon asked with a curious expression.

’A Tomb...’

"Tomb ?"

’Lesarath hath only heard rumours of such things... Yet they speak of a most wicked demon — one who canst bend minds to his will, twist reality itself, and determine the outcome of any battle fought beneath these cursed skies... Yet for all his terrible power, he was betrayed by those he held most dear... and hath been imprisoned within these depths ever since.’ Lesarath replied.

"I see... Well, I think it’s time I take my leave." Leon spoke as he started walking towards the room’s gate.

’Where dost thou think thou art going? The gate to the third floor lieth this way.’ Lesarath spoke as he pointed towards the opposite direction.

"I have to go back to the first floor in order to do something. Then I will come back." Leon replied with a neutral expression.

’Well, Lesarath will look forward to meeting Leon Luster again.’ Lesarath replied as he bowed down a little showing respect.

"Me too..." Leon replied as he did the same and started walking towards the gate.

As he reached near the exit gate’s gap... Leon heard something,

"...ter."

"Hmm...?" He immediately looked around and tried to find the source of low pitched voice.

"M-Mas...ter."

"Dusk..." Leon spoke as his eyes widened in shock.

"Dusk... Where are you ?" He shouted loudly as he started looking around with a worried expression.

"Mast...er."

It didn’t took him long to find Dusk, who was lying behind a small mountain of obsidian, his body sweating like crazy and even though a thick layer of mana was covering his body, his fur was slowly burning down.

"What the fuck are you doing inside ?" Leon spoke in serious tone as he walked upto him, picked him up in his hands and started running towards the exit gap under the door.

Without wasting another second... Both of them exited the room and Leon jumped to the 29th stare in order to get away from the blazing heat waves that were coming from under the gap.

As soon as he got away from the heat waves... Adaptive Body stopped working and his body returned back to it’s original form.

"Heal." Leon spoke as he placed his hand over Dusk’s head and suddenly his whole body started to glow green.

"Fucking hell... You are hotter than the sun right now." Leon spoke as he felt his hand’s skin ripping away from the heat.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 341: RE:Spell
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

*Blink-Blink*

Dusk slowly opened his eyes and noticed a blurry face in front of him.

"...ght ?"

"... Hello ? Can you you hear me, Dusk ? Are you alright ?"

"Master...?" Dusk asked with a confused expression as his vision cleared up and he noticed that Leon was looking at him with a smile.

"Well, at least you are alive." Leon replied with a neutral expression as he looked at Dusk.

"Master... What happened ? I-I went inside and-"

"Why the fuck would you go inside when you know that your fur can’t handle that much heat ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Master... I heard your voice from inside and I felt a huge surge of mana being used inside. Then I heard that loud explosion and I gave into my instincts." Dusk replied with a neutral expression as he looked at Leon.

"Well, your instincts weren’t wrong... It was dangerous inside but not anymore, I made a new friend inside. He is kind of a big deal." Leon replied with a smile as he stood up from the floor.

"Master... Your aura..." Dusk spoke as his eyes shined brightly blue and he sensed that something is different about Leon.

"What ?" Leon asked with a neutral expression.

"It has changed so much..." Dusk replied with a shocked expression.

"Yeah... I killed an SS-rank monster, must be because of that." Leon replied in neutral tone as he turned back and started looking at stairs that were going up.

"Master... H-How did you killed an SS-rank monster ?" Dusk asked with a scared and shocked expression.

"That’s a long story... But, I have found a way to get out of this dungeon, it’s from the seventh floor but first we need to go back to the first floor." Leon replied with a neutral expression as ideas started to fill his mind.

’Turning my weight zero can work but It will be purged as soon as I step-up to the twenty-eighth step.

Wait... Can I make a spell that can stop it ?’ Leon thought as he immediately activated his skill.

<Inspect>

He then looked at the 28th step and a semi-transparent screen popped up in front of him.

[Name: Serimac Stone]

[Rank: S]

[Description: Serimac Stones can only be found inside S-rank dungeons or above. These stones are used to make staircases in between two dungeon floors in order to stop monsters from invading each other’s space.

These stones are enchanted with very powerful {Null Area} magic that prohibits any type of magic to be used until you are standing on them.]

’As expected... These stares are magic enchanted. So breaking them won’t be any useful until I can break the magic but since the magic in itself is a <NULL> spell, no matter what kind of spell I take near it, the spell will just disable it...’ Leon thought as a sat back down and started thinking with a very serious expression on his face.

Ten minutes passed but Leon didn’t stopped thinking, he thought of every possibility that could’ve been used to get back to the first floor.

Dusk on the other side continued to rest as he recovered his lost mana.

20 minutes later...

"Okay, this should work." Leon spoke as he used another skill.

<Skill Crafter>

[Please pick a name for the skill...]

"RE:Spell"

[Skill ’RE:Spell’ has been created.]

[Rank has been settled to ’D’ by default.]

[State the use of skill.]

’This is something of a concept that I saw back on earth in comic books and manhwas that I used to read. They called it reverse engineering spells.

If any kind of magic is being used this spell will scan it and reverse engineer it. Once I can see how the spell is working, I will be able to remove it or maybe make some adjustments.’ Leon thought as he started speaking.

"This skill will analyse magic spells for mana patterns and how they work by reverse engineering them. The skill doesn’t need to come in contact with the spell in order to scan it. Once scanned the spell’s working knowledge will be directly sent to my brain."

[Processing....]

’Since it’s just a monitoring spell and doesn’t do anything, it should get acce-’ Before he could’ve completed his thoughts another screen popped up in front of him.

[Description has been accepted.]

"Good."

[Please select the chant for the skill {RE:Spell}.]

"Re:Spell" Leon spoke with a smile.

[Chant ’Re:Spell’ has been selected.]

[Estimated mana cost to use the skill has been calculated, 100,000 mana/use.]

[Cooldown has been calculated, 10 seconds after every use.]

[Would you like to learn the skill or Destroy it ?]

"Hmm... Mana amount is not an issue. Learn it." Leon replied with a neutral expression.

[Congratulations ! You have successfully learned the skill {Re:Spell}]

"Good..." Leon spoke as he immediately raised his hand towards the 28th step and activated it.

<RE:Spell>

As soon as he used the skill, two completely blank white magic circle appeared hovering on the backside and on the frontside of his hand.

Suddenly the magic circle that was on the front side of his hand started to shoot white lasers at the stair’s step as if scanning it.

*Zzzzzzzz*

Suddenly a half-transparent screen popped up in front of Leon.

[<Null> Spell has been found.]

[Copying has begun.]

[1%>>>>2%>>>>>3%>>>>....]

Incantations started to appear inside the white magic circle that was on the frontside of his hand.

’The Front magic circle copies the spell. The back one must be for reverse engineering it.’ Leon thought as he noticed that another circle has appeared on the front side.

"It’s not a small spell..." Leon spoke in a low voice as he waited patiently.

30 minutes later,

The spell that started copying with one single magic circle has now increased upto 31 magical circles some big, some small and some that had ancient enchantings in them.

[98>>>> 99%>>>> 100%.]

[Copying process has been completed.]

"Fucking finally..." Leon spoke with a relived expression as he noticed that all the circles have started to spin slowly.

[Reverse Engineering the spell]

[1%>>>2%>>>>]

"Fucking Hell..." He spoke in a frustrated voice as he sat down on the ground.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 342: Focus
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

One hour later...

Leon is sitting on the twenty nineth stair looking at sixty black magical circles hovering in the air in front of him, alongside with a small screen.

[98%>>>>99%>>>>100%]

[Reverse Engineering has been completed.]

[All the information will be sent to host’smind now.]

"Final-" Before Leon could’ve completed his sentence, he felt a severe headache.

He started loosing his balance as an incredible amount of knowledge entered inside his mind without any stops.

"Fuck..." Leon spoke as he fell down to his knees and barely managed to stay conscious.

[All the information has been submitted.]

*Thud*

He immediately fell down to the ground on his back and stared at the darkness above.

"HUFF-HUFF-HUFFFFF.... What the fuck was that ?" Leon spoke with a serious expression as he grabbed his head tightly.

’I can feel the information inside my brain... The enchantment of <Null> spell is a closed loop enchantment that can be casted using a mana destroying skill known as mana eater.

This skill is the centre of this enchantment... What it does is whenever someone gives it even a shred of mana, the intensity of mana eater scales accordingly and it consumes even more mana and the loop continues.

In order to protect the loop from being broken, the enchantment is covered with 36 different magical circles working as guards, out of these thirty-six circles, ten circles share the same configuration to the mana eater but they miss the looping component turning them into the perfect decoys.

Even for a super intelligent Lich who starts to read this enchantment today... It will still take him around 70 years just to find the right magic circle that this enchantment works on. If he successfully copies the skill on a paper.

But, my skill only took 90 minutes to decode all of that at the risk of exploding my skull and brain, but it was so fucking worth it.

Now that I know it’s working all I need to do it keep my mana away from the mana eater’s magic circle and as long as it doesn’t get activated, the rest of the enchantment is stupid.’ Leon thought as he stood up and walked upto the twenty-eighth step.

"When a skill is used mana is released from the body... It spreads around without any limitations but when body soaks in crude mana from the enviornment it only soaks in a limited amount.

All I need to do is when I cast a spell... The extra mana that is being released from my body, I need to soak it back in before it can reach the mana eater." He spoke in a low voice as he switched off all of his passive skills, placed his hand over twenty-eighth step.

’As expected nothing is happening... But if I started using mana right now, the enchantment will activate. Now let’s see if I can do it.’ Leon thought as he raised his another hand to activate a skill.

<Fireball>

Slowly a small spark generated over his hand but...

[You have entered into a <NULL MANA> zone.]

[All type of magic will be neglected here.]

"Fuck..." Leon spoke in a low voice as he removed his hand from the stair and placed it back.

<Fireball>

This time Leon focused very carefully on his body as he felt the mana that was being released with the spell.

"Come on..." He spoke in a low voice as he closed his eyes.

*Inhaleeee*

Suddenly he felt a warm sensation near his face.

"What the- FUCK !" Leon shouted as he noticed a huge ball of fire hovering over his hand that was coming all the way to his face and it was shining brightly blue.

He immediately cancelled the spell and took a step back from the twenty-eighth stair.

’What the fuck was that ?’ Leon asked with a shocked expression.

"Wait... Wait..." He spoke as he raised his hand in front of him once again and activated the spell.

<Fireball>

Immediately a small orange fireball appeared over his hand.

"Okay..." Leon spoke as he closed his eyes and focused once again.

*Inhaleeeee*

As soon as he sucked in all the extra mana that was being released by the spell, the small fireball in his hand immediately changed colours to blue.

Leon slowly opened his eyes and noticed that not only did it changed colour but also it’s size has started to grow bigger.

’The mana consumption of the spell...’ Leon thought as his eyes widened in shock.

"When a spell is being used it will use some mana and release rest of the mana through the body but when I sucked back in that mana it’s intensity increased and from the results, I don’t think this spell is using even 40% of it’s total mana usage.

Fucking Hell..." Leon thought as he stopped the spell at once and used another skill.

<Skill Crafter>

[Please pick a name for the skill...]

"Mana Absorber"

[Skill ’Mana Absorber’ has been created.]

[Rank has been settled to ’D’ by default.]

[State the use of skill.]

"A basic passive skill that when active, it can suck back the mana that is being released when any spell is used."

[Processing....]

’Why didn’t I think of this before ?’

[Error: Skill’s description doesn’t match the Rank. Please Try Again...]

"Huh...? But... It’s a basic skill, the description is like two lines long. I can’t even change anything in it, it’s a mana-" Suddenly something clicked inside Leon’s brain.

"Ah right... It’s a fucking mana based spell, why would the system let me make a spell that will help me modify any spell to work at it’s 2-3x original power.

Haa... Fuck it, I will just use my concentration to do it. Destroy the skill." Leon spoke as he destroyed the skill.

"Wait... I can make that." Leon spoke as he activated the skill once again.

<Skill Crafter>

[Please pick a name for the skill...]

"Body Enchantment"

[Skill ’Body Enchantment’ has been created.]

"When used this skill will consume mana regularly to strengthen the host’s body’s strength, agility and dexterity."

"Yeah... I need to add defined parameters. How about, ten thousand mana per second for increasing everything by ten folds ?"

[Error: Skill’s description doesn’t match the Rank. Please Try Again...]

"Nine folds."

"Eight Folds."

[Error: Skill’s description doesn’t match the Rank. Please Try Again...]

....

"Okay... Five Folds."

"Hmm... What if I increased the mana consumption to twenty thousand per minute ? Will the strength increase by ten folds then ?

Since I have such a big mana reserve now I should be able to do that, let’s try... Destroy this skill as well. We will start from the start."

[Skill has been Destroyed.]

Chapter 343: THE RISING FULL MOON
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Error: Skill’s description doesn’t match the Rank. Please Try Again...]

"Master we were coming down from the stairs and as soon as you stepped onto this step, you just stopped moving all of a sudden." Dusk explained.

Without wasting another second, Leon used his skill on the white mist.

<Inspect>

Suddenly a red window popped up in front of him.

[System is Offline.]

"What the-" Before the words could’ve come out of his mouth, a huge green hand grabbed his shoulder.

Leon turned and noticed a huge orc standing behind him holding a wooden club in his hand.

He immediately raised the club and brought it down upon Leon with full power.

"RAAWWHHHHHHHH..."

"Fucking hell." Leon immediately dodged to the side and noticed that the orc had disappeared from his location.

’What the fuck is happening !? Why is the system offline ? And where the hell did Dusk go ? He was standing right here a moment ago.’ Leon thought with a serious expression as it struck him all of a sudden.

"This is an illusion too..." Leon spoke as he immediately slapped himself really hard.

*SLAP*

"HAAAAAAH..." He took a long breathe as he woke up from the illusion and noticed that he is standing on the last stair with dusk in one hand and the world tree plant in another hand.

Dusk’s eyes have turned white as the mist is continuously entering his nose and playing with his brain.

"Dusk... Wake up." Leon spoke as he placed the world tree plant down and started shaking dusk really hard in order to wake him up.

But dusk wasn’t waking up.

"Leon Luster..." Suddenly a familiar voice came from behind.

Leon immediately turned around and noticed a girl with huge boobs standing amidst the fog her face not visible clearly.

"Cherry..." Leon spoke with a serious expression as he looked at her.

"What are you even doing Leon ? Come to me... Let’s go on a date or something. You used to ask me out so much during the high school, right ?" Cherry spoke in a low voice as she started walking towards Leon.

"There is no fucking way you are here. I am still in the fucking illusion." Leon spoke with a serious expression as he looked at her.

"Why does that even matter ? You can now do anything you want with me..." Cherry spoke as her clothes disappeared instantly and her boobs jiggled up and down as she walked forward.

"... I am the same girl that didn’t even wanted to look at your disgusted face, but I am ready to become your plaything now. Come use me as you want or do you want to change things in me ?

Want bigger boobs ?" Cherry spoke as the size of her already huge boobs doubled instantly.

Suddenly a perverted smile appeared over Leon’s face as he walked forward and grabbed her neck.

"Oh... You want to do rough play ? Well-"

Without wasting another second, Leon twisted her neck to the side.

*Crack*

"Why would you do that now ?" Cherry asked as she looked at Leon with her broken neck.

"Bitch... I always wanted to do that with you. No one looks down on me. I know it’s an illusion but it still feels good." Leon replied with a wide smile.

Chapter 362: The Mist -II
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Okay." Cherry spoke as she immediately cracked back her neck into position.

"I love this woman but I also have a mind and my memories, although you are trying to make them fuzzy using your stupid techniques.

Like I don’t even remember so many things already about my past life and even this one, but there is one thing that you can’t make me forget and that is my death.

That memory is etched into my mind and soul both. You can’t erase that even if you tried your best." Leon spoke with a smile as he looked at cherry.

"You are right... You do have memories of two lifes and the memory of your death is very hard to erase but the more time you spend in my illusions the more your mind will go crazy and at one point your consciousness will become one with me." The mist spoke as she looked at Leon.

"And why do you think that you can keep me inside your illusions ?" Leon asked with a smile.

"You tried to get out but what you didn’t notice was that you were getting even more deeper into my illusions and what will you even achieve after getting out ?

Pain, agony and hardwork... But, if you decided to stay here, I will give you everything. I will create everything that your mind desires even the wildest dreams will come true, you will feel everything as if it’s happening in reality, touch senses will get heightened and even having sex will feel ten times better.

I won’t even allow death to hurt you." The mist replied.

"Hmm... You have all of my memories already. Why do you want my consciousness to become your part ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Your mind is very strong. I will have so much fun breaking it and it’s been a while since I did that to someone." The mist spoke with a smile.

"Very well then... Give it your best fucking shot because I like to have sex with real women not some fucked up mind fantasy you are going to create." Leon spoke with a smile.

"You could’ve choosen a life so easier and yet you choose the suffering... I really can’t understand human beings.

I will create the worst demons that you have only seen in your dreams, you will feel misery and pain until your senses go numb and that’s when you will start to beg for death but I won’t give it to you." The mist spoke as she disappeared.

’From sex and having an easy life to making me die a dog’s death, well that’s an interesting change.’ Leon thought with a smile as he immediately sat down on the ground and closed his eyes.

’Illusions are really just because you think they are real. She can mimic everything from my memory but she can’t mimic the system.

Whenever I used a skill, the system shows that it’s offline but is it really offline ?’ Leon thought with a serious expression.

’Oi white fox... Can you hear me ?’ Leon asked in his mind.

’Hmm...? What happened now kid ?’ White Fox’s voice came from the other side.

’How do I believe that you are real and not the mist talking to me ?’ Leon asked in a confused tone.

*Shhhh*

Suddenly his consciousness was pulled out of his body...

*Blink-Blink*

Leon opened his eyes and noticed that he was standing on the same beache as before.

White fox was sitting on a wooden chair and enjoying the sun under a pine tree.

"What do you mean by if I am real or not ?" White fox asked as he stood up from the chair and started walking towards Leon.

"I am trapped inside a web of illusions and I doubt that even you are real." Leon spoke with a cautious expression.

"Inside an illusion...? Let me see." White fox spoke as he immediately waved his hand into the air and everything around them changed instantly.

The sky turned to night and suddenly a projection started playing all over it.

A projection of Leon’s eyes and what he was seeing.

In the projection there was nothing but a white mist dancing in front of his face, it was going in and out of his mouth, leaving him completely in a trance like state.

"Adaptive Body didn’t take effect that means the mist is something different. If only I can go back a few stairs,.." Leon spoke with a neutral expression.

"Hmm... It’s playing with your mind. Well, just fight back." White fox spoke with a neutral expression.

"How the hell am I supposed to do that ? She can control the illusions and the more I try to get out of them, the more I get wrapped up inside them." Leon replied with a confused expression.

"Well then... Change the battlefields. Take it somewhere, where you have the upper hand.

If she is controlling your mind, move your consciousness to your soul realm and fight there, I can sense some pretty strong creatures in your soul realm. Fight there you will win." White fox replied with a smile.

"Even if I kill her monsters in the soul realm... She will just make more of them." Leon replied with a neutral expression.

"No... That’s where you are wrong, once you defeat them in the soul realm, I will take care of the rest." White fox spoke with a smile.

"Are you going to take over my body ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"I don’t need to... Now go and move the battle to a battlefield that’s advantageous for you. Because you will only get a single chance to gain control over your body, don’t miss it." White fox replied with a smile.

"Fine... I will trust you on this one since I don’t have any other options but why are you helping me this time ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"If you ended up dying before learning the technique, I will stay trapped in here for even more centuries." White fox replied with a smile as he touched Leon’s forehead and suddenly his consciousness faded away returning him back inside the illusion.

Chapter 363: Is that Ragal ?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Phew... Fine, let’s do this." Leon spoke as he stood up and noticed a figure coming towards him from the mist.
