The creature’s head was missing but it had two huge eyes on it’s chest and that shined brightly yellow and continuously stared at Leon.

*Swish*

Suddenly it disappeared and re-appeared behind Leon.

"Fuck-" as soon as Leon tried to move away he noticed that his legs were trapped inside the ground and he couldn’t get them out.

The creature raised one of it’s hand and brought it down upon him with full power.

Without wasting another second, Leon raised both of his hands and covered his head with them.

*THUD*

*Cough-Cough*

The creature didn’t wait and immediately raised another one of his hands and got ready once again.

’Yes, Sir Leon... I can hear you.’ Nexus’s voice resounded in his head.

Suddenly Leon and the creature both disappeared.

"Hmm...? Intresting. Let’s see how far can you run away." The mist spoke with a smile as she observed Leon’s consciousness disappearing from her illusion.

Inside the soul realm,

*Blink-Blink*

Leon slowly opened his eyes and noticed that he was standing near a flowing river and the moon is shining brightly in the sky.

"I greet you, Sir Leon." A sound came from behind.

Leon immediately turned around and noticed that a man in his thirties with black hairs and black moustache was standing behind him, he was wearing a butler like dress and holding a glowing blue stick in his hands.

"Nexus ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Yes sir... Although I don’t have a real form. This form seems suitable to serve you or should I change it ?" Nexus asked with a confused expression.

"Keep it... What happened to that demon that was attacking me ?" Leon asked with a serious expression as he looked around with a confused expression.

"You mean this bug ?"

*THUD-THUD-THUD-THUD-THUD-THUD-THUD-THUD*

Suddenly eight white hands came rolling down on the ground, clearly belonging to the creature that Leon pulled into the soul realm with him.

As soon as he looked up towards the source of the heavy voice, Leon noticed a demon standing over a huge rock near the river’s bank.

The demon had six red eyes on his face, the upper two eyes pointing down diagonally, the middle ones horizontal and the lower ones pointing upwards diagonally.

He had a lean but muscular body with eight-pack abs and long hairs coming down to his legs.

He was holding two blood red swords in his hands and there was an evil smile on his face.

"Is he... Ragal ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Yes, Sir." Nexus replied with a neutral expression.

"Damn... He wasn’t looking like that when I last saw him." Leon spoke in a low voice.

"I spent around nine hundred years in hell until I got summoned into that weak demon, I don’t know how he even got the sacred seal to summon me into his body.

When you killed me- no let me rephrase that one. When you deceived me and then forced a sacred ritual to kill the body that I was in, I was pissed off and believe me I was pissed off enough to breakout of hell and come back just so I can kill you.

But... Then you stole my soul fragment as well and locked me in here with this motherfucking crazy bastard." Ragal spoke as he raised his sword and pointed towards Nexus.

"Hmm...? Him ? Crazy ?" Leon asked as he looked at Nexus with a confused expression.

"I tried to kill him thousands of times but this motherfucker just doesn’t die. He traps me in time loops and leaves me in them for thousands of years.

I hate that and that’s why we made a deal... I will live freely in your soul realm and do whatever I want in exchange for protecting it from any attackers just like the one that I just killed. It’s been a while since my blades tasted blood and it felt good killing him." Ragal explained as he looked at Leon.

’Damn, so that’s how he is re-shaping them into protecting my soul realm.’ Leon thought with a neutral expression as he looked at Nexus.

"Someone is invading the soul realm, Ragal." Nexus spoke as he looked at Ragal with a neutral expression.

"Hehehe... Just tell me the location. I have never felt like this in years." Ragal spoke with a maniacal smile as he licked his blades.

"Northern mountains... Thousands of monsters are coming towards this direction. They might be here for Sir Leon." Nexus spoke with a neutral expression.

"Will be back in a bit..." Ragal spoke as he immediately disappeared.

"Can you take me there ?" Leon asked as he looked at Nexus.

"As you command, Sir." Nexus replied as he waved his hand and suddenly the water from the river started floating into the air and made a circular portal that opened up on top of a mountain on the other side.

"Hmm... Cool. Can I do that as well ?" Leon asked with a neutral expression as both of them travelled through the portal.

"It’s your soul realm, sir. You can travel to anywhere with just a mere thought, but I would restrict you from doing that as it de-stablizes with the basic rules of the realm.

The more stable your soul realm remains, the stronger it will become. That’s why I have given it the form of a real world destination." Nexus explained.

"Hmm... Understood." Leon replied with a neutral expression as he looked down and noticed a huge cloud of white fog covering the mountains in itself and thousands of deformed demons were coming out of the fog.

"Come on motherfuckers... Grandfather will teach you how it’s done." Ragal shouted as he taunted the demons.

"Should I go down there ?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Sir you haven’t trained your consciousness to fight yet. If you were to go down there right now, you will get killed immediately and if your consciousness dies in here, you will die in reality as well." Nexus explained.

"Fucking Hell."

"But don’t you worry, sir. I have faith that Ragal will be more than enough for them as we are fighting in our own terrain." Nexus spoke with a smile as he waved his hand.

Chapter 364: Form of Nine-tails
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Soul Realm stretched out as an infinite, haunting expanse, a landscape born of the subconscious. It was a world of jagged peaks and deep, shadowed valleys, where the ground felt less like solid earth and more like the manifestation of memory itself.

The air was thick and stagnant, heavy with the weight of an approaching storm. From the mountain peak where Leon and Nexus stood, the horizon was swallowed by a dense, unnatural fog. This blanket of mist didn’t just obscure vision; it felt like a living shroud, creeping up from the front valley with a predatory patience.

Out of the shifting mist, the nightmares took shape. They emerged in waves—creatures of twisted flesh and jagged bone, their forms defying the natural order.

Most were terrifying, hunched abominations that growled with a hunger for consciousness. Yet, towering above the rank and file were two entities so colossal they dwarfed the very mountains, their heads piercing the low-hanging clouds of the realm.

"Well... Those are some really big ass monsters," Leon spoke, his expression remaining neutral despite the scale of the threat.

Suddenly, the environment behind him responded to his presence. The jagged stones of the peak began to grind and shift, sliding over one another with a rhythmic clatter, stacking themselves into a high-backed, imposing throne.

"Please, take a seat Sir," Nexus spoke as he pointed towards the throne.

"Okay," Leon replied with a neutral expression as he sat down on the cold stone.

"Leon Luster..." A loud voice, sharp and piercing, cut through the mist like a blade.

Leon immediately looked at the source of the sound and noticed a woman walking out of the white haze. She wore a white robe and had nine white tails standing tall and rigid behind her. She also held a small folding fan in her hand that she waved at her face, a gesture of mock sophistication.

"A nine-tails..." Leon spoke in a low voice.

"It’s a creature that I choose from your mind... You find it quite terrifying for some reason," she spoke with a wide, predatory smile as her fangs appeared.

"Fucking bitch," Leon spoke in an angry voice as he grabbed the stone hand rest of the throne tightly.

"I will take over your soul realm and then your consciousness will have no place to escape. It will be mine and mine forever," she spoke with a wicked smile as she looked at Leon.

"RAGAL..." Leon shouted as he stood up from the throne. His eyes widened from excessive anger.

"Hmm...?" Ragal immediately looked up at Leon with a confused expression.

"... I WANT THAT BITCH’S HEAD," he shouted loudly as he pointed towards the nine-tails.

"Who? Her? Very well then... She is my target now," Ragal spoke as he grabbed his blades and started running towards the horde of the monsters.

"Kill," the nine-tails spoke with a smile as she raised her hand towards Ragal.

*GRAAHHHHHHHHHH*

The valley erupted as every monster in the mist immediately started running towards Ragal.

"Come come comeeeeeeee... HUP..." Ragal shouted as he immediately jumped from his position, clearing the first wave. The monsters stopped running as they looked up and noticed that Ragal was coming down on them instead.

*THUD*

*BOOOOOOOOM*

As soon as he landed on the ground, a shockwave of pure force rippled outward. All the monsters standing in a hundred-meter area around him got vaporized immediately, reduced to ashes by the sheer pressure.

"COME ON MOTHERFUCKERS," Ragal shouted as he raised his swords towards the monsters. Without wasting another second, the remaining horde surged forward again.

"BLOOD VIPER."

Ragal spoke as he activated his skill. He raised both of his swords into the air and slashed the air vertically, carving twin gashes into the atmosphere. Two huge crimson vipers shot out of his blades, circulating around him in clockwise and anti-clockwise directions.

"HISSSSSSSSSSSSS..."

"HISSSSSSSSSSSSS...."

As soon as the vipers touched the monsters, they turned into ashes.

"Fucking Hell. He really is one strong bastard," Leon spoke with a serious expression as he looked at Ragal from his high vantage point.

"I am coming for you bitch," Ragal spoke with a maniacal smile as he looked at the nine-tails.

"KILL HIM," the nine-tails shouted as she looked at Ragal.

*GRAAAAHHHHHHH...*

A huge ogre-like monster started running towards Ragal to intercept him.

"I don’t want to fight you bugs... Get out of my way," Ragal spoke as he immediately disappeared from his position and re-appeared behind the monster.

*Slash-Slash-Slash-Slash*

The monster was immediately sliced down into four different chunks of meat as he turned into ashes and disappeared.

"GAWWWHHHHHHHHHH..." Two huge monsters that were taller than the mountains themselves started walking towards Ragal. One of them held a club made out of metal with jagged spikes on it, while the other held a massive trident in his hands.

"They are several times bigger than him," Leon spoke with a serious expression.

"Sir Leon... No monster can win against Ragal," Nexus replied with a neutral expression.

"What?" Leon asked with a confused expression.

"Ragal is too arrogant, Sir. These monsters are made out of illusions and since Ragal was never defeated in his entire life by anyone, he just doesn’t believe that there is someone or something stronger than him in this world. Illusion only works if the target has self-doubts, but Ragal is completely driven by his arrogance.

He will never accept defeat and that’s why these monsters can’t defeat him," Nexus explained as he looked at Leon.

’This motherfucker is winning just because he simply doesn’t believe that there is no creature or army in this world can defeat him?’ Leon thought with a serious expression as he turned to look at Ragal.

"HUAWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWW...." The two demons rawred as they raised their weapons into the air and brought them down on him with full power.

*SWISSSH*

*SWISSHHH*

"Heh... The bigger you are, the harder you fall." Ragal spoke with a wide smile as he disappeared from his position and re-appeared behind one of the monster’s legs.

*SLASH-SLASH-SLASH-SLASH*

Without wasting another second, he did a number of attacks on both of his legs before disappearing and re-appearing behind the other monster’s legs and repeating the same thing.

*SLASH-SLASH-SLASH-SLASH*

Both of the monsters immediately lost their balance and fell down to the ground.

*THUDDDDDDDD*

*THUDDDDDDDD*

As soon as they hitted the ground, their bodies turned into ashes.

’How can my illusion fail like that ?’ The nine-tails thought as she looked at Ragal.

"You are next." Ragal spoke as he disappeared and re-appeared behind her.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 365: Mists of Ilirai
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Die... Bitch." Ragal’s voice was a low, jagged rasp as he swung his blades in a wide, lethal arc aimed directly at the Nine-Tails’ throat.

*SLASH.*

The blade whistled through the air, but there was no resistance. As soon as the blades touched her neck, they passed through her as if she were made of smoke. Her figure flickered, dissolving into the swirling white mist.

"You really think you can kill me? I am not something that can be killed." The voice drifted from behind him, cold and mocking. Ragal spun around, his eyes locking onto a jagged rock where she had already re-manifested, looking down at him with detached amusement.

Without a moment’s hesitation, Ragal launched himself across the distance, a blur of motion, but she vanished once more before his fingers could even graze her robe.

"What the hell?" Ragal muttered, his expression darkening into a mask of grim focus. He turned again, finding her standing on the exact same rock as before, as if she hadn’t moved at all.

From his throne on the mountain peak, Leon watched the cycle repeat. He leaned forward, his eyes narrowing.

’She is playing with him,’ he thought, noting the way the illusion shifted to frustrate Ragal’s directness.

...

"What happened? Couldn’t catch me?" the Nine-Tails taunted, her smile widening as she looked down at him.

Ragal didn’t respond with anger this time. He looked at her with a neutral, almost hollow expression, then slowly lowered his gaze to the ground beneath his feet.

"Hmm...? Are you disappointed that you couldn’t catch me?" she asked, her voice dripping with fake sympathy.

*STAB-STAB.*

Suddenly, Ragal drove both of his blades deep into the rock he stood upon. Before the mist could react, he vanished, reappearing nearly a hundred meters to the east.

"Hmm...?" The Nine-Tails tilted her head, her smile faltering.

Ragal didn’t attack. Instead, he raised his leg and brought his heel down with shattering force against the ground.

*THUMP.*

"Falir... The one who sees all," Ragal intoned.

His figure blurred again, reappearing a hundred meters to the north. Once more, his leg lashed out, his foot driving into the earth like a stake.

*THUMP*

"Ulgir... The one who knows no fear." Swish. He was gone again, re-emerging a hundred meters to the west.

*THUMP*

"Chyol... The one who has sun’s pride."

"What are you even doing?" the Nine-Tails demanded, her confusion evident. Around her, the endless horde of monsters stood unnervingly still, their mindless aggression dampened by the strange rhythm of Ragal’s movements.

*THUMP*

"Karon... The one who serves final justice."

...

Leon’s eyes widened in sudden realization. "Seals..." he whispered.

’Those are seals... I read about them in a book. In this technique, five seals are applied in order to make a pentagon around the target and cast the formation. But this technique is so old that no one uses it anymore except the demons.’

*THUMP.*

"Una-mura... The one who has hold over gods," Ragal declared as he planted the final seal. In a flash, he returned to the central rock, his hands gripping the hilts of the swords he had left behind. He ripped them from the stone with a shower of sparks.

"WAKE UP... THE FIVE DEMONIC GENERALS."

<COUNTER-FORMATION: DEMONIC FIVE GENERALS>

The atmosphere of the Soul Realm buckled. All five points where Ragal had struck the ground began to glow with a fierce, blinding radiance. Bright threads of light shot out from the seals, connecting with surgical precision to form a massive, shimmering pentagon that caged the area.

Every monster trapped within the geometry of the light was instantly incinerated, turning to fine gray ash. Only the Nine-Tails remained, her eyes wide with genuine shock as the trap snapped shut.

From the glowing seals, five towering figures rose. They were shapeless and formless, entities of pure, shifting shadow that seemed to swallow the light around them.

"You can’t stay in your soul realm for long, Leon Luster. I will be back!" the Nine-Tails shrieked. She tried to dissolve back into the mist, her form beginning to blur.

"Not so fast," Ragal growled, leveling a blade at her.

Instantly, one of the shadow figures raised a hand. A heavy, crimson chain erupted from its palm, lashing through the air with a metallic *Krrrrrr*.

The links wrapped tightly around her neck and waist, anchoring her to the physical plane and halting her escape.

"W-What the-!?" She struggled against the spectral iron, her eyes darting to Ragal as he began to walk toward her with slow, predatory steps.

"You thought you could do whatever you want, huh? You are just a mere illusion, and you thought you could trick me with those stupid tricks of yours?" Ragal’s grip on his swords was white-knuckled.

To the north of the Pentagon, another shadow raised its hand. Above its head, a massive black balance scale manifested in the air, tilting precariously.

"Karon... What does your libra say?" Ragal asked, his voice echoing in the sudden silence of the valley.

"EXECUTION." the word was not spoken so much as it was felt, a loud, authoritative roar that vibrated through the very foundations of the realm.

"Very fucking well then. Execution it is," Ragal said, an evil smile curling his lips as he closed the distance.

"S-STOP HIM!" the Nine-Tails screamed, her voice cracking as she commanded the remaining monsters outside the barrier to charge.

But as the horde threw themselves at the lines of light, a third shadow raised its hand.

"NO ONE SHALL PASS." The authoritative boom acted as a physical wall. Any monster that dared to cross the threshold of the pentagon was instantly reduced to ash, their bodies disintegrating before they could even touch the light.

Ragal stood before the Nine-Tails, looking into her frantic eyes. His smile was maniacal, the expression of a man who found joy in the destruction of his enemies.

"Woah..." Leon spoke with a shocked expression.

"I know you are just an illusion, but killing you will give me more satisfaction than killing a thousand living beings," he whispered.

With a blur of motion, he swung. His blade moved with the full weight of his arrogance and power, beheading her in a single, flawless strike.

*Thud.*

As her head hit the ground, the oppressive white mist that had choked the mountains vanished instantly, revealing the jagged peaks of the Soul Realm once more.

Outside the consciousness, the White Fox watched the shifting energies. "Very well then... It’s time I do my job," he murmured, a sharp smile appearing on his face.

<LAST RISE FOURTH FORM: CLEANSING THUNDER>

The world around him changed in a heartbeat.

The sky bruised purple and black as heavy clouds swirled into a vortex. A massive bolt of lightning descended, striking the White Fox’s blade with a deafening crack. As the electricity surged through the blade, he performed a fluid 360-degree spin, discharging the lightning in every direction like a radial wave of cleansing fire.

*BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM*

...

Back in the Soul Realm, white Fox’s voice resonated inside Leon’s head. ’Kid, now’s your chance.’

"Yes," Leon answered as he pulled his consciousness back, retreating from the soul realm and returning back to the physical world.

*Blink-Blink.*

His eyes snapped open. The suffocating haze of the mist was cleared from inside his body.

"HUP... HUP." Without wasting a second, he leaped back two stairs, reaching the safety of the clear air where the remnants of the mist couldn’t reach.

"I have to confirm," he whispered, his voice steady.

<Inspect>

[Name: Mists of Ilirai]

[Rank: SS]

[Description: Mists of Ilirai are the very powerful and most dangerous mists that can only be found inside an SSS-rank dungeon. Once inhaled, it is impossible to get out of its illusion spell. Mists of Ilirai are made up of thousands of consciousnesses mixed together; these mists contain the anger, pain, and misery of others and use them in its illusions to break their next prey.]

[Warning: Host is strongly advised not to inhale the mist at any cost.]

Leon stared at the floating blue screen. "I really need to be more careful of what I do next," he muttered, looking down at the path ahead.
